title: Markets Non-Financial Risk Oversight Lead
description: JOB SUMMARY The Markets Governance team supports the governance of the Markets and Financing Securities Services (FSS) businesses with respect to non-financial risks ensuring alignment with Bank Policy and Enterprise Risk Management Framework.  This will include chairing and / or supporting and / or setting standards for meetings that support country and central governance.  The role will also be responsible for reviewing and leading pan Markets existing risk and control framework, remediation activities for alignment. Roles & Responsibilities As such this role will: •    Support the holistic Markets/FSS governance framework, through understanding Market’s strategy, actions under the overall strategic plan, the flow of reporting information within Markets and understanding the delegations of authority / stakeholder landscape for the businesses. •    Lead pan Markets deliverables and initiatives, to align with group and CIB strategy. This is performed through review of existing framework, leading multiple Markets-wide initiatives, and being the coordinator for the Global FSS Non-Financial Risk Committee. •    Collaborate with stakeholders to drive the pan Markets agenda and its related Book of Work •    As directed by business, drive the right risk culture within Markets and /or FSS and support implementation of various related Initiatives. •    Own the global Markets Product Risk Management Process, driving change, efficiency and strong risk management discipline •    Manage communication of status updates to key stakeholders including Senior Management through presentations and reports. •    Provide robust oversight to support adherence to relevant internal and external policies across Markets and / or CIB Strategy •    Provides input and maintains consistency with Bank / CIB Conduct standards and policy. Business •    Good understanding of the Markets and FSS business, how Markets operate within Group, how it aligns with CIB strategy and the external market environment in which we operate including Regulators. •    Good understanding of Risk standards and Policy relevant to the Markets/FSS business and scope of the role. •    Awareness and understanding of the wider Markets/FSS business, in which the Group Operates. This role leads a team that provides core support to the business by providing risk management support. Processes •    Responsible for implementing processes to ensure continued adherence to Policies and Standards relevant to the Markets business. •    Responsible for improving efficiencies and timeliness of relevant processes. •    Provide guidance and analysis for FSS related matters across the Markets Process Universe and process owner support as required. People & Talent •    Responsible for continuously developing a team of strong risk managers. •    Responsible for leading through example and building the appropriate culture and values. •    Sets appropriate tone and expectations for the team and work in collaboration with risk and control partners. •    Responsible for driving training and communications to promote awareness and continuous learning for risk, control, and governance aspects. •    Responsible for ensuring the provision of ongoing training and development of people and ensure that holders of all critical functions are suitably skilled and qualified for their roles ensuring that they have effective supervision in place to mitigate any risks. Risk Management •    Oversee the non-financial risks which can impact the business. Ability to connect the themes and issues across products, functions and locations. •    Monitor control performance, behaviour, trends and internal/external events to proactively identify non-financial risks. •    Perform reviews and assessments of risk metrics (losses, near miss incidents, control effectiveness) to identify top themes and trends. •    Responsible for driving cross-business risk initiatives in support of Target Operating Model. •    Contributes to defining annual priorities for periodic review process as a form of First Line Assurance (i.e. “peer or targeted reviews”). •    Identify opportunities for rationalisation of controls and streamlining of risk administration processes, e.g. Risk Self Assessments (RCSA), (i.e. “control simplification”). •    Responsible for risk tooling and associated processes working in partnership with Group OR and vendors (e.g. Incident Management, KRI Dashboards, etc.) •    Oversee risk oversight documentation (top risk profiles, risk committee materials) and monitoring day-to-day risk treatment progress (residual risk assessments, risk treatment plans, management action plans). Governance •    Promote the development of risk-oriented culture within the organization. •    Accountable for the effective running of all Markets governance committees including FSS Non-Financial Risk Committees •    Drive standards and consistency in 1-down regional/country/function forums relevant to role and providing consistent escalations to 1-up forums/committees. •    Representative at required other unspecified Markets Governance forums and Committees as required to provide input relevant to role. •    Ensure Bank policies and standards are fit for purpose for FSS seeking changes and deviation where required. •    Create, set and evaluate governance standards, frameworks and processes to ensure they are fit for purpose to effectively manage Markets business risk. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    Markets Business Heads •    Markets Group and Regional COO teams •    Markets Functional Heads •    Markets and Group CFCC •    Group OR •    Markets MT Other Responsibilities •    Live by the Here for Good promise and the brand values as stated for Group, Business and Country •    Perform other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures. •    Responsible for developing a strong working relationship across all Markets risk and risk ops teams and locations. •    Responsible for leveraging the opportunity provided by Corporate Social Responsibility to enhance the Group’s internal and external reputation and indirectly influence the bottom line. •    Responsible for promoting the Group’s brand and Here for good with employees, clients and regulators. •    Responsible for performing other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures. •    Responsible for maintaining effective communication with key stakeholders, and staff. Our Ideal Candidate •    Strong risk management and controls background – Individual with more than 10+ years of experience in risk management and compliance. title: Investment Analyst
description: Position: Investment Analyst To Apply Please kindly prepare the following information and submit your job application via this link: https://www.korupartners.com/jobposting/?jobid=87 Resume. Academic Transcript. 500 words or less response to “ What matters to you the most? And why ?” (An email will be sent to you requesting this essay the next working day after the job application is submitted). Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis and all applications with missing information and no justifiable reason will be considered incomplete and will not be processed. Overview Koru Partners is a thematically driven alternative investment firm identifying attractive investment opportunities that are backed by macro-holistic themes encompassing forward-looking social, economic, demographic, and technological changes. We are inspired to be the world’s leading business partner for unique insights, understanding and decision making. It is our mission to understand the challenges facing humanity and to empower people and companies, working to transform such challenges into opportunities through allocation of capital. We are looking for an Investment professional who is looking to grow with us. Currently, we are primarily focused on late stage private opportunities as well as public equities but will shortly be expanding into other asset classes. If you are up for the challenge, we can promise a great journey and an opportunity to learn the various parts of our business. Be part of a scaling organisation where your contributions directly influence our growth! What You Can Expect Salary: SGD 6,000 to 11,000 per month Benefits: Group Insurance. Medical Reimbursement. Health Screening Reimbursement. Vision Care and Health Reimbursement. Company culture: Strong emphasis on company core values to guide you in your day-to-day work and life. A dynamic team who will challenge and support you. Flexible and transparent work culture. Responsibilities Research on wide-ranging topics that include macro, thematic, industry, and company. Prepare detailed presentations, reports, and financial models. Develop independent views on investment opportunities. Manage investment due diligence process and various stakeholders involved. Monitor, review, and analyse the performance of existing investments. Collaborate in the design of the investment process and framework. Liaise with the Product team to develop in-house analytics tools. Assist other departments (such as Operations, Finance, etc.) as and when required. Essentials to Have At least 1-2 years of experience in investment banking, investment-related or relevant fields with a preference for direct experience in public and/or private equity. Excellent verbal and written communication skills (English). Ideal Requirements First principles thinker. Intellectually curious. Has a growth mindset. Able to learn and work independently. Able to manage multiple deliverables with a decent amount of uncertainty. Logical problem solver. Detailed oriented. Able to clearly articulate findings and conclusions in a concise manner. Proficient in financial modelling, MS Office, Bloomberg, Factset and other tools. Singapore Citizen or PR preferred. About You We are a firm expecting collaborators to be highly aligned with our core values. Therefore, you should be: Dedicated and Driven . You are a self-starter who is able to work effectively with low levels of supervision or minimum direction while under pressure in a fast-paced environment. You are energetic, resourceful, and a hands-on individual who pursues goals with sense of urgency. You readily cope with changing priorities, take initiative, and able to follow through with assigned tasks. Integrity and Ethics . You have a strong, ingrained sense of professional integrity and ethics. Entrepreneurial and Effective Team Member . You have the ability to see the commercial angle, while being mindful of associated risks. You have strong leadership qualities and can assume responsibilities, while being a team player within a high-performance group. Agile Thinker . You enjoy solving complex problems and able to see the big picture while mapping out the details to achieve your goals. You are creative about providing innovative solutions (new processes, technologies, and approaches). To Apply Please kindly prepare the following information and submit your job application via this link: https://www.korupartners.com/jobposting/?jobid=87 Resume. Academic Transcript. 500 words or less response to “ What matters to you the most? And why ?” (An email will be sent to you requesting this essay the next working day after the job application is submitted). Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis and all applications with missing information and no justifiable reason will be considered incomplete and will not be processed.

title: Finance Manager
description: About the Company A leading technology solutions provider delivering scalable, secure, and AI-enabled platforms to clients across the Asia-Pacific region. The company is at the forefront of cloud, SaaS, and IT innovation, driving digital transformation across industries. Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills, with proven stakeholder management capabilities. Reg. No: R21103564 EA License: 23S1921

title: Manager/Assistant Senior Manager (Experiential Learning), Undergraduate Studies Office
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description: About NUS Business School Founded in the same year that Singapore gained independence, NUS Business School stands today among the world’s leading business schools. It is distinctive for offering the best of global business knowledge with deep Asian insights, preparing students to lead Asian businesses to international success and to help global businesses succeed in Asia. For more than 50 years, NUS Business School has offered a rigorous, relevant and rewarding business education to outstanding students from across the world. That would not have been possible without a group of dedicated professionals who are passionate about building a positive environment to grow and groom future business leaders. And we are looking to add to our BIZ family! To learn more about the NUS Business School, please visit https://bschool.nus.edu.sg/ Job Description As part of the Student Life and Experiential Learning team, the Manager/Assistant Senior Manager will manage the Experiential Learning components of the NUS BBA, with primary responsibility for the Field Service Projects (FSP), a credit-bearing graduation requirement that integrates academic learning with real-world business problem-solving. This role provides end-to-end leadership of the FSP ecosystem, working closely with faculty, industry partners, students, and internal stakeholders to ensure academic rigor, operational excellence, and a high-quality student experience. At the same time, this role will also work closely with the Team Lead to oversee that Business Internships (BI), a key element of the Work Experience Milestone for NUS BBA students. Programme Ownership and Strategy Provide overall stewardship of Field Service Projects (FSP), including special series FSP with distinct objectives, as a core graduation requirement of the NUS BBA. Review and enhance FSP policies, frameworks, and processes to ensure alignment with programme learning outcomes and evolving industry expectations. Contribute to broader work experience and experiential learning strategy within the undergraduate curriculum. Industry and Faculty Engagement Develop, manage, and sustain relationships with industry partners to source meaningful, high-impact FSP projects. Work closely with faculty advisors to support project scoping, academic supervision, assessment alignment, and quality assurance. Serve as the key liaison between external partners, faculty, and the School. Student Experience and Delivery Oversee student lifecycle for FSPs, including project matching, briefings, timelines, issue management, and completion. Ensure consistent and transparent communication to students on expectations, requirements, and milestones. Manage student-related escalations and provide sound judgment on complex or exceptional cases. Operations and Governance Lead and execute end-to-end operational planning and execution of FSP cycles, including schedules, project approvals, grading liaison, and graduation clearance. Ensure compliance with University and School policies, assessment standards, and audit requirements. Analyse programme data and feedback to inform continuous improvement. Stakeholder and Team Collaboration Work closely with colleagues across the Undergraduate Studies Office, Career Services (BIZCareers), faculty teams, and central units. Where applicable, supervise or guide junior staff supporting experiential learning administration and delivery. Prepare management updates, reports, and recommendations for senior leadership. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree required; postgraduate qualification is an advantage. At least 5–8 years of relevant experience in higher education administration, programme management, or experiential learning initiatives. Experience managing complex, multi-stakeholder programmes is highly desirable. Strong programme management and organisational skills, with attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple timelines. Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills, including confidence working with faculty and industry partners. Sound judgment, maturity, and discretion in handling student and academic matters. Comfortable working in a structured yet evolving academic environment. Data-informed, improvement-oriented mindset. (Appointment job grade will commensurate with the selected candidate's experience)

title: Vice President - Loans Operations - Operations
description: Company overview Nomura is a global financial services group with an integrated network spanning approximately 30 countries and regions. By connecting markets East & West, Nomura services the needs of individuals, institutions, corporates and governments through its three business divisions: Wealth Management, Investment Management, and Wholesale (Global Markets and Investment Banking). Founded in 1925, the firm is built on a tradition of disciplined entrepreneurship, serving clients with creative solutions and considered thought leadership. For further information about Nomura, visit www.nomura.com Department Overview Nomura Loan Operations supports the Asia Ex-Japan Securitized Products & Private Credit Businesses. This is a high growth business, driven by innovative product portfolio, highly experienced team and strong franchise position, that operates in a rapidly expanding market segment. Loans Operations works closely with the Loans Origination and Trading desk providing support in all pre and post trade execution, product life cycle to ensure accurate risk capture in the firm’s books and records.  The candidate will act as the face-off between the Trading Desk and other stakeholders, identify and remediate control risks, maintain strong governance and drive strategic change initiatives. Responsibilities Portfolio Operations & Life Cycle Management o   Oversee end-to-end life cycle events and controls to ensure loans transactions are processed timely and accurately o    Monitor portfolio activity, pipeline and volume to assess operational capacity requirements o    Validate deal documentation and ensure correct system/product set-up to capture risks correctly. o    Provide trade support for other products traded by the Loans desk including vanilla cash Bonds and Equities, vanilla derivative products, and credit insurance Control, Risk Management and Governance o    Maintain strong operational controls to ensure compliance with internal policies, regulatory requirements and audit standards o    Lead and perform root cause analysis and implement action plans to improve controls/ productivity o    Review and present risk and control updates to senior management. Ensure risks are captured with appropriate action plans to address and manage risks indicators within risk threshold o    Strong understanding of front to back trade flows as well as Risk and P&L o    Assess new product initiatives and ensure operational readiness to support with appropriate controls Stakeholder Management o    Main Operational contact for Front Office and regional stakeholders across functions (legal, compliance, Finance, other operational teams, etc). o    Drive engagement with stakeholders to plan and deliver new deal origination, new product launches and complex transaction planning. o    Handle escalations, resolve issues within agreed timeline and ensure smooth coordination across functions to support the business o    Liaise with external parties including trade counterparties, agents, banks, intermediaries, to ensure timely trade execution and settlement Process Improvements o    Drive automation and digitalization initiatives to improve productivity and enhance controls o    Implement best practices and utilize data to drive reviews and facilitate decision making. Requirements Minimum 10 years experience supporting loan products. Demonstrable experience reviewing, interpreting trade documentation aligned to LMA/APLMA standards for Private Credit Business. Hands-on experience in syndicated loans, bilateral lending, agency servicing and loan products Prior experience with Loan IQ General knowledge of other Global Market products The candidate will need to manage and develop the team to deliver multiple priorities and support the business Strong stakeholder communication with confidence to challenge and escalate appropriately High attention to detail, with strong numerical skills and ability to manage operational risks Proven capability to manage complex transactions / resolve exceptions Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines.  Demonstrating a strong sense of ownership and commitment to delivery Possesses strong analytical and problem-solving skills, and ability to adapt to dynamic situations Self-motivated person, not afraid to suggest changes and re-invest process Nomura Competencies Explore Insights & Vision Identify the underlying causes of problems faced by you or your team and define a clear vision and direction for the future. Making Strategic Decisions Evaluate all the options for resolving the problems and effectively prioritize actions or recommendations. Inspire Entrepreneurship in People Inspire team members through effective communication of ideas and motivate them to actively enhance productivity. Elevate Organizational Capability Engage proactively in professional development and enhance team productivity through the promotion of knowledge sharing. Inclusion Respect DEI, foster a culture of psychological safety in the workplace and cultivate a "Risk Culture" (Challenge, Escalate and Respect). Diversity Statement Nomura is committed to an employment policy of equal opportunities, and is fundamentally opposed to any less favourable treatment accorded to existing or potential members of staff on the grounds of race, creed, colour, nationality, disability, marital status, pregnancy, gender or sexual orientation. title: Analyst, Market Business Partner
description:
Be a part of a revolutionary change At PMI, we’ve chosen to do something incredible. We’re totally transforming our business and building our future on one clear purpose – to deliver a smoke-free future. With huge change, comes huge opportunity. So, wherever you join us, you’ll enjoy the freedom to dream up and deliver better, brighter solutions and the space to move your career forward in endlessly different directions. Being part of the Finance team at PMI feels like building a new business – but with the accumulated expertise of a global brand behind you. Bring us your curiosity and capacity to engage people and you can be part of the Finance team that’s driving our commercial organization forward. Your ‘day to day’ In this role with Philip Morris Singapore Pte Ltd, you will
be responsible for the following: Financial Planning & Forecasting • Manage monthly & Financial Year volume forecast to
ensure forecast accuracy • Support monthly Integrated Business Planning, Monthly Business Updates & budget management - coordinate volume, Share of Market and financials target setting • Monitor key volume drivers across channels and seasonal cycles to enhance forecast accuracy and guide financial decision‑making. • Strengthen supply planning processes with the supply chain team to ensure accurate volume alignment, mitigate operational and inventory‑related risks, and enhance working capital efficiency. • Support Commercial budget, Original Budget/Rolling Forecast reporting and commercial budget allocation and deployment • Provide recommendations, insights, and Return of Investment evaluation on commercial initiatives, Point of Sale Marketing investment, contract outlets, Partner for Performance mechanics/analysis Commercial Finance/ Scenario Building • Deliver data‑driven performance evaluations to generate commercially relevant insights. • Analyze & simulate macro-economic trend to support strategic direction setting & decisions making • Support scenario modelling by analyzing key volume drivers and their implications for business performance. Commercial deployment • Ensure effective utilization of commercial resources to strengthen business performance oversight. • Define channel & customer segmentation and determine priorities • Develop and support channel / account strategy • Support commercial cycle objectives framing and deployment Who we’re looking for • Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience holder, preferably in Finance, Economics, or Business Administration. • Minimum of 3 years of related experiences • Strong business acumen and budgeting & forecasting skills. • Proficient in financial modeling, analytical, and problem-solving skills. • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively collaborate with diverse teams. • Proactive, results-oriented, and able to work independently as well as part of a team.

title: Global Banking & Markets, Transformation Office, Analyst, Singapore
description: ABOUT GLOBAL BANKING & MARKETS Our core value is building strong relationships with our institutional clients, which include corporations, financial service providers, and fund managers. We help them buy and sell financial products on exchanges around the world, raise funding, and manage risk. This is a dynamic, entrepreneurial team with a passion for the markets, with individuals who thrive in fast-paced, changing environments and are energized by a bustling trading floor. Global Banking & Markets: Transformation Office Are you a looking for a new opportunity to showcase your intellectual curiosity, advisory and project management skills? Do you have a strategic mindset and the ability to influence change? Do you wish to be valued for your innovative approach to problem-solving? We have an excellent new position available within our Transformation Office team for Global banking & Markets. The Global Banking & Markets Transformation Office Team is searching for dedicated individuals who thrives in a collaborative and challenging environment to join our team. Global Change Managers serve as program or product managers on high priority initiatives within the Global Banking & Markets Division. You will work alongside other Change Managers, Product Managers, Sales, Strats, Engineers and Designers to ensure solutions are designed and implemented for the Global Banking & Markets Division in true ‘front to back’ fashion. title: Senior Software Engineer
description: JOB SUMMARY SABRE is Standard Chartered’ s Analytics Booking and Risk Engine and is the strategic risk and PnL platform for Financial Markets. The development team is a world class technology group in Standard Chartered bank to provide technical solutions to support the bank’s Financial Markets business as well as support requirements from other projects with a wider scope such as regulatory books of work. We bring a deep understanding of the market, a scientific approach, and innovative technologies to bear on the challenges of profitable trading in the world's highly competitive environment. This is a rare opportunity to get into an organization working with smart, great technologists globally in financial markets domain. The culture in SABRE is open, intellectual, and fun. Learning opportunities are plentiful: organized classes and seminars; freewheeling discussions; intensive one-on-one mentorships. Career advancement is always waiting for those high-energized talents to pursue. The successful candidate will play a leading role in the implementation of highly visible strategic programmes with the primary responsibilities around building components within the SABRE risk platform. Any developed solution need to be scalable, robust and fault tolerant catering to the existing and future business needs in a timely manner. In addition the successful candidate will be expected to develop innovative solutions with a view to these being released quickly to gain feedback before advising on the full scale roll out. The candidate will be a natural leader and mentor with a driven yet pragmatic, delivery focused mindset and a desire to deliver constant business value through the effective and innovative use of technology with a proven background in delivering high quality business critical software. The candidate will be expected to take ownership, responsibility and accountability for critical deliveries. In addition, the successful candidate will have come from a mature and demonstrable agile background and be comfortable dealing with the resistance that comes with change. An important aspect of the role is second line support in a dynamic and fast-paced front-office environment. The candidate need to be comfortable talking directly with the business to understand the issue and take ownership of the issue to provide a timely and accurate resolution. The candidate will work closely with the other team members and senior stakeholders in order to design and implement critical strategic solutions whilst collaborating and engaging with Business Stakeholders to deliver to exacting requirements. The candidate is expected to work in a dynamic global team with business analysts, project managers, business stakeholders and other technical experts across multiple locations Key Roles & Responsibilities •    Work closely with the Development Manager, Project Manager, and a team of developers to deliver quality features and enhancements to the internal business users •    Demonstrate a systematic and disciplined architectural, system design and programming approach •    Deeply passionate about best-practice software design principles, you will write robust code along with a high-level of test coverage •    Self-starter who can drive/facilitate requirements and development effort •    Design smart and scalable solutions following the bank standards •    Coordinate with other teams in Technology •    Develop, prepare and maintain system documentation, including program descriptions and operational procedures •    Act as the second line of support during production problems. Diversity & Inclusion Standard Chartered is committed to diversity and inclusion. We believe that a work environment which embraces diversity will enable us to get the best out of the broadest spectrum of people to sustain strong business performance and competitive advantage. By building an inclusive culture, each employee can develop a sense of belonging, and have the opportunity to maximize their personal potential. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Lead to achieve the outcomes set out in the Bank’s Conduct Principles •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Our Ideal Candidate •	8+ years C#/WPF/.NET development experience building performance critical rich desktop applications •	8+ years hands on development within investment banking IT •	Strong knowledge and experience of working in Agile teams with Agile practices •	Strong understanding of the software development life cycle •	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills •	Strong understanding of technology and the ability to learn new technologies quickly •	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build and maintain relationships with all stakeholders •	Self-starter that is enthusiastic and proactive •	Be able to work in a fast-paced front-office environment •	Be able to prioritize and work on multiple workstreams simultaneously •	Be able to take ownership of issues and projects, and see them through to delivery •	Possess good judgement and decision making skills with a pragmatic and common-sense approach to solving problems Role Specific Technical Competencies •	C#/WPF/.NET development of rich desktop applications •	Multithreaded, concurrent, and asynchronous programming using TPL and Rx •	MVVM frameworks, preferably Prism •	Dependency Injection containers, preferably Unity •	LINQ/Rx with a functional mindset and coding style •	RESTful APIs and message queues such as ActiveMQ •	DevExpress, Infragistics, or any other popular WPF component library •	Object-Oriented Design and Programming (OOD/OOP) •	NUnit, Moq, TDD, BDD, UI Automation •	CI/CD Pipelines such as Azure DevOps •	Agile/Scrum practices •	Visual Studio and ReSharper About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. title: Enterprise Risk / Operational Risk (Listed FI)
description: Lico Resources is partnering with a prominent financial market infrastructure provider at the heart of Singapore’s capital markets. This organisation plays a pivotal role in safeguarding systemic stability and is committed to upholding the highest standards of enterprise risk management. A unique opportunity has arisen for an experienced and detail-oriented risk professional to join the Operational/Enterprise Risk team in a highly visible role. This position offers broad exposure to group-wide operations and senior stakeholders, contributing directly to the effectiveness of the enterprise risk framework across critical business and support functions. Key Responsibilities Enhance and implement the enterprise risk management (ERM) framework in alignment with global standards such as COSO and ISO 31000. Prepare materials and coordinate inputs for senior governance forums, including Board-level risk and audit committees. Lead the facilitation of Risk and Control Self-Assessments (RCSA) with various business units; maintain central risk registers and control inventories. Perform and document assessments of inherent and residual risks, control effectiveness, and ensure timely closure of action plans. Investigate operational risk incidents, perform root cause analysis, and track remediation actions to prevent recurrence. Develop and maintain dashboards, risk appetite indicators, and emerging risk summaries for executive reporting. Support enterprise-wide change initiatives by evaluating risk implications of new processes, technologies, and services. Provide input into product and service risk assessments, particularly for new or significantly altered offerings. Collaborate with resilience and technology teams to integrate enterprise risk perspectives into testing outcomes, recovery planning, and critical dependency mapping. Coordinate responses to regulatory reviews, audits, and internal assurance exercises, ensuring follow-through on recommendations. Ensure data quality and consistency across GRC systems; support workflow optimisation through process and tool enhancements. Deliver targeted risk training sessions and facilitate awareness-building engagements across the organisation. Qualifications & Experience **Essential:** A recognised Bachelor's degree in Finance, Business, Engineering, Information Systems, or a related discipline. 4–6 years of relevant experience in enterprise risk, operational risk, internal audit, or control functions within financial services or market infrastructure environments. Strong grasp of ERM practices including RCSA, KRIs, risk appetite frameworks, incident analysis, and regulatory expectations (especially MAS guidelines). Excellent communication and analytical skills, with the ability to produce concise, insightful reports for executive audiences. Adept at working across functions and facilitating risk workshops with stakeholders from varied disciplines. Proficient in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint; working knowledge of GRC platforms (e.g., Archer, ServiceNow GRC) is beneficial. **Preferred:** Exposure to data visualisation tools such as Power BI or Tableau. Professional certifications such as FRM, PRM, CRM, CIA, CISA, or equivalent. ### Personal Attributes Highly accountable, with a proactive and structured approach to execution. Calm and dependable in fast-paced, regulatory-driven environments. Team-oriented with strong influencing and relationship-building skills. Upholds high standards of integrity and sound judgment, aligned with the organisation’s role in maintaining market confidence. --- If you are interested in this role, please send us your updated resume today to nicole@licoresources.com quoting reference number **A09012**. title: Trade Finance Relationship Manager
description: About the company: One of the largest and most reputable financial institutions in the world, with a strong presence in both domestic and international markets. Known for its comprehensive range of banking services committed to providing innovative financial solutions and fostering long-term relationships with its clients. The bank prides itself on its robust corporate governance, customer-centric approach, and dedication to sustainable development. Job responsibilities: - Develop and maintain strong relationships with corporate clients to understand their trade finance needs and provide tailored solutions. - Manage and grow a portfolio of trade finance clients, ensuring high levels of customer satisfaction and retention. - Collaborate with internal teams to structure and execute trade finance transactions, including letters of credit, guarantees, and supply chain finance. - Conduct market research and analysis to identify new business opportunities and stay updated on industry trends. - Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and internal policies in all trade finance activities. - Prepare and present detailed reports on trade finance activities and performance to senior management. - Provide expert advice and guidance to clients on trade finance products and services. Job requirements: - Bachelor's degree in Finance, Business Administration, or a related field; advanced degree preferred. - Minimum of 8 years of experience in trade finance or corporate banking, with a proven track record of managing client relationships. - Strong understanding of trade finance products, services, and market dynamics. - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build rapport with clients and internal stakeholders. - Proven analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to make sound business decisions. - Proficiency in financial software and Microsoft Office Suite. - Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment.

title: Management Consultant - KYC/AML/SOW

Since 1996, we've been shaping the future of financial services by delivering cutting-edge expertise in strategy, operations, and technology to banks and insurers worldwide. What sets us apart is our people. At Synpulse, we cultivate a collaborative, high-impact culture where initiative and creativity are valued. With 21 offices across Europe, the Americas, and Asia-Pacific, we bring together diverse perspectives and talents. We believe innovation thrives where everyone feels they belong and can contribute. We don’t just offer jobs – we offer the chance to develop, make an impact, and be part of a team that’s redefining the future of financial services. You will play a key role in building trusted client relationships through high quality deliverables and leveraging our industry expertise. Our consultants are our greatest asset, and we show this by providing a comprehensive training and mentoring program to equip you to become a skilled consultant. Conduct research, data collection and analysis on the needs and issues of the client through interviews and workshops to translate their requirements into well-formulated solutions. Present findings and recommendations to the client using business proposals and presentation decks. Document the project into systems and processes for management review. Close interaction with clients through regular project updates and networking. Support team members with other project activities. Job Requirements: Degree holder. At least 5 years of professional experience in AML/KYC, financial crime compliance, SOW (Source of Wealth) within Private Banking or consulting sector. Knowledge of Business Integration / Migration / TOM projects within Private Banking. Good understanding of core business processes and products in the Private Banking industry. Excellent analytical and conceptual skills. Excellent presentation and communication skills with clients and colleagues. Capable of applying technology principles to business. Excellent spoken and written English. Why us: Flexible working hours with part-time working models and hybrid options Attractive fringe benefits and salary structures in line with the market Modern and central office space with good public transport connections Can-do mentality and one-spirit culture Varied events and employee initiatives Your documents to start the process: Resume Job references Qualifications (bachelor/ master diploma, etc.) with certificate of grades Motivation letter: Why Synpulse? Are you proactive and willing to go the extra mile for your clients? Are you motivated not only to design solutions but also to implement them? As a flexible and goal-oriented person, you will quickly assume entrepreneurial responsibility with us. Do you appreciate the spirit of a growing international company with Swiss roots and a strong corporate culture? Then we look forward to receiving your online application at . Synpulse Careers

title: Head of Finance
description: Xendit provides payment infrastructure across Southeast Asia, with a focus on Indonesia, the Philippines and Malaysia. We process payments, power marketplaces, disburse payroll and loans, provide KYC solutions, prevent fraud, and help businesses grow exponentially. We serve our customers by providing a suite of world-class APIs, eCommerce platform integrations, and easy to use applications for individual entrepreneurs, SMEs, and enterprises alike. Our main focus is building the most advanced payment rails for Southeast Asia, with a clear goal in mind — to make payments across and within SEA simple, secure and easy for everyone. We serve thousands of businesses ranging from SMEs to multinational enterprises, and process millions of transactions monthly. We’ve been growing rapidly since our inception in 2015, onboarding hundreds of new customers every month, and backed by global top-10 VCs . We’re proud to be featured on among the fastest growing companies by Y-Combinator . About the Job You will be leading Xendit Group’s Finance and Accounting department, overseeing several areas such as Accounting, FP&A, Treasury, Internal Audit and Tax. This is a hands-on leadership role that requires someone who is able to support both high-level complex accounting and finance responsibilities as well as not being afraid to dive into the details on a day-to-day basis. You will be working closely with the CEO and COO as part of the executive team, as well as manage the current team of +40 in the finance org in different countries from junior to senior level. In addition, as part of the business partnering responsibilities, you will work closely with the Corporate Development (M&A), Finance Operations, Sales and Marketing senior management as well as the Engineering team to drive efficiency and potential cost savings plus other business leaders. This includes, but is not limited to, functions such as: accounting, audits, tax compliance, managing an ERP implementation and automation initiatives, month-end closing processes, monthly reporting, forecasting, budgeting, long range planning, business partnering, modeling, return on investment analysis and other value added financial analysis. Minimum Qualifications Minimum 7-10 years of experience in Finance and/or Accounting, with at least 3-5 years of leading a finance org and taken a company through a rapid growth stage Bachelor's degree required; Accounting or Finance preferred; Master’s degree preferred, CPA desirable Any combination of relevant education and experience and/or related professional designations/certifications in this field is highly desirable Regional experience in Southeast Asia (bonus if you have US experience as well) Experience in payments, fintech or technology industry and start up market expansion Proven track record of developing and implementing successful financial strategies Essential Skills Ability to pay strong attention to detail and deliver work that is of a high standard Ability to work in a self-directed and remote environment Demonstrated ability to draft clear and professional correspondence and other documents of a legal, compliance and business nature Excellent analysis and problem-solving skills Strong process improvement and automation mindset, with the ability to identify inefficiencies and implement practical solutions Structured and clear communication within team and cross-team Excellent demonstrated organizational skills, including time management, record keeping, self-scheduling, and follow-up Demonstrated ability to handle multiple priorities in a fast-paced and dynamic environment Able to work independently as an individual contributor, but also be able to lead a team as we grow Preferred Qualifications Expertise in finance process efficiency, automations, cost management, and financial controls Been on the acquiring side of M&As Involvement/experience in raising capital is a plus Responsibilities Main priorities Lead and scale Finance Organization: oversee Accounting, FP&A, Audit, and Finance Automation across multiple markets. Scale Finance Organization and support new market expansions by optimizing current resources and driving efficiency. Oversee group audits and ensure timely completion within six months of year-end, with minimal or no audit adjustments. Drive finance Automation: lead automation initiatives in reconciliation, financial reporting, and billing to enhance accuracy, speed, and efficiency. Foster a culture of continuous improvement and process excellence. Enhance cash flow and working capital: improve cash conversion and AR days Strengthen FP&A: redesign FP&A processes, automate management reporting, and empower budget owners with financial insights. Lead end-to-end financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and performance analysis to ensure optimal resource allocation. Provide strategic leadership: partner with senior management to translate financial insights into strategic actions that drive growth, profitability, and long-term value creation. Other responsibilities M&A and capital strategy: collaborate closely with the M&A team on capital planning, fundraising, and strategic transactions. Treasury and liquidity management: work in partnership with Treasury and Finance Operations to ensure effective liquidity, funding, and cash flow management. IPO and Corporate Development Readiness: support the company’s long-term IPO readiness and related financial transformation initiatives. Enterprise-wide support: lead finance-related components of organization-wide initiatives, including post-merger integrations (PMI) and other strategic projects.

title: Risk Analyst (Welcome Fresh Graduates)
description: Abaxx Exchange is looking for a Risk Analyst who will assist in overseeing and shaping the financial risk management practice in Singapore. The candidate will join a dynamic team, be exposed to a wide range of products and be challenged with complex risk problems. This is an exciting, multifaceted role where you will interact with various teams to broaden your exposure. Responsibilities: Risk Control Assist with the establishment and maintenance of the risk management framework by adhering to internal risk policies and staying up to date with regulatory standards and industry best practices Execute daily risk control processes including initial and variation margin calculations, mark-to-market valuations, and risk reports, working alongside Clearing Operation teams to manage clearing risk Investigate and document margin breaches and risk limit excesses, including root cause analysis, member communication, escalation procedures, and regulatory reporting requirements. Maintain detailed audit trails and contribute to post-incident reviews to strengthen risk controls Support the calculation and monitoring of option margin requirements using portfolio margining methodologies (e.g., SPAN, VaR-based approaches), including reviewing volatility surface parameters, stress scenarios, and option Greeks for complex positions Assist in the administration of non-cash collateral including government securities, letters of credit, and emerging digital assets. Support the evaluation of collateral valuation methodologies, haircut policies, and operational processes including digital title transfer mechanisms for tokenized assets Monitor collateral adequacy and concentration limits across clearing members, including daily reconciliation of posted collateral against margin requirements and guarantee fund contributions Build and maintain reports for regular management, member and regulatory reporting Monitor market trends, geopolitical events that may impact products to proactively identify emerging risk and opportunities Develop and maintain close working relationships with the Clearing & Exchange Operations, Compliance and Finance teams Compile and provide information to external parties (e.g. regulators, auditors, external independent validators, consultants) on routine and ad-hoc bases Participate in new product launches, new member onboarding, regular clearing member reviews and run thematic or ad-hoc market research on relevant risk topics Risk Development Contribute to development and enhancement of in-house risk application leveraging Python, SQL, GIT and AWS cloud infrastructure to support advanced analytics and risk management process. Monitor the performance of risk models, ensuring their appropriateness and maintaining the integrity and accuracy of risk data. Participate in stress testing exercises to evaluate margin adequacy under extreme market scenarios, including reverse stress testing and sensitivity analysis for key risk parameters Develop and leverage upon advanced analytics tools and models, e.g. tools and models related to Artificial Intelligence (AI) Design test cases and perform testing for risk systems development and system upgrades Contribute to the development and successful delivery of visual analytics initiatives. These initiatives typically include the development of reports, analysis, data visualizations and dashboards from aggregated data sources such as risk databases with the objective of bringing risk monitoring, analysis and strategic decision making to the next level. Risk Culture Contribute to the establishment and promotion of risk culture within the Company including through daily responsibilities, awareness efforts and training efforts Contribute to the culture of governance and risk oversight within the Company Qualifications: Degree in Risk Management, Business, Economics, Mathematics, Science, Engineering or IT, or a related discipline Working level competency in Excel VBA/SQL/Python and Microsoft Office applications Understanding of derivatives products including futures and options, margin methodologies, and collateral management principles preferred Strong attention to detail with a focus on process execution Keen interest in financial, commodity and energy markets and derivative instruments Key player in an agile and diverse environment that emphasizes proactive collaboration and risk identification Quick learner with excellent written and communication skills, team orientation, and ability to work with counterparts in different organizational areas Self-starter who takes initiative to improve the current state of things and is adaptable to change Prior relevant professional experience in risk management, data analysis, portfolio management, and/or risk systems in prime brokerages, fund houses or clearing houses preferred

title: Senior Digital Marketing Executive

Our mission is “ We Care to Make You a Better Investor ”. Through our community with thousands of members in Asia and learning platforms, we have so far trained at least 50,000 people in Asia. Countries and cities that we have impacted include Malaysia (Kuching, Kuala Lumpur, Penang), Thailand (Bangkok), Cambodia (Phnom Penh), Vietnam (Ho Chi Min City), Hong Kong, Japan (Tokyo) and of course our own home, Singapore. You will have a chance to be the pioneer in spearheading new strategies. 2. We are result oriented and we believe in compensation that correlates with results . Our Top Performers in FY 2024 received a total of 8 months bonus. You are the limit! 3. We give you the space to do crazy things as long as it works . We are unlike MNCs or big companies. We grow by breaking the rules. We constantly challenge ourselves to scale new heights by doing new things. If you dislike red tape, this is the place. 4. You will get a chance to meet very successful people in the speaking industry. Our past Keynote Speakers include Mary Buffett, the Internationally Acclaimed Author and Speaker of How Billionaire Warren Buffett invests, Lauren Templeton, etc. 5. Regular lunches, Movie Shows, Popcorns, Makan, etc! We do these regularly after every project! The best part is we do ALL these Fun things during office hours! 6. Office politics is a dirty phrase, and we eliminate any politics and bureaucracy 7. We constantly DO New Things. If you dislike routines and wanted to learn New Things, you will get a chance. 8. Most importantly, you will learn from the Founder, Cayden Chang, himself. (C) WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR 1) 3 to 5 years of hands-on experience in digital marketing and sales funnel management, with a proven track record of results. 2) Strong written communication skills, with hands-on experience crafting effective email copy that drives engagement and results. 3) Meticulous with the ability to handle administrative and reporting efficiently and collaborate effectively across teams. 4) Familiarity with tools such as Aweber, Facebook Ads Manager, Google Ads, Google Tag Manager, and AI-powered marketing tools is a plus. 5) Experience acquiring online memberships or subscriptions is a strong advantage . (D) WHAT YOU DO 1) Plan and execute digital marketing campaigns across Google, YouTube, TikTok, Facebook, and other relevant channels to drive real, measurable results. 2) Build and optimize end-to-end sales funnels, including landing pages, ad copy, email sequences, and remarketing campaigns for both new and existing customers. 3) Own the customer acquisition budget, monitor performance closely, and ensure KPIs and ROI targets are met. 4) Collaborate closely with in-house and external marketing, content, and creative teams to support business growth goals. 5) Set up, manage, and analyze marketing analytics to track performance, identify trends, and improve conversion outcomes. E) TO APPLY, YOU NEED TO DO THE FOLLOWING 1) Provide cover letter why we should meet you. 2) Email résumé to resume@mindkinesis.com NOTE: Please note that we don't read off-the-shelve résumé.

title: Research Analyst
description: Key Responsibilities: Advanced Market Research & Quantitative Analysis Conduct deep quantitative analysis of global dry bulk, tanker, and container shipping markets, integrating physical freight fundamentals and derivatives (FFA) pricing dynamics. Assess geopolitical, regulatory, and macroeconomic developments and quantify their impact on freight demand, ton-mile evolution, and forward curves. 1. Model Development & Data Engineering: Conduct advanced quantitative research on Forward Freight Agreements (FFAs) across dry bulk and/or tanker markets. Design, build, and maintain end-to-end analytical pipelines using Python and R, including data ingestion, cleaning, feature engineering, and validation. Develop statistical and econometric models for freight forecasting (e.g. regression models, regime-switching models, Monte Carlo simulations, scenario analysis). Build and maintain forward-curve models, basis analysis, and spread structures across tenors. Automate recurring analyses and reporting workflows, replacing manual Excel processes with reproducible code-based solutions. Create detailed reports, presentations, and dashboards to communicate findings effectively to stakeholders. 2. Industry Monitoring: Track key industry metrics such as vessel utilization, shipping supply and demand, and pricing trends. Monitor competitor activity, trade agreements, and global economic indicators influencing trade flows. 3. Forecasting & Strategic Insights: Write production-quality Python code to support market analysis, dashboards, and internal tools. Create interactive dashboards to visualize freight curves, volatility, seasonality, and scenario outcomes. Integrate multiple data sources into coherent analytical frameworks. Apply version control and follow disciplined coding and documentation standards. Analyse freight market risk using volatility metrics, stress testing, and scenario analysis. Translate complex quantitative outputs into clear commercial insights for trading, chartering, and senior management teams. 4. Collaboration & Communication: Continuously monitor shipping indices, broker reports, and market intelligence sources, critically assessing data quality and bias. Work closely with commercial, trading, and risk teams to support real-time decision-making. Liaise with external stakeholders, such as analysts, consultants, and industry experts, to validate and enhance insights. Able to closely monitor market movements and work overnight when required. 5. Risk Assessment: Identify potential risks and opportunities in trade and shipping operations, including supply chain disruptions and regulatory impacts. Propose mitigation strategies and solutions based on research findings. Requirements: Education Bachelor’s degree or higher in Quantitative Finance, Applied Mathematics, Applied physic, Data Analytics, Applied Analytics, Maritime Studies. Shipping & Market Knowledge Demonstrated experience in shipping research, freight analytics, commodity markets, or freight derivatives (FFA). Strongunderstanding of both physical and paper freight markets, including: 1. Supply/demand mechanics 2. Freight rate formation 3. Forward curves, spreads, and seasonality Prior exposure to dry bulk and/or tanker markets is highly preferred. Experience with derivatives pricing, volatility modelling, or risk management is an advantage. Programming & Technical Skills Strong proficiency in data analysis tools Familiarity with version control tools and structured research or development workflows. Prior experience in financial services, trading, research, or related commercial industries is an advantage. Experience supporting or developing internal tools, reports, or dashboards for business or commercial use. Analytical & Quantitative Skills Strong grounding in statistics, time-series analysis, and forecasting techniques. Ability to independently design analytical frameworks and challenge assumptions. High attention to detail and data integrity. Communication Ability to clearly explain complex quantitative results to non-technical stakeholders. Strong written communication skills for producing structured research reports and presentations.

title: Institute of Higher Learning Course / Program Director
description: Stag Match Institute (SMI) is seeking a Independent , resourceful, dynamic and collaborative Course/Program Director to provide strategic leadership and management for a portfolio of courses and programs, with a significant focus on fostering strong linkages with the Continuing Education and Training (CET) sector and the wider Institute of Higher Learning (IHL) landscape in Singapore. This pivotal role encompasses business development , sales and academic leadership across the assigned portfolio, curriculum development aligned with industry needs and CET frameworks, ensuring student success across diverse offerings, and crucially, driving enrollment growth through effective outreach and strategic partnerships within the CET and IHL ecosystem. The ideal candidate will be a visionary leader ,resourceful and sale oriental with a passion for lifelong learning, a deep understanding of the CET landscape and IHL collaborations, and proven experience in managing multiple programs and engaging with diverse stakeholders. Responsibilities : Academic Leadership & Portfolio Management (CET & IHL Focus): * Provide strategic direction and academic leadership for a portfolio of courses and programs, ensuring alignment with the institute's overall academic vision, quality standards, and the evolving needs of the CET sector and relevant IHL initiatives. * Oversee all aspects of program delivery within the portfolio, including curriculum design, development, review, and continuous improvement, with a focus on modularity, stackability, and relevance to working professionals and industry demands within the CET framework. Oversea and responsible for sale and business development with existing and new partnership Manage and mentor course coordinators and faculty members teaching within the portfolio, fostering a collaborative environment that encourages engagement with CET methodologies and IHL best practices. Ensure all programs within the portfolio adhere to relevant accreditation standards, regulatory requirements, and the specific quality benchmarks of CET programs in Singapore (e.g., SkillsFuture). Monitor student progress across the portfolio, provide guidance on academic and career pathways relevant to CET, and implement strategies to enhance student retention and success, including recognition of prior learning (RPL) where applicable. Develop and manage the overall budget for the assigned portfolio, ensuring resources are allocated efficiently to support program goals and collaborative CET/IHL activities. Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with industry partners, SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG), other CET providers, and relevant departments within other IHLs to ensure the portfolio's relevance, create opportunities for student upskilling and reskilling, and explore potential collaborations across different programs. Sales & Enrollment Growth (Portfolio & CET/IHL Engagement): Develop and implement strategic enrollment plans for the portfolio of courses and programs, with a specific focus on attracting working professionals, career switchers, and individuals seeking CET qualifications for various offerings. * Represent the institute's CET-related programs at recruitment events, industry seminars, and collaborative initiatives with other IHLs and CET providers. * Collaborate closely with the marketing and admissions teams to develop targeted marketing materials, website content, and digital campaigns that resonate with the CET audience for the diverse program offerings and highlight their alignment with national skills frameworks. * Engage with prospective CET learners and their employers across the portfolio, providing detailed information about the various program curricula, career advancement opportunities, and the benefits of utilizing SkillsFuture credits and other CET funding schemes. * Conduct presentations and workshops for prospective CET students and corporate clients across the portfolio, emphasizing the relevance of different programs to industry needs and their articulation with other IHL qualifications where applicable. * Track and analyze enrollment data across the portfolio, including the profile of CET learners in different programs, to evaluate the effectiveness of recruitment strategies and partnerships within the CET and IHL ecosystem. * Build and maintain relationships with alumni from various programs who have leveraged their education for career progression within the CET framework. * Explore and implement innovative recruitment strategies tailored to the CET audience for the diverse portfolio, including partnerships with employers, online learning platforms, and collaborations with other training providers and IHLs. Qualifications : A degree in a relevant field. Minimum of 2 years of experience in academic administration or program management within a higher education institution, with demonstrable experience in or strong understanding of the CET sector in Singapore. Demonstrated experience in developing curriculum relevant to industry needs and aligned with CET frameworks (e.g., modularization, stackability) across multiple programs. Proven track record of successful student recruitment, particularly within the adult learning or CET market, for a range of educational offerings. Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and influence diverse audiences, including industry professionals and CET stakeholders, across different program areas. Strong leadership, organizational, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects and initiatives concurrently and a collaborative mindset. Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment and across different institutions and organizations. Familiarity with the higher education and CET landscape in Singapore, including SkillsFuture initiatives and relevant funding schemes. Experience in developing and managing partnerships with industry and/or CET providers across a portfolio of programs is highly desirable. title: Chief Commercial Officer (International Education)
description: About the Role This is a newly created Executive Leadership position with a strong regional growth mandate, with particular emphasis on the China and ASEAN markets. As SagePaths Global Education continues its expansion across Asia, the Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will play a pivotal role in shaping the Group’s commercial strategy, driving sustainable revenue growth, and strengthening its international education ecosystem. The CCO will be responsible for building a scalable, data-driven, and high-performance commercial framework that integrates student recruitment, admissions, marketing, partnerships, and digital systems across all business units. This role is instrumental in translating strategic vision into commercial success and long-term market leadership. About SagePaths Global Education SagePaths Global Education is a leading regional education group headquartered in Singapore, operating a diversified portfolio of education and education-related businesses. Our brands include SagePaths Global Education, Singapore Education Development Centre, SagePartners Business Consultancy, SOVO Student Apartment, Pickering Institute, Pickering Global Campus, as well as a growing network of Overseas Teaching Centres across China. We specialise in building a fully integrated education ecosystem that supports international students across their entire academic journey, including: International student placement and recruitment Cross-business services centred around international students Student accommodation development and operations Academic pathways and university articulation programmes Foundation and Diploma programmes Online and blended education delivery Transnational education and cross-border programme delivery Our vision is to build a globally connected education ecosystem that empowers students through high-quality, accessible, and future-oriented learning pathways. We work closely with universities, private education institutions, and overseas partners to deliver well-recognised academic programmes and meaningful international collaboration opportunities. As we enter our next phase of strategic development, we are strengthening our Executive Leadership Team to support rapid regional expansion and organisational scaling. The Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will be a core member of this leadership group, working directly with the Managing Director and the Board to lead the Group’s commercial transformation, revenue growth strategy, and international market expansion. This is a rare opportunity to shape the commercial architecture of a fast-growing international education group and to make a lasting impact on its regional and global footprint. Key Responsibilities Reporting directly to the Managing Director, the Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) will lead the Group’s end-to-end commercial strategy, with full responsibility across: Marketing, branding, and corporate communications for all SagePaths and Pickering brands Student recruitment strategy, sales performance management, admissions operations, and conversion optimisation Agent network development, channel management, and strategic partnerships (both domestic and international) Commercial systems architecture, including CRM, websites, digital marketing platforms, and data analytics infrastructure Pricing strategy, revenue diversification initiatives, and market expansion planning The role carries executive accountability to: Drive sustainable enrolment growth and strong revenue performance across both onshore and offshore markets Build a predictable, data-driven recruitment pipeline supported by disciplined sales governance and reporting structures Optimise admissions workflows, conversion rates, and overall student acquisition efficiency Lead CRM and digital platform integration to deliver a seamless, consistent, and high-quality student recruitment experience Strengthen commercial compliance with Singapore PEI regulations (SSG, EduTrust), overseas regulatory frameworks, and partner contractual obligations Champion an exceptional student experience from first engagement through to enrolment, consistently applied across all business units Develop and execute a customer-centric, brand-driven marketing strategy aligned with SagePaths’ premium and professional positioning Build, manage, and scale strategic partnerships with universities, private education institutions, overseas schools, and pathway providers Establish scalable commercial processes, structures, and performance frameworks that support rapid regional and international growth The Successful Candidate As a key member of the Executive Leadership Team, the CCO will work closely with the Managing Director, Executive Dean, and Operations to translate strategic direction into measurable and sustainable commercial performance. We are seeking a commercially astute, internationally minded leader with: A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Commerce, or a related discipline; postgraduate qualifications are strongly preferred 10 years of senior commercial leadership experience, MUST within international education, transnational education, or higher education services Bilingual proficiency in English and Mandarin Chinese is essential , with the ability to conduct professional-level communication, negotiations, and relationship management with business partners, universities, and partner institutions across Chin Proven experience leading large-scale student recruitment, admissions, and marketing operations across multiple markets Deep understanding of international education ecosystems, student mobility trends, agent networks, and education partnerships Strong familiarity with compliance frameworks, including Singapore PEI / EduTrust requirements and international regulatory standards A strong track record in building, scaling, and optimising CRM systems, digital marketing platforms, and performance dashboards Experience integrating CRM with LMS, finance, admissions, and student management systems A strategic mindset combined with strong execution capability and operational discipline Exceptional leadership skills with the ability to build high-performing, values-driven teams across marketing, recruitment, and admissions Comfort and effectiveness operating within a multi-country, multi-brand, and fast-growth environment Regular travel across Singapore, China, and Southeast Asia will be required to support regional operations and partnerships.

This pivotal role encompasses business development , sales and academic leadership across the assigned portfolio, curriculum development aligned with industry needs and CET frameworks, ensuring student success across diverse offerings, and crucially, driving enrollment growth through effective outreach and strategic partnerships within the CET and IHL ecosystem. The ideal candidate will be a visionary leader, Hand-on  ,resourceful and sale oriental with a passion for lifelong learning, a deep understanding of the CET landscape and IHL collaborations, and proven experience in managing multiple programs and engaging with diverse stakeholders. Responsibilities: Academic Leadership & Portfolio Management (CET & IHL Focus): Provide strategic direction and academic leadership for a portfolio of courses and programs, ensuring alignment with the institute's overall academic vision, quality standards, and the evolving needs of the CET sector and relevant IHL initiatives. Support all aspects of program delivery within the portfolio, including curriculum design, development, review, and continuous improvement, with a focus on modularity, stackability, and relevance to working professionals and industry demands within the CET framework. Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with industry partners, SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG), other CET providers, and relevant departments within other IHLs to ensure the portfolio's relevance, create opportunities for student upskilling and reskilling, and explore potential collaborations across different programs. Supporting learners in their career placement pathway . Creating Recruitment fair for industry partners and learners to network and create placement event. Sales & Enrollment Growth (Portfolio & CET/IHL Engagement): Develop and implement strategic enrollment plans for the portfolio of courses and programs, with a specific focus on attracting working professionals, career switchers, and individuals seeking CET qualifications for various offerings. Represent the institute's CET-related programs at recruitment events, industry seminars, and collaborative initiatives with other IHLs and CET providers. Collaborate closely with the marketing and admissions teams to develop targeted marketing materials, website content, and digital campaigns that resonate with the CET audience for the diverse program offerings and highlight their alignment with national skills frameworks. Engage with prospective CET learners and their employers across the portfolio, providing detailed information about the various program curricula, career advancement opportunities, and the benefits of utilizing SkillsFuture credits and other CET funding schemes. Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and influence diverse audiences, including industry professionals and CET stakeholders, across different program areas. Strong leadership, organizational, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects and initiatives concurrently and a collaborative mindset. Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment and across different institutions and organizations. Familiarity with the higher education and CET landscape in Singapore, including SkillsFuture initiatives and relevant funding schemes. Experience in developing and managing partnerships with industry and/or CET providers across a portfolio of programs is highly desirable. Experience with CRM systems and enrollment management software, as well as platforms relevant to CET learners, is an advantage. Personal Attributes: Passionate about lifelong learning and empowering working professionals through a diverse range of educational opportunities. Highly motivated and results-oriented with a proactive approach to building partnerships and driving enrollment within the CET sector for a portfolio of programs. Strong understanding of the needs and motivations of adult learners across different fields. Excellent networking and stakeholder management skills. Strong ethical standards and a commitment to academic integrity and the principles of continuing education. Adaptable and resilient in a dynamic environment, with a willingness to engage with the evolving CET landscape across various program areas. A strong advocate for the institute and its diverse program offerings within both the IHL and CET communities.
