title: Project Analyst - Finance Tools Implementation
description: YOUR ROLE As part of our Finance Convergence project, we are implementing new finance tools to standardize and optimize processes across the Asia-Pacific region. To ensure the success of this initiative, we are seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Project Analyst to lead the management of prerequisites for these tools in collaboration with local teams. WHAT ARE YOU GOING TO DO? Managing the prerequisites for the deployment of finance tools across the Asia-Pacific region. Coordinating with local teams to define and validate user roles, workflow approvers, work methodologies, and process volumes. Ensuring that all required data and configurations are prepared and ready for seamless integration in the finance tools. Identifying potential bottlenecks or risks in the preparation process and proactively implementing solutions. Collaborating with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment between local and regional requirements. Providing regular updates and progress reports to project stakeholders, ensuring adherence to timelines and objectives. Supporting the local teams with ad hoc analysis, training, and troubleshooting related to the finance tools implementation. WHAT ARE WE LOOKING FOR? This role is for a contractual period of 15-months At least a Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field. Demonstrated ability to adapt to new systems, tools, and processes quickly. Proven ability to manage multiple tasks and meet tight deadlines in a structured and organized manner Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including data analysis, pivot tables, and formula creation. Strong analytical skills with the ability to assess and organize complex data sets. Familiarity with financial processes and reporting requirements. Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to engage and collaborate with stakeholders at all levels. Highly detail-oriented with a strong focus on accuracy and quality. Adaptable and comfortable working in a dynamic, fast-paced environment. Challenge-driven and proactive in identifying and resolving issues. Strong time management skills and adherence to deadlines. Effective communication and collaboration skills to work with diverse teams across multiple countries. Self-motivated and capable of managing tasks independently while maintaining accountability. Experience in finance tools implementation or a similar role is highly desirable. Solid understanding of finance tools and workflows, with experience in finance tools implementation being a plus.

title: Manager, Strategy and Market Intelligence
description: Role Summary Certis Group is redefining the future of security and operations through innovation, technology, and strategic foresight. With a mission to transform the way safety and operational excellence are delivered, Certis is accelerating its evolution into a technology-driven market leader with global ambitions. The Strategy and Market Intelligence team – a small (~4 pax) and high performing team - plays a pivotal role in this transformation. Its mission is to is to move from insight to foresight, continuously scanning the horizon for emerging trends, anticipating disruption, and ensuring Certis is positioned to act decisively and ahead of the curve. The Senior Manager of the Strategy & Market Intelligence team will be a strong individual contributor, leading key projects that define and shape our corporate priorities. Responsibilities Maintain a structured, ongoing analysis of all major markets, adjacent opportunities, and competitors—providing Certis with a clear, data-driven perspective on market economics and strategic relevance. Establish an “always-on” market intelligence capability that proactively identifies emerging trends, risks, and opportunities, enabling Certis to act early and make informed strategic decisions. Act as a catalyst for growth by collaborating with business leaders to define where Certis should compete and how it can win, ensuring the strategy function is a trusted partner in shaping the company’s future direction. Define, articulate, and drive Certis’ long-term strategic direction, aligning business objectives with emerging market trends and disruptive technologies. Lead the development and execution of growth plans for Singapore and key international markets, with a particular focus on Southeast Asia. This includes pinpointing priority markets and adjacent sectors, quantifying opportunities, and evaluating potential acquisitions and strategic partnerships. Support the GCEO and Board in developing high-impact communications materials for senior stakeholder engagements Requirements 6-8 years of experience in Corporate Strategy, Management Consulting or Strategic Planning roles Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, or a related field. Proficient in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint. Excellent communication skills. Experience with driving projects independently. Exhibits structured thinking. Strong at stakeholder management. Organised, proactive, and a team player.

title: Asset & Wealth Managed Services (AWMS) - Risk & Compliance Solutions - Experienced Associate
description: As part of our recruiting process, interested candidates are to complete an application through our careers website at: https://www.pwc.com/sg/en/careers.html We believe that challenges are better solved together. That's why you'll join a diverse, global community of solvers - an unexpected mix of people that come together to build trust in society and solve important problems. With us, you are encouraged to lead with your heart and values, and where your unique skills are developed and put to work in unexpected and exciting ways, superpowered by technology. We are Asia’s leading financial services practice and our firm is offering you an exceptional opportunity to work with both large scale and boutique reputable asset management clients in this sector. Our AWMS practice provides an invaluable safeguard in today’s complex operating environment with insights, empowered by leading technologies into marketplace developments and global opportunities. Our team is multi-disciplinary, made up of proven specialist professionals dedicated to this sector. Our AWMS team is expanding rapidly and we are looking for seasoned professionals to join us. A career within AWMS Risk & Compliance Solutions will provide you with the opportunity to help companies rethink their approach to risk and compliance and create a sustainable business advantage. We’re part of a unique client proposition that advises our clients on routine and complex risk and regulatory matters and offers bespoke outsourced support, from market entry through to execution, to help our clients better manage their ongoing compliance, risk and internal audit needs. You’ll work as part of a fast-growing team of subject matter experts with extensive industry experience in areas relating to regulatory compliance, risk management and internal controls. The team’s track record and credentials include several “industry-firsts” and numerous high profile engagements. Your clients will include leading local, regional and global asset managers, alternative asset managers (private equity, private credit, real estate, hedge funds and venture capital), traditional and digital wealth managers, digital assets and other capital market intermediaries. Digital alliances and digital solutions are core to the delivery of our services to clients. You will enjoy a stable yet flexible working environment with certainty in hours, and the ability to manage and control your own time. Specific responsibilities include but are not limited to: Providing regulatory advice to fund management clients on areas relating to risk, regulations and compliance. Conducting compliance reviews and internal audits for asset managers and formulating recommendations for improving processes and controls to better comply with industry/market practices, regulatory requirements and regulators’ expectations. Conducting business processes and controls reviews, including control assurance reporting to identifying gaps, assessing risks and providing recommendations for remediation. Providing outsourced compliance support to clients. This includes regulatory reporting, operationalising changes and updates to regulations, conducting compliance monitoring and reporting to senior management, assisting on AML/KYC reviews and providing regulatory advice. Helping clients develop, implement and strengthen their enterprise risk management and compliance frameworks. Managing key stakeholder relationships, including communication of the scope of work, findings and observations, industry updates. Perform walk-through interviews, identify key controls and tailor audit procedures to evaluate the design and validate operating effectiveness of internal controls in various business processes About you: Bachelor’s Degree in a related area Accounting / Commerce including students from a local/overseas university Min. 1-2 years of financial services regulatory, legal or internal audit experience in a Big 4 audit firm, regulator or financial institution. Previous employment with a regulator will be highly preferred. Good understanding and interest in the asset management or private wealth management landscape (preferable) Knowledge of local MAS regulatory requirements for capital markets intermediaries gained through experience in regulatory compliance, internal audit or processes, risk and controls experience. Regulatory compliance, internal audit or processes, risk and controls experience, preferably in a Big 4 audit firm, regulator or financial institution (preferable) Good track record of academic achievements Meticulous and attention to details, possessing an analytical and enquiring mind-set Professional, confident and highly motivated self-starter, with an ability to handle multiple tasks in a fast pace environment with tight deadlines, Positive attitude to learn and strong team player. Able to work under pressure and tight deadlines Possess strong values, personal integrity and emotional intelligence. Good verbal and written communication skill in English. Additional proficiency in Mandarin will be an advantage. We regret that only short-listed candidates will be notified.

title: Commercial Analyst (Finance) 3 months contract extendable
description:
The primary role of this position is to support the Finance team in consolidating, reporting, and analyzing financial actuals and forecasts for both P&L and balance sheets. Additionally, this role supports FP&A Manager by preparing insightful financial analyses and KPI performance tracking for review with the General Manager and Commercial teams. Financial Reporting & Analysis: Create summary reports and ensure timely submission of forecasts during quarterly cycles. Coordinate and consolidate daily/weekly sales reports, tracking and monitoring sales and margin performance. Trend Analysis & Stakeholder Engagement: Provide analysis and commentary on trends, proactively addressing variances with business stakeholders. Collaborate with the team to establish clear timelines, responsibilities, and review processes for the region. Develop tools and templates to enhance understanding of performance metrics. Financial Close & Reporting: Support the monthly financial close process, including preparing and submitting general ledger entries to ensure accurate financial reporting. Additional Responsibilities: Analyze financial data across countries to generate business insights and identify cost-saving opportunities. Assist in preparing presentation materials for monthly business reviews. Drive standardization and streamlining of planning and forecasting processes. Participate in commercial demand review meetings, leveraging sales and inventory data to provide business insights. Contribute to ad hoc projects, internal audits, and business analyses as required by divisional and regional leadership. Job Requirements: Degree in Finance/Accounting or equivalent. Minimum of 5 years of experience in Financial Planning & Analysis or Audit. Strong knowledge of accounting principles, financial modelling, budgeting, and forecasting. Good understanding of the demand trend and analyst reason for variations Experience with performance/variance analysis, forecasting and budgeting including templates creation & consolidation of budget data from various sources at speed. Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, with the ability to analyze large datasets. Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and critical thinking skills. A proactive, innovative, and collaborative attitude with a focus on quality and efficiency. Attention to detail and adaptability in a fast-paced environment. Familiarity with data reporting systems such as Power BI Facilitate  adoption of BI tools, especially Power BI. This includes developing dashboards and Power Query models to improve efficiency in data extraction and visualization Must be organized, analytical, self-motivated and able to work independently in a dynamic environment Proactive  and able to coordinate across different functions and region Understanding of the workings of strategic operational, business planning Sound analytical abilities and great attention to detail Effective communication and problem-solving skills Location : Town Salary : S$5,000.00 - $6,500.00 Interested applicants, please submit your application to Teresa.chan@persolapac.com We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. title: Senior Project Manager
description: Hydroinformatics Institute Pte. Ltd. ("H2i") are looking to strengthen its leadership team with a Senior Project Manager. This is a pivotal role for a professional capable of not only executing complex consultancy and technology development projects in water resources and environmental management but also playing a strategic role in shaping our Consulting business. You will act as a primary interface with our customers, fostering strong partnerships while holding them accountable to project prerequisites and timelines. You will provide leadership to cross-functional teams and contribute directly to the company’s strategic initiatives for growth and market leadership. Key Responsibilities ·      Strategic Leadership & Business Shaping o   Collaborate with the COO to refine delivery methodologies, improve Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), and drive operational excellence within the division. o   Provide mentorship and leadership to cross-functional teams, fostering a culture of accountability and high performance. o   Proactively work with management to develop new business opportunities and lead the preparation of high-quality proposals for new projects. ·      Client Management & Accountability o   Act as the trusted advisor to clients while rigorously managing client-side dependencies. You will hold customers accountable for their deliverables (data, approvals, resources) to ensure project momentum. o   Lead difficult conversations regarding scope creep and change control, ensuring that project objectives remain aligned with commercial agreements. ·      Project Execution & Financial Control o   Execute and manage consultancy and technology development projects related to real-time water resource management (e.g., forecasting systems, water quality modelling) from initiation to post-completion handoff. o   Develop detailed project plans outlining scope, milestones, and resource allocations. Proactively identify risks and implement mitigation measures. o   Financial Management: Manage project budgets (> $1 million SGD) and cash flows, including processing payment claims, assessing financial impacts of changes, and ensuring projects are delivered within budget. o   Reporting: Maintain accurate data in the project management system and report project status to senior management on a bi-weekly and ad-hoc basis. Minimum Qualifications ·   Minimum 8-10 years of experience in managing consultancy and technology development projects, with a proven track record of delivering projects on time and within budget. ·      Master of Science degree in relevant field. ·      Must be legally eligible to work in Singapore. Singapore citizens and PRs preferred. Desirable Qualifications ·      Familiarity with working with government procurement procedures and executing projects in public sector domains. ·      Experience in managing projects with budgets exceeding $1million SGD. ·      Experience or affinity with real-time control applications and operational management systems (e.g. FEWS, MIKE Operations). ·     Experience or affinity in working with water modelling software packages (e.g. WFLOW, Sobek, Mike11, Delft 3D). ·      Experience or affinity in working with management/collaboration tools such as Confluence, Slack, Zoho and Gitlab. ·      PMP certification or equivalent. Skills and Competencies ·      Ability to navigate complex regulatory environments. ·      Excellent communication skills for stakeholder management and reporting progress updates within and outside of own domain. ·      Analytical skills for problem solving and risk mitigation. ·     Strong leadership skills to manage cross functional teams effectively.

title: GRIT: Group Legal and Compliance, Transformation
description: Why Join Be at the heart of change within OCBC’s Group Legal & Compliance division. As a Transformation Programme Associate, you’ll help build a more efficient and forward-thinking legal and compliance function. You'll gain hands-on experience with cutting-edge technologies and contribute directly to impactful initiatives, shaping the future of risk management at Singapore’s longest-established bank. Success lies in your ability to collaborate effectively with diverse stakeholders, delivering on projects with meticulous attention to detail. A curious mindset and a willingness to experiment will be key in driving innovation. Project Support: Track action items, maintain project documentation, and coordinate with cross-functional teams. Tech Exploration: Build prototypes using tools like Power Automate, Power Apps, and Gen AI to demonstrate potential solutions. In an evolving world where we’re actively driving digital transformation, it’s no easy task. But we thrive on the challenge because we’re future-ready and focused on driving change. Who you are A recent graduate from a Polytechnic or University with a diploma or degree in the relevant field. Possess a Business, Information Systems, or a related discipline. Demonstrate a proactive approach and a strong work ethic. Exhibit a keen interest in emerging technologies and process improvement. Showcase strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Possess excellent communication and interpersonal abilities. Learning Objectives This role will provide you with: Hands-on experience with process mapping, analysis, and improvement techniques. Practical skills in project management and execution. Exposure to cutting-edge technologies like Power Automate, Power Apps, and Gen AI. Opportunities to collaborate with stakeholders at all levels. A deeper understanding of legal and compliance operations within a leading financial institution. Who we are As Singapore’s longest established bank, we have been dedicated to enabling individuals and businesses to achieve their aspirations since 1932. How? By taking the time to truly understand people. From there, we provide support, services, solutions, and career paths that meet their individual needs and desires. Today, we’re on a journey of transformation. Leveraging technology and creativity to become a future-ready learning organisation. But for all that change, our strategic ambition is consistently clear and bold, which is to be Asia’s leading financial services partner for a sustainable future. We invite you to build the bank of the future. Innovate the way we deliver financial services. Work in friendly, supportive teams. Build lasting value in your community. Help people grow their assets, business, and investments. Take your learning as far as you can. Or simply enjoy a vibrant, future-ready career. Your Opportunity Starts Here. What we offer Competitive base salary. A suite of holistic, flexible benefits to suit every lifestyle. Community initiatives. Industry-leading learning and professional development opportunities. Equal opportunity. Fair employment. Selection based on ability and fit with our culture and values. Your wellbeing, growth and aspirations are every bit as cared for as the needs of our customers.

title: Syndicated Loans, Indonesia Market - Global Investment Banking
description: Who We Are: As Singapore’s longest established bank, we have been dedicated to enabling individuals and businesses to achieve their aspirations since 1932. How? By taking the time to truly understand people. From there, we provide support, services, solutions, and career paths that meet their individual needs and desires. Today, we’re on a journey of transformation. Leveraging technology and creativity to become a future-ready learning organisation. But for all that change, our strategic ambition is consistently clear and bold, which is to be Asia’s leading financial services partner for a sustainable future. We invite you to build the bank of the future. Innovate the way we deliver financial services. Work in friendly, supportive teams. Build lasting value in your community. Help people grow their assets, business, and investments. Take your learning as far as you can. Or simply enjoy a vibrant, future-ready career. Your Opportunity Starts Here. Why Join Play a key role in shaping the loan origination and syndication strategy for one of Asia's leading banks. As an Executive Director in our Global Investment Banking Division, you'll have the opportunity to work on complex, high-profile transactions, develop deep relationships with clients, and contribute to the growth of our loan business. How you succeed To excel in this role, you'll need to combine strong technical skills with commercial acumen and a deep understanding of the loan market. You'll work closely with our origination, syndication, and distribution teams to identify opportunities, structure transactions, and execute trades. Your ability to analyze complex financial data, think critically, and communicate effectively will be essential in driving success in this role. Position Overview: The candidate will be a pivotal member of the Loans Origination (Indonesia) team within Loan Capital Markets, with a primary focus on independently originating and executing syndicated loan transactions specifically within the Indonesian market. Deep expertise and a strong network in Indonesia’s syndication and capital markets are essential. What you do Proactively originate and structure syndicated loan deals tailored to the unique dynamics and regulatory environment of the Indonesian market. Utilize an extensive and well-established network within Indonesia’s syndication markets to identify and secure high-quality lending opportunities. Conduct in-depth analysis of Indonesian clients’ financing needs, encompassing transaction due diligence, debt capacity, and market liquidity, while staying abreast of local pricing trends and market developments. Provide strategic recommendations on financing solutions that align with Indonesia’s economic conditions, regulatory framework, and market practices. Prepare comprehensive financing proposals, credit memoranda, term sheets, cash flow models, information memoranda, and investor presentations, leveraging local market insights and collaborating closely with junior team members. Lead the end-to-end execution process for Indonesian deals, including syndication launch preparation, documentation review, investor engagement, and deal closing, ensuring compliance with local regulations and market standards. Collaborate effectively with internal teams and external partners to facilitate smooth transaction execution from mandate through closing, with a focus on the Indonesian market landscape. Partner with the distribution team to develop underwriting, pricing, liquidity, and syndication strategies that reflect the nuances of Indonesia’s capital markets. Mentor and guide junior team members, sharing deep knowledge of the Indonesian market to build team capability. Who you work with Global Investment Banking gives you the way into Capital Markets, Corporate Finance, and the Mezzanine Capital Unit. The team offer wide-ranging funding solutions, across key markets, ensuring local customers benefit from international expertise. In return, the chance to build deep experience, across the entire project lifecycle, exposure to multiple industries, and an international career. Who you are Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related discipline. Minimum of 15 years’ relevant experience in corporate banking, investment banking, and/or loan syndication, with a strong track record specifically in the Indonesian market. Demonstrated expertise in credit analysis, loan structuring, loan syndication, and capital markets within Indonesia. Strong analytical skills with meticulous attention to detail. Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build and maintain relationships in Indonesia’s financial sector. Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a fast-paced environment. Client-oriented mindset with a passion for delivering innovative financing solutions tailored to Indonesian clients. Proficiency in Bahasa Indonesia is highly desirable. What we offer: Competitive base salary. A suite of holistic, flexible benefits to suit every lifestyle. Community initiatives. Industry-leading learning and professional development opportunities. Your wellbeing, growth and aspirations are every bit as cared for as the needs of our customers.

title: Quantity Surveyor[Central/Exp in Coordinating procurement activities/ quotation requests, price comparisons and negotiations, purchase order processing]
description: Roles & Responsibilities Manage procurement activities across all projects, including sourcing quotations, conducting price evaluations and negotiations, issuing purchase orders, and handling post-award documentation. Support the development of site budgets for newly awarded projects to ensure alignment with allocated costs. Source market pricing and quotations across all trades to update and maintain an accurate pricing database, ensuring Purchase Order (RPO) requests and future budgets remain within acceptable cost parameters. Secure multiple quotations for comparison and review quotation exclusions to minimize risk of unforeseen additional costs. Coordinate and update trade award status reports in relation to the master programme, working closely with site teams. Lead price and contract term negotiations prior to award confirmation. Gather and update quotations to support pricing charts and cost data records. Compile and analyze statistical data and reports for completed projects. Monitor and verify site expenses and ensure proper documentation and justification for cost adjustments in relation to approved budgets. Draft general correspondence and maintain accurate tracking of all documentation and reference numbers. Foster teamwork by sharing information and collaborating with team members to meet departmental objectives and performance targets.

title: 12 Months Contract KYC Analyst- SOW
description: Job Scope: This position sits within the Private Wealth BCCO unit and is responsible for supporting the front office in ensuring timely and compliant onboarding of new clients. The successful candidate is expected to be well-versed in AML/CFT and CDD standards, and familiar with the documentation and regulatory requirements for Private Wealth account types. The role requires a meticulous eye for detail, strong communication skills, and the ability to manage multiple stakeholders and competing priorities. Review and assess onboarding documentation to ensure completeness, accuracy, and compliance with MAS regulations and internal policy standards. Identify and escalate any deficiencies or red flags in client KYC, source of wealth (SoW), and background information. Liaise with Relationship Managers (RMs) to clarify submissions and obtain supporting documentation where required. Provide guidance to RMs on KYC requirements and support them in meeting submission standards. Ensure proper risk classification based on client profile, SoW, and overall risk factors. Coordinate with Financial Crime Compliance (FCC) and other control functions where escalation or additional review is necessary. Monitor onboarding turnaround time and ensure cases are processed within SLA. Support internal projects and initiatives related to onboarding process enhancements, checklist updates, and policy changes. Ensure proper documentation and record-keeping to support audit readiness and regulatory inspections. Ability to work independently with minimal supervision and collaborate effectively with both front office and control functions. title: Events Specialist (Members Engagement), Member & Corporate Relations
description: The Singapore National Employers Federation (SNEF) is the national trade union of employers, representing more than 3,900 employers and the interests of all sectors of the economy. In the tripartite system, SNEF is the body representing employers and it aims to be the trusted partner of the National Trades Union Congress representing the labour movement, and the Ministry of Manpower representing the Government. SNEF’s vision is to help organisations achieve sustainable growth that is supported by responsible employment practices. Therefore, the federation aims to strengthen industrial and labour relations through advancing tripartism and responsible employment practices. Industrial peace will, in turn, help organisations achieve business sustainability. The Event Specialist, Members Engagement will play a pivotal role in engaging SNEF’s membership base and partners. This function is responsible for organising events, meetings and conferences, to attract new members, driving engagement with key decision-makers, partners and organisations. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced B2B environment, enjoys interacting with senior management, and demonstrates strong ownership of projects and budgets. This is a 2-year contract role with provision of extension yearly based on performance. Responsibilities: Plan, coordinate, and execute events end-to-end, including concept development, scheduling, budgeting, logistics, and post-event evaluation. Brainstorm and initiate event ideas to increase outreach to members, partners and organisations. Coordinate with internal teams, vendors, and external partners to ensure smooth event delivery. Track event outcomes and prepare post-event summaries and reports. Be open to working weekends on event days as required (off-in-lieu will be provided) Maintain and administer the CRM system to ensure accurate data entry, data integrity, and regular data cleansing. Manage and build strong relationships with members Take on projects and tasks as assigned by the reporting officer. Requirements: Relevant experience in planning meetings, incentives, conventions, conferences and/or special events. Experience in managing outside vendors, sourcing and negotiating contract services. Highly motivated, results-driven, and resilient with the ability to operate independently while collaborating effectively within a team structure. Ability to communicate effectively both verbally and in writing. Ability to work well under pressure and adapt to ever-changing scenarios Capability of problem solving – Anticipating, initiating and resolving issues Strong relationship-building and communication capabilities, with the confidence to engage and influence external stakeholders at senior levels.

title: Accounting & Tax Manager
description: Bestar is a full-service accounting and corporate services firm in Singapore. A Manager-level role at Bestar involves overseeing a portfolio of clients (ranging from SMEs to international subsidiaries) and leading a team of associates. Job Title: Accounting & Tax Manager Employment Type: Full-Time Job Purpose As an Accounting & Tax Manager at Bestar, you will be responsible for the high-level management of client portfolios, ensuring all financial reporting and taxation requirements are met with precision and in compliance with Singapore’s regulatory frameworks (SFRS and IRAS). You will act as a key advisor to clients and a mentor to junior staff. Key Responsibilities 1. Client Portfolio Management Oversee the preparation of full sets of accounts and management reports for a diverse range of clients. title: FINANCIAL ANALYST
description: APJ Juweller Pte Ltd, is a trusted shopping destination for Silver and Gold jewellery in Singapore. We are reputed organization inLittle India, serving Singaporeans for more than a decade. We do follow fair practice in recruiting staffs for our firm. We are young and growing company and hence, we are looking for people who are versatile in their work. Their main task is to ensure the high-quality stock is available in right-quantity in the shop, as per customer’s needs in right-time. Being in Jewellery field, they need to do small ornament repairs or quality check work on their own. Currently we are looking for FINANCIAL ANALYST  for our firm . He / She plays a crucial role in maintaining company’s Financial and Business Matters thereon. Role Overview We are looking for a high-caliber Financial Analyst who can operate in the complex, high-value environment of the gold and diamond jewelry business . This role requires a sharp financial mind, strong banking or investment-sector fundamentals , and the ability to rapidly learn industry-specific dynamics such as gold pricing, inventory valuation, and margin structures. This is a hands-on, accountability-driven role . You will be expected to move quickly from learning to execution and to protect profitability in a business where price volatility and working capital risk are constant. Core Responsibilities (Common Across All Units) Perform detailed financial, margin, and variance analysis across products, channels, and geographies. Analyze working capital , cash flows, and inventory funding structures. Build and maintain financial models for pricing, forecasting, and scenario analysis. Track exposure to gold price volatility , foreign exchange (for exports) and interest costs. Support budgeting, forecasting, MIS, and management reporting . Act as a financial control partner to business teams, ensuring data integrity and discipline. Continuously learn and adapt to jewelry-specific costing, valuation, and operational workflows. Unit-Specific Responsibilities Retail Jewelry Focus Analyze store-level profitability , same-store sales growth, and category-wise margins. Track making charges, discounts, schemes, and promotional impact on margins. Monitor inventory aging, fast vs slow movers , and capital lock-in. Evaluate customer financing, gold exchange programs, and loyalty schemes . Manufacturing Unit Focus Analyze manufacturing costs , wastage, yield loss, and recovery rates. Monitor WIP inventory , production efficiency, and cost absorption. Partner with operations to improve bill-of-materials accuracy and process costing . Identify cost leakages and recommend process and control improvements . Banking / Investment Skill Integration (Critical Requirement) Apply banking-sector discipline to cash flow forecasting, credit utilization, and fund deployment. Analyze interest costs, loan structures, and inventory funding mechanisms . Support investment-style decision-making : ROI analysis, capital allocation,and risk-adjusted returns. Interface confidently with banks, lenders, and financial institutions . Leverage experience in equity research, credit analysis, treasury, or corporate banking to elevate financial insights. Required Qualifications MBA in Business Management , Business Administration preferred 4-6 years of experience in: Jewelry industry OR Banking, investments, corporate finance, or treasury with strong analytical      exposure Advanced Excel skills ; experience with ERPs or financial systems preferred Key Competencies Strong learning agility – ability to master industry-specific concepts  quickly Analytical mindset with a risk-aware approach Ability to work in a fast-moving, high-value environment Professional skepticism—comfortable questioning numbers and assumptions Strong adherence and Compliance to Singapore Statutory requirements. Performance Expectations Achieve working knowledge of gold, diamond, and jewelry costing within the first 60–90 days Deliver actionable financial insights that improve margins and cash flow Strengthen financial reporting quality and decision-making Who This Role Is Ideal For Analysts from banking, investment, or financial services seeking to move into a real-asset, operating business ** WE REGRET TO INFORM THAT ONLY SHORT-LISTED CANDIDATES WILL ONLY BE NOTIFIED ***

title: Director, Transactions Management
description: About the role You will deliver Portfolio Strategy and Transaction Management (TM) services to some of the world’s largest companies. This position calls for an energetic, detail-oriented professional with excellent project management, analytical, communication and collaboration skills as well as experience of managing real estate strategies and transactions across Asia Pacific. Comfortable in working towards achieving performance targets, you will be responsible to deliver a range of possible real estate and workplace solutions to clients looking for the next generation benefits that come from a vested partnership with JLL. Portfolio Strategy & Planning – Country, City and Metro plans, Global Initiatives Assessment of portfolio performance, portfolio rationalization and divestment, lease vs. ownership structures, cost reduction initiatives and bid support to achieve business change. Lead planning process to develop strategies to lower real estate costs and improve workplace effectiveness. Identify opportunities for optimization, cost saving and improved ROI in the Client portfolio Conduct fact-based analysis using occupancy, portfolio, real estate, and business data to align stakeholders on informed real estate plans. Through strong business relationships and clear, compelling business cases, challenge status quo thinking to drive unexpected portfolio optimization opportunities Develop overarching strategies related to workplace, organization, process, sustainability, operations and finance to govern the portfolio and set a foundation for city master plans Analyze inputs from the strategic planning process and on-account systems (business requirements, costs, facility information, cap-ex requirements, forecasts, occupancy data, lease and market data) Analyze and make recommendations with respect to strategic dispositions, investment and market opportunities, hold versus sell analysis, feasibility studies, asset review and market studies, highest and best use analysis, acquisition and advice analysis and valuations for sale/leaseback purposes Develop multiple practical options supported by strong business cases that optimize NPV and enhance productivity and innovation Be a SPOC for all Portfolio related client inquiries and a representative in global calls and initiatives Manage expectations, timelines and delivery against Client requirements, represent both JLL and the Client’s interest on the long term Transaction Management You will execute a wide variety of real estate transactions, including capital and lease acquisitions, sale and leasebacks and disposition transactions through local Markets teams across Asia, demonstrating a high level of proficiency and knowledge. You will actively engage with client’s senior stakeholders to define objectives and agree requirements at the outset of a strategy and transaction. Financial Acumen You will need to: Prepare and evaluate financial analysis documents. Support and contribute to the client’s budget forecasting. Forecast revenue and initiate and close-out projects to meet monthly cashflow plan. Monitor and closely control legal budgets and resolve billing queries. Relationship Management You will build strong and sustainable working relationships and help the client navigate through the JLL delivery platform in an efficient and productive manner. You will be the face of Portfolio Strategy and Transaction Management towards the Client both regionally and globally. People Management You possess strong leadership skills to manage brokers across geographies and actively engage across the client’s wider corporate real estate team and internal stakeholders in a collaborative manner. You demonstrate a high level of proficiency and knowledge in portfolio management and possesses the ability to orchestrate complex activities across multiple countries/properties/services at once to accomplish goals and objectives. Commercial Acumen You will understand account profitability, formulate an account plan to achieve revenue and performance targets whilst meeting client service standards. Armed with a sharp business acumen, you will leverage on technology and new products within JLL to deliver the next generation of benefits for our client. Operating a Global Account Can you see the bigger picture? Can you juggle regional delivery that aligns to a global strategy? As the TM lead for Asia on a Global Account, you are proactive in bringing Asia into the forefront, highlighting successes or potential issues whilst providing tangible solutions for the client. Can you communicate clearly and guide the client and your team on the best way forward for best in class processes and portfolio strategies at an account level? Sound like you? Here is what we’re looking for: Great Communicator Can you get your ideas across clearly both verbally and in writing? You’ll be working with many diverse interest groups so the way you share information and your ability to persuasively convey information is highly important. A high EQ is must for this role. You will have exceptional client management, communication and presentation skills and the ability to absorb and translate broad concepts and ideas into clear and logical communications. Ambitious You will be results-oriented with confidence to liaise effectively with all levels of management. Have commitment to collaboration and teamwork, and would thrive to exceed team goals. Resilient Transaction Management is a highly dynamic part of JLL’s business. It’s fast paced and you’ll need to be fast paced as well. You’ll need a positive mind set and a ‘can do’ attitude when multi-tasking driving change and delivering efficiencies. Naturally proactive, you’ll possess the innate ability to identify risks in delivery before it becomes an issue. At JLL, we have a continuous improvement mentality across our business and we’ll be looking for you to have the same. A flexible, resourceful and proactive individual who is able to produce results and guide the team to deliver the same. You will have the ability to work well under pressure and support the team without compromising quality. Experienced You will be an experienced real estate professional with a minimum of 10-12 years’ experience who has delivered positive results across the APAC region. You will be familiar with working with fast moving MNC clients with a regional presence and multiple service lines and possess prior experience in managing a team.

title: Sales Manager (1 Year Contract)
description: About Singapore Red Cross Academy (SRCA) The Singapore Red Cross Academy is the training arm of the Singapore Red Cross, dedicated to building community resilience by equipping individuals and organisations with essential humanitarian knowledge and skills. We are the region's go-to institute for humanitarian studies, offering a comprehensive suite of courses in First Aid & Resuscitation, Psychosocial Support, and other humanitarian and caregiving programmes. We strive to empower individuals and communities to help save lives. Job Purpose The job incumbent will be responsible for driving the sales performance of the Singapore Red Cross Academy's range of courses and training programmes to corporate clients, government agencies, educational institutions, and the general public. This role requires a dynamic and results-driven individual with a passion for humanitarian work and a proven track record in achieving ambitious sales targets. Remarks: Competitive basic pay plus commission/ incentive based on performance. Key Responsibilities 1. Sales Strategy and Execution: Develop and implement strategic sales plans to achieve and exceed course enrolment and revenue targets for SRCA. Identify new business opportunities and target markets for SRCA's training offerings, particularly within the corporate and institutional sectors. Conduct market research to stay abreast of industry trends, competitor activities, and pricing strategies. Prepare and deliver compelling sales presentations, proposals, and tender submissions to potential clients. 2. Client Relationship Management (CRM): Cultivate and maintain strong, long-term relationships with key corporate accounts and institutional partners. Act as the primary point of contact for large-scale training enquiries and customised corporate training solutions. Ensure high levels of client satisfaction and secure repeat business and referrals. 3. Sales Operations and Reporting: Manage the end-to-end sales cycle, from lead generation and qualification to negotiation and closing. Maintain accurate records of sales activities, pipelines, and forecasts using the CRM system. Prepare regular sales reports and dashboards for management, detailing performance against targets and key metrics. Work closely with the Operations and Marketing teams to ensure seamless course delivery and effective promotional campaigns. 4. Product Knowledge and Advocacy: Maintain a deep understanding of all SRCA courses, accreditations (e.g., SRFAC, MOM), and subsidy schemes (e.g., SkillsFuture). Act as an ambassador for the Singapore Red Cross humanitarian mission and the value of its training programmes. 5. Team Collaboration (if applicable to team structure): Mentor and provide guidance to junior sales or account management staff, fostering a high-performance sales culture. Collaborate with the Curriculum Development team to communicate market needs and client feedback for continuous course improvement. Job Requirements Qualifications and Experience: Minimum Diploma or Degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field. Minimum 3 years of relevant sales experience. Experience in the training and education sector is a plus. Proven track record of consistently meeting or exceeding sales quotas. Skills and Knowledge: Excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation skills. Strong business acumen and ability to understand client training needs. Proficiency in using CRM software and Microsoft Office Suite. Knowledge of Singapore's training landscape, including government grants and accreditation bodies, is highly advantageous. Demonstrates a high level of initiative, self-motivation, and integrity. Ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team.

title: Finance Director
description: X Star is a Singapore-based AI innovator transforming the automotive finance industry. Since our founding in 2023, we have expanded our operational footprint across Asia Pacific, Latin America, and the Middle East. We leverage proprietary artificial intelligence to provide consumers with cutting-edge financial services while delivering powerful technology solutions that enhance value for our partners. Our global network now includes over 2,000 car manufacturers, dealers, and financial institutions. Backed by a dedicated team of 200 professionals and a financing facility of $150 million as of H1 2025, X Star is accelerating its global expansion to bring intelligent, seamless mobility solutions to a worldwide consumer base. Headquartered in Singapore, X Star operates across key markets, including Japan and Mexico. Our Singapore business alone has facilitated over SGD 100 million in cumulative loans, demonstrating strong market traction and scalability. As a result of continuous growth of business, we are looking for an experienced Finance Director to join us and play a critical role in shaping our future success. Key Responsibilities Strategic Financial Planning Develop and implement financial strategies aligned with organizational goals. Provide financial insights to support long-term business planning. Financial Management Oversee budgeting, forecasting, and financial reporting processes. Monitor cash flow, investments, and capital structure. Ensure compliance with accounting standards and regulatory requirements. Leadership & Governance Lead and mentor the finance team to achieve departmental objectives. Present financial reports to the board and senior management. Establish internal controls and risk management frameworks. Manage relationships with banks, auditors, and external stakeholders. Optimize financial systems and processes for efficiency. Support mergers, acquisitions, and other corporate transactions. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field Professional certification (e.g., CPA, ACCA, CFA) preferred. Minimum of 10 years’ experience in senior financial management roles Strong knowledge of corporate finance, accounting principles, and regulatory frameworks. Skills & Competencies Leadership & Communication : Ability to inspire and manage teams, with excellent presentation skills. Analytical Thinking : Strong problem-solving and decision-making abilities. Strategic Vision : Capacity to align financial planning with organizational strategy. Technical Expertise : Proficiency in financial software, ERP systems, and advanced Excel. Integrity & Compliance : Commitment to ethical standards and regulatory adherence.

title: Trade Finance Executive
description: Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition Headquartered in Singapore, Agrocorp International is a leading integrated agricultural commodity and food solutions provider with presence in 16 countries and 25 offices. For over 30 years, Agrocorp has played a distinct role in nourishing the changing world with our agri-food supplies and solutions. To better serve our vision of nourishing a changing world, we’ve grown in size and business verticals from farm partnerships to processing plants and to retail market distribution. Our product range has widened to include a wide variety of commodities & food ingredients. In retail space, Sansar foods offers a wide range of premium pulses and HerbYvore is our plant based food range which includes non diary cheese, eggless egg, vegan ice cream etc. Job Summary The Trade Finance Executive is responsible for managing and processing trade finance transactions related to import and export operations. The role ensures compliance with international trade regulations, bank policies, and documentary credit rules while providing efficient support to clients and internal stakeholders. Responsibilities Manage end-to-end processing of trade finance documents including issuance, advising, amendment, and settlement of Letters of Credit (LCs) Execute Documentary Collections for import and export transactions, ensuring timely payment to suppliers Process Bank Guarantees and Standby Letters of Credit accurately and within required timelines Track and monitor LC issuances, confirmations, discounting, expiry dates, and negotiate related terms to mitigate risks Apply for loans under trade transactions and follow up to secure necessary approvals and disbursements Monitor trade transactions for due dates and discrepancies, proactively initiating follow-ups to resolve issues Provide internal guidance and support on trade finance transactions to resolve operational challenges and ensure smooth processing Coordinate effectively with banks, clients, shipping companies, and internal departments to facilitate seamless trade operations Maintain and update accurate records and Management Information System (MIS) reports for daily reporting to management Support back-office functions by performing timely data entry and assisting team members during absences Collaborate closely with Trade Finance team members and cross-functional teams to deliver unified support and achieve operational goals Skills & Competencies Demonstrate strong knowledge of trade finance products and apply ICC rules (UCP 600, URC 522, ISP98) to ensure compliance and accuracy Apply excellent attention to detail and analytical skills to identify and resolve discrepancies in trade documents Communicate clearly and coordinate efficiently with multiple stakeholders to drive transaction completion Manage multiple trade finance transactions under tight deadlines while maintaining accuracy and compliance Utilize MS Office and trade finance systems proficiently to support transaction processing and reporting Maintain a compliance-oriented mindset to uphold regulatory and bank policy standards

title: Sales Support (Learner Ambassador)
description: Job Overview: This role involves reaching out to learners who are at risk of withdrawing, becoming unresponsive, or rescheduling their courses. The primary objective is to encourage learners to stay committed to their courses, reinforcing their initial motivation for enrollment and ensuring successful course completion. Responsibilities Proactively reach out to learners who have withdrawn, become unresponsive, or rescheduled their courses to understand their concerns and re-engage them. Effectively communicate the benefits of course completion and provide motivation tailored to each learner’s goals. Utilize persuasive and empathetic communication strategies to encourage learners to stay enrolled. Maintain accurate records of learner interactions, concerns, and resolutions in the organization’s CRM system. Collaborate with the academic and administrative teams to address learner concerns and provide relevant support. Identify common reasons for learner withdrawal or disengagement and suggest improvements to enhance learner retention. Conduct follow-ups to ensure learners attend and complete their courses successfully. Provide excellent customer service and create a supportive learning environment for all students. Stay updated on course offerings, schedules, and learner support resources to provide accurate information. Requirements Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to engage, persuade, and empathize with learners. Customer service-oriented mindset with a proactive and positive attitude. Tech-savvy and adaptable to using CRM systems and other digital tools. Ability to multitask and manage multiple learner cases simultaneously. Strong problem-solving skills with a learner-first approach. Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a dynamic environment. Prior experience in customer service, student engagement, or education-related roles is a plus.

title: AVP, Real Estate Business Management & Operations (JR 16958)

title: Manager | Credit Risk
description: Join our Credit Risk division, which is responsible for analysing and assessing all credit and equity exposures taken by Macquarie to minimise the risk of loss arising from failure of counterparties to repay loans or honour contracts. Our division is also responsible for undertaking reviews of counterparties and transactions, and establishing appropriate limit frameworks. On principal transactions, we work with the deal team to understand risk versus reward profiles and develop risk mitigation strategies. At Macquarie, our advantage is bringing together diverse people and empowering them to shape all kinds of possibilities. We are a global financial services group operating in 31 markets and with 56 years of unbroken profitability. You’ll be part of a friendly and supportive team where everyone - no matter what role - contributes ideas and drives outcomes. What role will you play? You will conduct in-depth credit reviews and analyses of Commodities and Global Markets' counterparties in the Asia region, focusing on corporate clients. You will collaborate with global credit offices to secure credit approvals, partner with Macquarie's business groups to evaluate new products and transactions, and provide well-founded recommendations to credit approvers. Additionally, you will assist in structuring transactions, reviewing legal terms, and offering general advice and analysis. You will perform annual and new reviews of counterparties, stay updated on portfolio developments, and address ad-hoc credit requests. You will also maintain strong connections across the commodities market to stay informed of the latest news and developments. What you offer Over 5 years of regional Asia credit risk experience in a large financial institution and/or commodity trading house Physical commodities trading experience including energy products and trade finance Strong background in transactional credit risk analysis and approvals of OTC derivatives across commodities, including familiarity with negotiating ISDA and CSA documentation Proven experience in preparing counterparty credit reviews, credit recommendations and presenting to senior risk management Comfortable planning and re-prioritising workload in an unpredictable environment Confident and able to provide independent credit judgement and articulate this succinctly to the senior management team High degree of ownership, delivery and timeliness in a fast-paced, output focused environment Ability to build strong, open, dialogue-based relationships across a range of stakeholders. We love hearing from anyone inspired to build a better future with us, if you're excited about the role or working at Macquarie we encourage you to apply. What we offer At Macquarie, you’re empowered to shape a career that’s rewarding in all the ways that matter most to you. Macquarie employees can access a wide range of benefits which, depending on eligibility criteria, include: 1 wellbeing leave day and 5 additional paid Flexi-Leave days per year 20 weeks’ paid parental leave for primary caregivers along with 12 days of paid transition leave upon return to work and 6 weeks’ paid leave for secondary caregivers 2 days of paid volunteer leave per annum and donation matching Option to purchase additional annual leave Benefits and initiatives to support your physical, psychological and financial wellbeing including, comprehensive medical and life insurance cover and additional flexible wellbeing credits Access to a wide range of learning and development opportunities, including reimbursement for professional membership or subscription Hybrid and flexible working arrangements, dependent on role Reimbursement for work from home equipment About the Risk Management Group Our Risk Management Group works as an independent, and centralised function, responsible for independent and objective review and challenge, oversight, monitoring and reporting in relation to Macquarie’s material risks. We are a global team that aims to manage the risks of today and anticipate the risks of tomorrow. Our divisions include compliance, credit, financial crime risk, market risk, operational risk, aggregate risk and prudential, and central. Our commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion We are committed to fostering a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and welcome all identities, including race, ethnicity, cultural identity, nationality, gender (including gender identity or expression), age, sexual orientation, marital or partnership status, parental, caregiving or family status, neurodiversity, religion or belief, disability, or socio-economic background. We welcome further discussions on how you can feel included and belong at Macquarie as you progress through our recruitment process. Our aim is to provide reasonable adjustments to individuals who may need support during the recruitment process and through working arrangements. If you require additional assistance, please let us know in the application process.

title: Lead, Treasury Data & Analytics
description: JOB SUMMARY Business Lead – Delivery, IDL Analytics is responsible for end-to-end delivery and enhancement of intraday liquidity analytics solutions within the bank’s treasury and liquidity ecosystem. This role improves real-time visibility of liquidity positions and drives innovation in liquidity forecasting and analytics through data-driven tools and automation. The Treasury Data & Analytics SME role is a key role in the data squad servicing end to end Treasury functions. Key Responsibilities 1. Product & Delivery Ownership • Lead the design, development, and rollout of the bank’s Intraday Liquidity Analytics (IDL) analytics and related tools. • Translate business requirements from Treasury Markets, Liquidity team, and Treasury Services into technical and analytical solutions. • Oversee end-to-end project delivery, ensuring quality, scalability, and timely execution across multiple workstreams. • Partner with internal teams to integrate payment, settlement, and collateral data from RTGS, SWIFT, clearing systems, SAP LMS etc. 2. Analytics and Insights • Drive the creation of real-time dashboards and KPIs (e.g., peak usage, liquidity buffers, payment throughput). • Develop forecasting and scenario analytics to support stress testing, funding optimization, and Basel III reporting. • Collaborate with data scientists and quantitative teams to apply machine learning or predictive analytics for liquidity flow modelling. 3. Stakeholder Management • Act as the primary liaison between Treasury, CIB, WRB, Operations, Risk, and Technology teams. • Engage with senior management to communicate analytics insights, tool capabilities, and track project progress. • Manage vendor relationships and ensure alignment of external tools with IDL objectives. 4. Governance and Controls • Ensure all tools comply with regulatory standards and internal model governance frameworks. • Oversee data quality, lineage, and reconciliation across liquidity sources. 5. Business Methodology • To act as a business solution owner of the projects’ target state and support analysis included in relevant concept and methodology papers required for preparation of BRDs • To ensure that detailed requirements are documented in BRDs, and are duly signed off by relevant stakeholders • To ensure that the new solutions comply with internal procedures / external regulatory guidelines and project deliverables are properly understood by business stakeholders, project team, and end-users. 6. Team Leadership • Lead a cross-functional team of business analysts, and visualization specialists. • Promote an agile delivery culture, fostering collaboration, transparency, and continuous improvement. • Mentor team members in liquidity analytics, deliver best practices, and stakeholder engagement. Our Ideal Candidate •	The ideal candidate combines deep domain expertise in liquidity management with strong delivery management, data and analytics skills with strong exposure in AI. •	10+ years of experience in project or product delivery, ideally within a banking or fintech environment. • Technical and Analytical • o	Strong understanding of intraday liquidity concepts, Basel III monitoring tools, and treasury operations. o	Proficiency in data analytics tools (e.g., SAP LMS, Python, SQL, Tableau, Power BI, Qlik) and data platforms (e.g., Hadoop, Snowflake). o	Experience with financial messaging systems (SWIFT, TARGET2, Fedwire, CHAPS). o	Familiarity with core banking and treasury systems such as Murex, Calypso, Finastra, or proprietary platforms. o	Experience in BI applications and related change management • Delivery and Leadership o	Proven track record managing complex data and regulatory reporting projects. o	Strong understanding of Agile methodologies and DevOps frameworks. o	Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills at executive and regulatory levels. Preferred Qualifications • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field. • Professional certifications such as PMP, PRINCE2, CFA, or FRM. • Experience in liquidity risk analytics, intraday funding, or payment system modernization programs. • Exposure to AI/ML use cases in liquidity forecasting and ISO 20022 payment data transformation. Role Specific Technical Competencies • Change / Programme Management • Agile methodology / strategic planning • Data Analysis and story-telling • Stakeholder Management and Influence • Effective Communications • Knowledge of Organizational Design • Trading and Treasury products • Understanding of Bank systems architecture About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. It means your application has progressed to an important stage of our recruitment process. Visit our careers website www.sc.com/careers

title: Vice President, International Credit Division (LBO team) (Job ID: 10074594)
description: Overview of the Department The International Credit Division (ICD) function in APAC evaluates and sanctions transaction proposals submitted by the Business and monitors counterparty credit risk. APAC covers all Asia including Australia, New Zealand, Korea, China, Hong Kong and Taiwan. ICD Credit Managers engage with APAC Business colleagues to evaluate business opportunities and credit submissions. Throughout the process ICD supports and advises the Business on due diligence, structuring and documentation requirements to mitigate credit risk. Credit Managers also risk manage and are accountable for their allocated portfolio of names and contribute to portfolio reporting. ICD covers a diverse range of credit and market risk products and counterparties including financial institutions, corporate counterparties, sovereign entities and funds. ICD also contributes and plays a role in the setting of risk appetite and formulation of policy in conjunction with other internal stakeholders. Main Purpose of the Role To be responsible for analysing and writing recommendations for transaction proposals to private equity led leveraged buyout (LBO) obligors. Potential and existing deals tend to be complex and will require early engagement and close cooperation with the Business, including the Leverage and Acquisition Finance team. This engagement is to support and guide the Business in respect to risk appetite, due diligence, structuring and financial modelling. This is an important hire within ICD APAC and the role requires a high calibre individual who can take on a high level of responsibility with minimum supervision and has the capability to progress within the organisation. Ability to deliver within tight deadlines is critical. Key Responsibilities Providing solutions and adding value to the Business via discussions, due diligence, giving clear guidance on policy and risk appetite, and influencing transaction structures and facility terms & conditions to mitigate risk. Present transactions in a concise and clear manner to senior Business and ICD management both locally and overseas for their approval, evidencing deep knowledge of the client, transaction, and risks & mitigants. Provide clearly articulated views on whether a client and proposal is bankable and if not, where appropriate, providing alternatives and risk mitigating solutions. Attend internal deal screening committees and communicate credit views and challenges on proposals. Evaluation of credit applications, internal borrower ratings and assumptions used for cashflow forecasts for new or existing corporate clients in APAC. This includes reviewing transaction material and the analysis submitted by the Business, where appropriate challenging assumptions and conclusions. Proactively track and monitor allocated portfolio including, but not limited to Leveraged Finance accounts from a counterparty, sector and country perspective, and where issues are identified, elevating these to internal stakeholders. Work with the Business to formulate mitigating actions where material risk issues arise. Support new initiatives by the Business providing guidance from a risk perspective. Involvement where appropriate in the development of credit and policy guidelines and participation and support on assigned projects including contribution to change initiatives to drive efficiencies. Work collegiately with all stakeholders across divisions, building a network of trusted relationships. Active involvement in term sheet, loan documentation and ISDA/CSA review and discussions with Business stakeholders, influencing credit terms where appropriate, ensuring these are acceptable from a risk and commercial standpoint. Job Requirements At least 10 years of corporate credit analysis and structuring experience within the banking/Big 4/ advisory consultancy industry. Requires product experience in the analysis and terms of traditional corporate lending and Leveraged Finance, including LBO/MBO’s and structured transactions. Experience in risk analysis and formulating recommendations on credit proposals across a broad range of client types, including corporates and private equity across various sectors and countries in APAC Good understanding of different sector risk dynamics, drivers and fundamentals, with an ability to apply this in the analysis of counterparty success factors and business and financial risk. Strong track record of working closely with Business partners, building trust and adding value by providing clear and early advice on risk appetite, due diligence requirements and structuring, and finding solutions to risk issues. Track record of providing a contribution beyond credit risk management, including on credit portfolio reporting, deep dive analysis, contribution to policy, special projects, and ideas for the improvement of process, quality and frameworks. Evidencing a strong graduate or post graduate academic record. Logical writing skills. Personal & Other Requirements: Excellent written and verbal communication considering the audience and any cultural sensitivities. Communicates verbally in a confident and effective manner to stakeholders at any level. Deep analytical skills and the ability to think laterally to find solutions and mitigation to risk issues. Ability to see the big picture on proposals and identify key risks and due diligence required. Ability to make clear recommendations balancing all risk and commercial considerations. Strong problem solving and decision-making skills and sound judgement. Understands the external market and keeps up with market trends. Decisive individual but listens and weighs up all views before formulating an opinion. Shares and escalates risk issues proposing remediation plans. Strong team player focused on team results and success of the Business. Excellent interpersonal skills and builds trusted relationships. Resilient individual able to operate under pressure with urgency. High integrity and leads by example creating a constructive culture. We regret to inform that only shortlisted applicants will be notified. Job ID: 10074594
