title: International Growth Practice Manager (Pharma & Healthcare)
description: At PwC, we help clients build trust and reinvent so they can turn complexity into competitive advantage. We’re a tech-forward, people-empowered network with more than 370,000 people in 149 countries. Across audit and assurance, tax and legal, deals and consulting we help clients build, accelerate and sustain momentum. Find out more at www.pwc.com. Headquartered in Singapore, PwC’s International Growth Practice is a global team supporting organizations to address their internationalization ambitions, whilst achieving rebalanced and resilient growth strategies. We are a truly global team, with satellite offices in Europe and the United States, made up of individuals from over 15 nationalities that reflects our diverse and extensive cross-country and multi-industry experience. The practice caters to a wide array of problem statements ranging from international market expansion, global location assessment, international supply chain transformation, international talent strategy, intra-regional trade flow analysis and strategic point of view development. Our global team focuses on several sectors including healthcare and pharmaceuticals, industrial manufacturing, automotive , consumer goods, telecoms, media and technology, financial services as well as government and global institutions. For more information about PwC’s International Growth Practice, please visit our website: www.pwc.com/internationalgrowth About the role We are seeking experienced professionals with expertise in Pharmaceuticals and Healthcare to join PwC’s International Growth Practice. This role focuses on helping leading pharma and healthcare organizations expand globally, optimize their supply chains, and build resilient operating models in an increasingly complex international environment. You will work with senior executives of multinational corporations and governments to design strategies that enable sustainable growth across diverse markets. Responsibilities Develop expansion strategies for clients from the sector seeking international expansion or to realign their existing supply chain and production footprints to being more rebalanced and resilient. Typical engagements will focus on developing in depth analysis and insights for: Market expansion strategies (Where to play) – market, consumer and competitor assessments as well as product and services analysis Market entry scenarios (How to win) – Channel strategies, operating model design, partner evaluation and selection. Global and regional site selection for new HQs, R&D sites, manufacturing / production plants Global talent assessments, to assess the availability of talent for growth strategies Supplier portfolio realignment , identifying and assessing suppliers to meet the needs of realigned supply chains and manufacturing. Strategic points of view focused on international growth Provide points of view for publication on opportunities and challenges of global trade and internationalization Lead bespoke client discussions and workshops to senior executives of corporate organizations and governments. Engage in business development and client pursuit efforts-Develop global relationships and build a pipeline of inbound and outbound projects to sell and deliver Work closely with PwC’s global network of teams and connect relevant expertise in PwC to address clients’ unique business challenges Establish strong international network of external stakeholders including clients and industry associations and participate in regional and international conferences and client events About you 8 + years of relevant experience working in Pharma and healthcare sectors in either a management consulting setting or within industry in strategy or general management role. Growth markets experience (E.g. Southeast Asia, China, India, Middle East, Latin America, Africa and CEE) Strong experience in delivering on client engagement with multiple stakeholders and teams in different countries Experienced in managing data collection and analysis and resourceful with excellent research and analytical skills; can transform quantitative and qualitative data into insights relating to country, sector and consumer trends, competitive intelligence Possess credentials in business development and sales with ability to develop insightful presentations to support proposals, client discussions and business development pursuits Thrives in a fast-paced and dynamic work environment Possess strong communication and presentation skills Team player comfortable with working in a multicultural and diverse global team

title: Actuarial - Non-Life Insurance (Financial Services) Senior Consultant, Risk Consulting
description: We are the only professional services organisation who has a separate business dedicated exclusively to the financial services marketplace. Join Financial Services (FSO) and you will work with multi-disciplinary teams from around the world to deliver a global perspective. Aligned to key industry groups including asset management, banking and capital markets, insurance and private equity, we provide integrated advisory, assurance, tax, and transaction services. Our Insurance and Actuarial Advisory Services practice comprises two areas: Life/Health Insurance and Property/Casualty products and services. Join this team and you will provide clients with traditional actuarial services (e.g., reserving, pricing and mergers and acquisition support), as well as leading edge services such as financial reporting and measurement, financial risk management, modelling, hedging, reinsurance, securitization, economic capital and solvency. With so many offerings, you have the opportunity to develop your career through a broad scope of engagements, mentoring and formal learning. That's how we develp outstanding leaders who team to deliver on our promises to all of our stakeholders, and in so doing, play a critical role in byulding a better working world for our people, for our clients and for our communities. Sound interesting? Well this is just the beginning. Because whenever you join, however long you stay, the exceptional EY experience lasts a lifetime. The Opportunity As part of our non-life actuarial team, you’ll focus on high-impact opportunities to apply your knowledge and experience that will shape our services. You’ll build valuable relationships with clients and develop strong capabilities, through both formal training and working with senior mentors and talented colleagues.  You will be at the forefront of issues that are shaping the insurance industry in Asia and gain a wide exposure to a diverse range of actuarial projects. Your Key Responsibilities You will be assisting and leading the delivery of a wide range of client engagements, leading or working as part of a project team, relating to, Merger & Acquisitions (M&A) and due diligence; valuation, pricing and product development and regulatory changes. You will also be performing evaluation in areas related to financial risk, product distribution and capital issues. Skills and Attributes for Success Good understanding of economic or market issues and the ability to interpret their impact on clients Possess strong interpersonal and teaming skills Has strong analytical and creative skills Leverage technology to continually learn, improve service delivery and maintain our leading edge best practices To Qualify for the Role you must have Minimum 2 years of experience in the non-life actuarial team or an actuarial consulting firm. Candidates with more experiences will be considered for a more senior level Newly or Nearly qualified with either the Institute and Faculty of Actuaries (UK), the Society of Actuaries (US) or the Institute of Actuaries of Australia Good written and verbal communication skills Ideally, you’ll also have Depth of experience in at least one of the following areas: financial modeling financial and regulatory reporting reserving product pricing and distribution strategy ERM / Solvency II M&A due diligence What we look for Highly motivated individuals with excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to prioritize shifting workloads in a rapidly changing industry. An effective communicator, you’ll be a confident leader equipped with strong people management skills and a genuine passion to make things happen in a dynamic organization. What we offer Continuous learning: You’ll develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next. Success as defined by you: We’ll provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way. Transformative leadership: We’ll give you the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs. Diverse and inclusive culture: You’ll be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs If you can confidently demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible. The exceptional EY experience. It’s yours to build Apply now. EY | Shape the future with confidence EY exists to build a better working world, helping to create long-term value for clients, people and society and build trust in the capital markets. Enabled by data and technology, diverse EY teams in over 150 countries provide trust through assurance and help clients grow, transform and operate. Working across assurance, consulting, law, strategy, tax and transactions, EY teams ask better questions to find new answers for the complex issues facing our world today.

title: Finance Director
description: Shape strategy. Lead finance. Create meaningful impact. We are conducting a confidential executive search for an accomplished and forward-looking Finance Director. This is a rare opportunity for a senior finance professional to join a non-profit purpose-driven organisation with a strong track record of service excellence. The ideal candidate will bring experience in complex, multi-stakeholder settings and a passion for strategic financial leadership, operational excellence, and long-term sustainability. About the Role The Finance Director will provide strategic direction and oversight of the organisation’s financial functions for various entities, supporting a broad range of programmes and services for the community. This role ensures long-term financial sustainability, compliance with relevant regulations, and effective stewardship of resources. Responsibilities span financial planning, budgeting, reporting, funding, and audit activities, in close coordination with internal teams and external stakeholders including government organisations and statutory boards. Core Areas of Responsibility 1. Strategic Financial Leadership Develop and execute a financial roadmap aligned with the organisation’s mission and goals. Guide the executive/senior leadership and governance bodies on performance, planning, and financial risk. Perform scenario modelling and financial analysis to support strategic decisions. Offer expert financial input to key functional leaders and operations teams 2. Financial Planning and Budget Oversight Lead and coordinate the annual budgeting process across departments. Track and report on financial performance and recommending interventions as needed. Ensure delivery of timely and accurate financial reports throughout the fiscal year. 3. Revenue Optimisation and Funding Strategy Monitor financial indicators and streamline revenue processes for service units. Manage relationships and negotiations related to funding frameworks with external partners and Government organisations. Provide analysis and insight into funding model changes and long-term financial viability. Ensure compliance with grant requirements and manage billing and collection functions. 4. Governance, Compliance & Risk Uphold financial and statutory compliance in accordance with regulatory standards. Oversee external and internal audits and manage communications with auditors and authorities. Identify financial risks and implement proactive mitigation controls. 5. Procurement & Capital Resource Planning Supervise procurement strategy to achieve cost-effectiveness and control compliance. Lead planning and budgeting for infrastructure, equipment and technology investment. 6. People & Cross-Functional Leadership Build and mentor a capable finance team committed to continuous improvement. Collaborate across departments to ensure financial alignment with service delivery goals. Foster effective communication and integration across operational units. What We are Looking For 11 to 15 years of progressive senior finance and leadership experience. Current or prior expereince in a Community Healthcare or acute public Hospital environment involving multiple funding sources and regulatory oversight and compliance will be HIGHLY ADVANTAGEOUS. Demonstrated experience in managing Government ministry/statutory board subventions, including budgeting, utilization tracking, reporting, and compliance with funding requirements and audit standards. Solid experience in financial analysis, budgeting, grant management, and audit readiness. Strong leadership and communication skills with experience collaborating across functional areas. Comfortable navigating complex stakeholder environments. Proficient in both written and verbal communication. Preferred Background Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. CPA or equivalent professional certification preferred. Postgraduate qualifications such as an MBA or MHA will be an added advantage. The selected candidate can look forward to a competitive remuneration package consisting of basic salary, 13th month AWS and variable/performance bonuses. Interested and suitably qualified applicants, please apply here or email to:- jerry.loo@recruit-inc.com Reg No: R1550439 EA Licence No: 14C7334 We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Student Recruitment Manager (China Market)
description: Role Overview The Student Recruitment Manager (China Market) is responsible for leading and driving student recruitment strategies across Mainland China for SagePaths and its partner institutions. This role oversees market development, recruitment operations, and team performance to ensure sustainable growth in student enrolment for higher education pathways, undergraduate, and postgraduate programmes. You will design and execute market-specific recruitment strategies, manage a high-performing recruitment team, strengthen institutional partnerships, and position SagePaths as a trusted and leading international education brand in China. The role requires strong market insight, strategic thinking, leadership capability, and deep understanding of Chinese student recruitment ecosystems. Key Responsibilities 1. Recruitment Strategy & Market Development Develop and implement annual student recruitment strategies for the China market aligned with company growth targets. Identify new market opportunities, cities, schools, and channels to expand recruitment coverage. Monitor recruitment performance, forecast enrolment trends, and optimise conversion pipelines. 2. Team Leadership & Management Lead, coach, and manage the China recruitment team to achieve individual and team KPIs. Set clear targets, performance benchmarks, and professional development plans for team members. Foster a results-driven, ethical, and student-centred recruitment culture. 3. Brand Building & Marketing Collaboration Work closely with marketing and communications teams to localise campaigns, messaging, and promotional materials for the China market. Ensure consistency of brand positioning and messaging across online and offline platforms. Drive high-impact recruitment campaigns through digital marketing, exhibitions, school visits, webinars, and roadshows. 4. Partnerships & Stakeholder Engagement Build and maintain strong relationships with: Chinese high schools and international departments Education agents and sub-agents Overseas partner institutions Alumni, parents, and key opinion leaders (KOLs) Represent SagePaths at education fairs, partner meetings, and industry events. 5. Market Intelligence & Data Analysis Conduct regular competitor analysis and market research on student behaviour, pricing trends, and channel effectiveness. Analyse recruitment data to identify gaps, opportunities, and strategic adjustments. Provide management with market insights, forecasts, and performance reports. 6. Process Optimisation & Compliance Ensure recruitment processes comply with partner institution requirements and regulatory standards. Optimise CRM usage, admissions workflows, and data reporting systems. Uphold ethical recruitment practices and protect institutional reputation. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in any discipline; Master’s degree preferred. Minimum 6 years of relevant experience, with at least 3 years in international student recruitment, higher education management, or education consultancy. Proven track record in managing recruitment teams and achieving enrolment targets in the China market. Strong understanding of: China education landscape and student decision-making behaviour Study abroad pathways and university admissions processes Digital recruitment channels (XHS, WeChat, Douyin, Bilibili, etc.) Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Highly analytical, data-driven, and commercially aware. Strong presentation and negotiation skills with schools, partners, and parents. Able to work under pressure, manage multiple projects, and meet tight timelines. Fluent in Mandarin and English (spoken and written). Preferred Attributes Experience working with international universities or private education institutions. Strong network within China’s international education sector. Strategic mindset with hands-on execution ability. High level of professionalism, integrity, and accountability.

title: Operations Executive (24/7 Shift)
description: About Us AsiaNext is an institution-only, global exchange for digital assets. A first-of-its-kind, combining the innovation of crypto with the robust security of traditional finance, we offer a comprehensive platform for trading and tokenising a diverse range of securities and non-bankable assets. Our services include listing, trading, clearing, settlement, and custody. A joint venture between SBI Digital Asset Holdings Co., Ltd. (SBI DAH) and SIX Group AG (SIX), AsiaNext is built to meet the growing demand from institutional investors for a safe venue to trade digital assets. Where applicable, our business activities will be subject to the strict regulatory oversight of the Monetary Authority of Singapore, and we adhere to the highest standards of corporate governance and internal controls across all activities. Leveraging the extensive networks of SBI DAH and SDX between Asia and Europe, as well as their joint expertise, we have built AsiaNext since 2020. Our vision is to become a trusted, market-leading exchange whose members can safely trade a wide variety of digital assets, permitting greater institutional access and driving the new era of digital finance worldwide. Why Join Us? With SIX, the Swiss stock exchange, and Japan’s finance conglomerate SBI DAH as joint shareholders, AsiaNext has significant financial backing. The team at AsiaNext are seasoned professionals from the finance industry and are passionate about digital assets, innovation and excellence. We listen to one another; we learn together, and we collaborate with a diverse ecosystem. Join AsiaNext and be part of a dynamic, fast-growing team with opportunities for professional growth and development. You'll have the chance to work on cutting-edge projects that are shaping the future of finance About This Role: AsiaNext seeks to develop a fair, orderly and transparent exchange that facilitates members and participants to have equal access to market information and transparent trading rules. This role will be performed on shift work basis where the candidate will be primarily tasked to monitor the continuous smooth running of the platform processes, hourly settlement run, and work on the market surveillance role in monitoring, identifying and escalating instances of market abuse or misconduct and other manipulative practices that compromise the fair, orderly and transparent exchange operations of the trading platform. Responsibilities Act as the first line of client support on Member’s account or trade related queries. Monitor platform processes and ensure the system batch run is executed successfully for the hourly settlement run covering critical process such as MTM margin calculation and risk limit notification. Activate contingent procedure to mitigate any system or data feed outage that affect the system batch run and hourly margin computation. Monitor member’s cash balance and margin limit position and follow up with Members proactively to manage their margin call if it is triggered. Monitor member’s credit or concentration limit and follow up with Members to manage their risk limit exposure if it is breached. Escalation to Ops management on any major operation process, system run or data feed failure for coordinated and prompt resolution. Perform level 1 check on market surveillance alerts triggered and forward to Ops Management for level 2 review on alert closure or further escalation if the alert event affects the working of a fair, efficient and transparent market. Use Python, R, and SQL to extract data, perform simple analysis, and generate a basic report. Able to use MIS/monitoring tools such as PowerBi, Zabbix, Graffana etc. Prior experience of conducting user acceptance testing (either manual or automated) Qualifications And Experience Education Diploma in banking and finance, business management/Higher NITEC in business. Experience and Skill Prior experience working in a related role in the banking/securities services/fintech/payment industry will be desirable. Good knowledge of relevant regulatory and market conduct rules and other related regulations will be preferred. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills Proven analytical and eye for details Collaborative, self-discipline, and team focus Ability to pick up the learning with the training provided and work in a fast-paced environment Willing to work in different shifts to cover the 24x7 operations Rotate on fortnightly basis :- Time: 7.30am to 7.30pm (Day Shift) 7.30pm to 7.30am (Night Shift) Employment Benefits Competitive staff benefits - annual leave, life insurance, and medical insurance (includes dependents)

title: Senior/Lead Manager, PW Mark Administration Office
description: The Progressive Wage Mark (PW Mark) is an accreditation scheme under the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) that recognises eligible firms that pay progressive wages to lower-wage workers. The PW Mark Admin Office (PWMAO), housed within SBF’s Workforce Development Division (WDD), serves as the public-facing body for PW Mark administration, certification, renewal and outreach to businesses. This role leads the strategic development and review of policies impacting PW Mark (such as the PW Model), oversees programme planning and evaluation, and drives data-informed decision-making to enhance impact and operational effectiveness. The incumbent will also spearhead stakeholder engagement and cross-functional initiatives to strengthen SBF’s role in the PW Mark and the larger Human Capital ecosystem. The role is critical to repositioning PWMAO and its strategies from a regulatory outfit for compliance requirements into a strategic enabler for enterprise transformation, offering employers skills, capability pathways, and ecosystem support. This is a strong value proposition aligned with One SBF and One WDD’s enterprise and workforce transformation priorities to build progressive and inclusive practices across PWM sectors and roles. Job Responsibilities: 1. Policy Development & Capability Development Conceptualise and lead initiatives that integrate the Progressive Wage Model (PWM) into broader enterprise and workforce transformation strategies, beginning with the Retail and Food Services sectors. Regularly conduct research work and translate emerging workplace policies and ecosystem developments into actionable and business-centric programmes that build enterprise capabilities. Co-design and deliver sector-specific PWM and capability bundles with SBF Business Institute (SBI), sequencing interventions with diagnostics and bundling leadership, human capital, digital and sustainability tracks. Integrate Career Conversion Programmes (CCPs), such as CCP for SME Executives, into PWM adoption journeys to enable job redesign and mid-career entry, aligning employer onboarding and funding navigation across SBI and other key ecosystem initiatives, e.g. Enterprise and Workforce Transformation Package (EWTP) and the Jobs-Skills Integrator (JSIT) initiative. Establish impact metrics covering engaged and benefitted firms, capability uptake and wage or skills progression proxies, and use analytics dashboards such as Oracle NetSuite or Power BI to inform programme pivots. 2. Industry Development and Stakeholder Engagement Cultivate and deepen strategic partnerships with government agencies, industry bodies and ecosystem partners to advance PWM adoption. Support the Director in leading and overseeing the team’s industry development and Know-Your-Customer (KYC) efforts across all PWM sectors. Design and execute outreach strategies that drive awareness and recognition of PW Mark, especially for sectors hiring PWM Retail and Food Services job roles across retail, restaurants, catering, attractions and hotels. Embed One WDD orchestration by partnering EWTP, JSIT and Mid-Career Pathways Programme (MCPP) teams to progress firms from PW Mark awareness to capability uplift with SBI and CCPs, leveraging TACs as multipliers. Drive TAC-led outreach and ecosystem partnerships to scale PWM and capability programme adoption, leveraging TAC networks for sectoral transformation. Convene sector relevant roadmaps and practitioner playbooks, for example PWM and tech-enabled productivity, omnichannel and service excellence for the retail sector, in partnership with TACs, IHLs and solution partners. 3. Operations Excellence Identify systemic challenges and implement solutions that enhance operational performance and stakeholder experience. Handle the end-to-end process of PW Mark application, renewal, approval or rejection, appeal and assist with escalated cases where necessary. Oversee the planning and execution of outreach sessions to create awareness, understanding and recognition for PW Mark among businesses. Drive operational technology improvements including PWMAO CRM optimisation and an AI chatbot for enquiries to reduce low-value administrative work and free up officer bandwidth for industry development and capability building. Institutionalise audit-ready processes and LOA compliance for MOM-funded workstreams and standardise impact reporting and knowledge management across PWMAO and SBI. 4. Mentorship & Capability Building Mentor and develop junior officers, fostering a high-performance culture within the team. Lead cross-functional projects that deliver holistic business solutions and accelerate PWM and/or PW Mark adoption. Coach officers to deliver cross-functional PWM projects paired with EWTP, JSIT, MCPP and CCPs, ensuring sector-tailored capability pathways and sustained employer outcomes. Job Requirements: 6 to 8 years of experience in policy development, programme management, public sector strategy, systems-thinking and/or strategic planning. Excellent conceptual thinking skills, able to structure analysis for complex issues, using qualitative and quantitative data to distil insights and propose direction and actionable solutions. Familiar with data analytics tools and methodologies (e.g., Power BI, Python). Experience translating PWM policy into sectoral capability solutions, with facility in enterprise dashboards or CRM systems for impact tracking and decision-making. Good understanding of enterprise dynamics and industry-specific challenges across sectors hiring PWM workers (e.g., Retail, Food Services, Hospitality). Ability to design programmes that balance regulatory compliance with business needs. Familiarity with CCP frameworks including CCP for SME Executives and the ability to orchestrate CCP deployment alongside PWM adoption and SBI capability programmes. Strong relationship-building and stakeholder-influencing capabilities to deepen partnerships across public and private ecosystems and drive win-win alignment on strategic initiatives. Ability to orchestrate TAC engagement and ecosystem partnerships to scale programme uptake and accelerate industry-wide transformation. Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to craft compelling narratives and clearly present complex concepts to diverse audiences across B2B, B2G and G2B contexts. Proven track record in delivering data-driven outreach campaigns, articulating employer success stories, and using insights (e.g. A/B testing for EDMs and social content) to convert interest into meaningful engagement and capability uptake. Proven strategic and cross-functional leadership capability in leading teams and driving end-to-end initiatives from policy to programme to outcomes with strong governance, with professional certifications (e.g. PMP, Agile, Lean Six Sigma) as an added advantage.

title: Market Analytics Manager
description: We are seeking a skilled Market Analytics Manager, Semiconductor to lead market intelligence, addressable market insights, and strategic analytics across the semiconductor ecosystem , with coverage of the semiconductor market alongside products, applications, and technologies. This role will steward data integrity, build robust reporting and dashboard solutions, and partner with cross-regional business stakeholders (internal & external) to deliver insights that influence strategic business decisions across product, technology, and commercial teams. Semiconductor Market & Addressable Market Analysis Define and maintain comprehensive Total Addressable Market (TAM) models covering different geographical semiconductor markets , including materials, devices, applications and technologies. Analyze market size, growth rates, competitive dynamics, customer demand, and material-specific trends (e.g., wafer fabrication and packaging materials, specialty gases, photoresists, etc.) that drive the semiconductor materials industry. Develop and deliver data and reporting outputs through continuously gather inputs from cross-functional and cross-regional teams to define and refine data elements, enhancing market data relevancy and accuracy . Establish scalable processes for data curation, validation, maintenance, and quality assurance. Dashboards, Visualization & Analytics Build and maintain interactive dashboards (e.g., Tableau ) to visualize market trends, materials segmentation, forecasts, KPIs, and competitive landscapes for business leaders across different geographical region. Steer management toward data-driven decision-making by revamping dashboards, automating reporting, and generating analytical insights that influence strategic outcomes. Partner with data engineering and analytics teams to standardize BI tools and reporting workflows for both regional and global use. Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver data solutions that provide insights, projections, measurable metrics, and performance tracking — including usage trends for semiconductor materials and related product portfolios. Act as a trusted adviser on market data, trends, materials dynamics, and strategic insights to influence senior leadership decisions. Qualifications Master’s degree / PhD in Engineering, Materials Science, Economics, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related field. 10+ years’ experience in market analytics, strategic insights, or market research —within semiconductors or electronics high-tech sectors with global and APAC remit . Strong expertise in addressable market modeling, materials segmentation, forecasting, data visualization, and reporting across products and materials markets. Experience with semiconductor industry data sources (equipment, materials, fabrication, packaging) and research vendors covering Global & APAC markets . Proven ability to manage large, complex datasets and ensure high standards of data integrity . title: SENIOR RELATIONSHIP MANAGER
description: Job Mandate Relationship Managers are high-level professionals who manage an affluent customer’s wealth holistically. They will be directly responsible for interfacing and engaging with clients on a day-to-day basis with the objective to grow and protect the clients’ wealth for the enjoyment of their successors and their heirs. With consultative process, Relationship Managers gather information about the client’s needs & wants, then tailor a personalized strategy that uses a range of financial products and services. They customize investment strategies to address each client’s unique circumstances as well as specific preferences for income, liquidity, and risk. Where appropriate, they pursue cross-border investment opportunities to enhance returns and provide additional diversification. The Relationship Manager (RM) is expected to achieve the annual business targets on business development, relationship management and customer profile balances based on more complex investment requirements while ensuring accurate and timely completion of the entire sales and compliance process. The Relationship Manager is primarily responsible for achieving business targets through the following: 1. Acquisition of new high net worth customers 2. Generation of additional volume from existing customers across sales channels 3. Development and engagement of strong relationships with assigned customers 4. Delivery of excellent customer service through investment products and service offerings Trust Advisory Referrals DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES A. Business Development 1. Achieves business targets on acquiring, on-boarding and deepening of Mass affluent customers. 1.1. Identifies and promotes a robust pipeline of prospective customers. 1.2. Sets appointments proactively and meets with customers to establish their needs & requirements. 1.3. Pursues marketing and solicitation of investible funds from existing and new accounts. 1.4. Develops proposals in accordance with the requirements of each account, in line with the client’s objective and needs relative to yield safety, liquidity and diversification. 1.5. Submits proposals and ensures follow-ups in a timely manner including preparation of call reports to apprise management of the account status. 1.6. Conducts client presentations. 1.7. Networks with customers in order to create new business contacts. 2. Liaises proactively with branch partners for referrals and cross-sell opportunities. 3. Develops and deepens relationships with assigned customers. 4. Engages support of specialists to provide technical advice to customers when necessary. 5. Analyzes trends in the market as well as customer profiles to identify potential opportunities for the organization. B. Client Relationship Management 1. Monitors customers’ accounts and portfolios providing proactive, timely, relevant market updates and advice through regular calls and meetings, thus building long-term relationships. 2. Seeks to establish and understand customers’ real needs and expectations. 3. Executes sales and investment strategies/activities effectively. 4. Maximizes each client or branch interaction by building on customer cues to gain commitment to products, services, and solutions. 5. Builds and develops relationships with customers –e.g., accompanying them at social/lifestyle events. 6. Reviews accounts regularly to ensure the growth of the aggregate fund level.

title: Market Strategist & Presenter
description: So, who are we? Hello, we're IG Group. We are a publicly traded FTSE250 FinTech company who run mobile, web and desktop platforms that help our clients trade stocks & shares, leveraged products, Futures & Options and Crypto. We are ambitious. Over 340,000 people already use our platforms. We're global with offices in 18 countries and products in 16 regions. We're hungry to move faster, ship better product for our customers and grow our user base. We believe in high autonomy, and we want people who are looking to do things differently in order to create better experiences for our customers. We work in cross-functional teams and are laser focused on increasing the number of active clients we serve to drive sustainable growth. Your role in the team We are seeking a dynamic and insightful Market Strategist and Presenter to join our team. You’ll be the market face of IG Group in Singapore, delivering actionable insights, building thought leadership, and engaging with the media and the trading community. You’ll also help support our content strategy and represent IG in media and public forums – bringing expert-level credibility and personality to every screen, page, and platform you’re on. As a Market Strategist & Presenter at IG Group, you’ll be both the voice and face of the company—delivering sharp, actionable insights that resonate with traders. You’ll shape our market narrative and drive client engagement through timely analysis, compelling reports, media appearances, engaging seminars, and social content. This is a high-impact, high-visibility role for someone who thrives on real-time market moves and knows how to transform analysis into actionable insights. What You’ll Do Market Commentary : Deliver daily and weekly market commentary, providing actionable insights on macroeconomic trends and trading strategies. Content Creation : Write articles covering significant market events, including US earnings, BOJ meetings, and Singapore banks' earnings. Live Hosting : Host live shows and video content on social media channels to discuss market trends and events. Social Media Engagement : Provide regular content updates across Twitter, Telegram, and Instagram to grow the company’s social media presence. Media Representation : Act as the go-to spokesperson for market views in external media, including radio and TV interviews, to share insights and build brand credibility. Webinars and Live Streams : Host regular market webinars and live streams to drive trader engagement and education. Client/ Public Events : Support and participate in client events to enhance engagement and foster brand loyalty. Who We’re Looking For You’re a natural communicator who thrives in the market cycle and knows how to turn complex themes into compelling stories. You're equally comfortable writing macro commentary, appearing on Bloomberg or CNA, or jumping into a live market call on social. You understand the stakes, the speed, and the scale of what IG clients want – and you're ready to meet that moment, every time. What You’ll Need for This Role Education: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in finance, Economics, Business, or a related field. Professional certifications such as CFA, CMT, or similar are highly desirable. Experience : A minimum of 3 years of direct experience in a market analysis or related role within the financial industry, OR Certifications : Successful completion of the RES5 and CM-CMP examinations. Strong understanding of macroeconomic trends and financial markets. Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to distill complex information into clear, actionable insights. Proven ability to create engaging written and video content for diverse audiences. Comfortable with public speaking and media appearances, including radio, webinars, and TV interviews. Proficiency in leveraging social media platforms (Youtube, Telegram, Instagram) to build brand presence. Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple priorities effectively. How we work We try to take a thoughtful approach to our ways of working as a company. When it comes to how we deliver, there are 5 things we want everyone to do to drive high performance, better learning and career satisfaction: Lead and Inspire: Drives trust, alignment, and enthusiasm Think Big: Focus on the problems that most impact commercial outcomes Champion the client: Understand and prioritise client's needs Deliver at pace: Push for fast, sustainable growth; Raise the bar: Take ownership, be accountable and share feedback We believe that diversity is vital to success, it fuels creativity, drives innovation and sets us up for global success. We're committed to building teams with a variety of perspectives and skills to help us realise our vision and strategy, that's why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join us on this journey. Learn more about our D&I approach here . The Perks Your growth fuels our success! Thrive with tailored development programs, mentoring opportunities with leaders, and clear career progression. Expand your network through committees, sports and social clubs. Enjoy extra time off for volunteering and community work. Flexible working hours and work-from-home opportunities. Performance-related bonuses. Insurance and medical plans. Career-focused technical and leadership training in-class and online, incl. unlimited access to LinkedIn Learning platform. Contribution to gym memberships and more. A day off on your birthday. Two days’ volunteering leave per year. Join us for this exciting journey. Apply now!

title: Director, FCR Integrated, Intelligence & Investigations (i3), ASIA
description: Job Summary The employee is a team member of Group Financial Crime Risks Investigations (GFCRI). The role will: Oversee, review and advise on projects and investigations to identify and mitigate financial crime risk; the causes, control issues and remediation. Key responsibilities include, setting and continued evolution of investigative methodologies, training, coaching and mentoring of investigators, and the delivery of compliant, thorough and insightful products to a range of clients and stakeholders internal and external to the bank. Act as part of GFCRI’s extended leadership team to support the setting and delivery of GFCRI’s OKRs and strategy, and contribute more broadly to the delivery of the CFCR Function of the Future. The role is expected to also support and lead on activities outside of investigations, with a view to generating a more integrated GFCRI across its (and FCR’s) various component activities. Key Responsibilities Strategy •    As part of GFCRI, support setting and implementation of OKRs, strategy, direction and leadership for the Investigations team, consistent with the vision and strategy for FCR and in support of the Group’s strategic direction and growth aspirations. •    Leverage GFCRI tools, standards and resources to identify and mitigate financial crime risk across the Group, with a particular emphasis on previously unknown client risk Business •    Lead and manage investigators and their investigations and projects. Advising directing and reviewing their investigations. •    Experienced in financial crime investigations, open source research, transactions analysis and report writing. •    Have a good understanding of global money laundering with an expertise in Asia region. •    Mentor, coach and train junior investigators and reports. •    Represent the Bank and GFCRI in internal and external workshops, briefings and forums. •    Work with stakeholders to understand their requirements and deliver solutions. •    Produce reports and policy type documents for senior stakeholders. Risk Management •    Provide subject matter expertise on priority and emerging financial crime topics and issues, drawing on financial crime intelligence and law enforcement and industry networks, professional qualifications and other experience. •    Manage and develop further relationships with GFCRI clients and stakeholders to ensure effective communication regarding relevant significant and key investigations. •    Assess and mitigate risks arising from products / segments / geographies / customers / transactions. •    Identify intelligence for typology studies and risk mitigation plans. •    Identify and assess financial crime risk indicators through various internal / external sources and the media; map and assess those risks, quantify the potential impact and escalate where necessary. Advise relevant clients and stakeholders on outcomes of financial crime risks identification and assessment methodologies. •    Promote the use of data and apply data analytics to large data sets in order to identify new and emerging financial crime risks. •    Analyse significant financial crime risk events and the potential network (e.g. SARs, non-compliant transactions, production orders) to ensure that all connected parties, particularly cross-border are identified and reported at the appropriate level internally as well as externally across all relevant jurisdictions. •    Manage end-to-end financial crime client investigations initiated by external sources (regulators, agencies, authorities or other external organisation), and internal events as applicable. •    Escalate to senior management serious regulatory breaches (or where risk tolerances have been breached) promptly. •    Ensure remedial actions (e.g. Regulatory reporting, client exits, additions to watchlists) are recommended, approved by applicable Risk Framework and Process Owners and implemented quickly. •    Partner with legal counsel for advice on technical matters. •    Provide investigative expertise to support management of Significant Regulatory / Legal cases / Speak up events. •    Conduct root cause analysis on control / other failures to ensure lessons are learned across the bank. •    Provide intelligence to GFCRI clients on specific clients, client-types and emerging risk typologies, and engage in external fora where appropriate. Governance •    Support senior oversight of GFCRI. •    In the event of serious regulatory breaches, or where risk tolerances have been breached, ensure senior management and relevant regulators are informed and that actions are taken quickly to remediate and/or activities are ceased. •    Prepare and cascade lessons learned from investigations as applicable. •    Propose control improvements, enhancements and simplifications where appropriate. •    Produce high quality written reports of findings, including observations and recommendations, suitable for internal and external stakeholders, including regulators where applicable. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    As part of the extended leadership team, lead GFCRI to achieve the outcomes set out in the Bank’s Conduct Principles: [Fair Outcomes for Clients; Effective Operation of Financial Markets; Financial Crime Prevention; The Right Environment]. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key stakeholders •    Head, GFCRI •    Head, GFCI Investigations •    Head GFCRI Intelligence •    GFCRI Team Leads •    FCC Country and Cluster Heads •    Senior FCR Leadership •    Senior CFCR Leadership •    CFCR Advisory •    FCSO •    Risk Framework and Process Owners •    Segment Business Heads/Teams Other Responsibilities Embed Here for good and Group’s brand and values in GFCRI; Perform other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures. ability to apply this knowledge appropriately to diverse situations. • Data Gathering and Reporting •    Knowledge of and ability to utilize tools, techniques and processes for gathering and reporting data in a particular department or division of a company. • Big Data Management •    Knowledge of and experience with day-to-day management, operations and support of a big data application, systems and platforms. • CFCC Policies and Standards •    Knowledge of and experience with CFCC policy and standards • Cross-functional Collaboration •    Knowledge of collaborative techniques and approaches; ability to promote a culture of continuous improvement and working together across functions to solve business problems and meet business goals. • Business Acumen •    Knowledge, insight, and understanding of business concepts, tools, and processes that are needed for making sound decisions in the context of the company's business; ability to apply this knowledge appropriately to diverse situations. title: Senior Manager Business Development

Strong command of English and able to engage stakeholders at all levels, including customers and principals. Good interpersonal, communication and presentation skills. title: Assistant Professor / Associate Professor in Dynamical Systems and Mathematics of Machine Learning
description: School of Physical and Mathematical Sciences (SPMS) Nanyang Technological University, Singapore Assistant Professor / Associate Professor in Dynamical Systems and Mathematics of Machine Learning Young and research-intensive, Nanyang Technological University (NTU Singapore) is ranked among the world’s top universities. The College of Science seeks a diverse and inclusive workforce and is committed to equality of opportunity. We welcome applications from all and recruit on the basis of merit, regardless of age, race, gender, religion, marital status, family responsibilities, or disability. The School of Physical and Mathematical Sciences at NTU offers multidisciplinary academic programs that provide students with a wide-ranging and up-to-date education. They offer undergraduate training leading to BSc (Hons} and graduate training leading to MSc and PhD degrees. We invite applications for an Assistant Professor / Associate Professor position specialising in Dynamical Systems and Mathematics of Machine Learning . We are looking for world-class candidates with expertise in dynamical systems, numerical analysis, stochastic analysis, and with experience in applying these domains to recurrent neural networks, state-space systems, reservoir computing, learning of dynamic processes, and mathematical learning theory in general. The successful candidate will be expected to teach both undergraduate and postgraduate courses in pure and applied mathematics, conduct high-quality research in the areas of dynamical systems, random dynamical systems, state-space systems, and their applications to machine learning theory and time series forecasting, and promote interdisciplinary collaboration among the disciplines of mathematics, physics, computer science, and engineering that push the development of reliable dynamical machine learning methods for applications. Candidates with strong academic portfolios are encouraged to apply. The successful candidate should actively seek collaborations with research groups beyond his/her research field and leverage on the multidisciplinary ecosystem of NTU. The candidates are expected to: · Establish a world-class research programme. · Enhance the College’s visibility as an international leader in education and research. · Secure research funding from competitive grants and industry partnerships. · Mentor and supervise graduate students and postdoctoral researchers. · Collaborate with interdisciplinary teams within the university and with external partners. · Deliver engaging and effective teaching to both undergraduate and graduate students. · Undertake essential administrative responsibilities within the Schools. Requirements: · PhD in mathematics or a related subject. · Strong research record and relevant publications in reputable peer-reviewed international journals and conferences. · A good track record of supervising research staff, postdocs, students, and interns in relevant fields · Experienced in cross-disciplinary research initiatives and collaboration. · Demonstrated excellence in research, evidenced by a strong publication record in reputable journals · Recognition in the research community, such as invited talks, awards, or memberships in professional societies. · Proven ability to secure research funding. Application Procedure Required application documents: · Cover letter. · Curriculum Vitae, including a full publication list. · Statement of current and future research interest. · Teaching statement. · Names of at least three referees. Please click here to apply.

title: 25926828 Transaction Management Director
description:
Whether you’re at the start of your career or looking to discover your next adventure, your story begins here.  At Citi , you’ll have the opportunity to expand your skills and make a difference at one of the world’s most global banks.  We’re fully committed to supporting your growth and development from the start with extensive on-the-job training and exposure to senior leaders, as well as more traditional learning. You’ll also have the chance to give back and make a positive impact where we live and work through volunteerism. Shapeyour Career with Citi Transaction Management will be responsible for supporting multiple aspects of the lending lifecycle including but not limited to loan closing and servicing coordination, facilitating complex client onboarding, data management, and quality control.  The team will be accountable and responsible for operational oversight of the closing and servicing of all loan transactions of the business division being supported to ensure all internal
and external requirements are met. Responsibilities: Perform leadership role relevant to the build and development of the team supporting loan origination and lifecycle management which includes large complex syndicated and bilateral loans covering but not limited to corporate, commercial, real estate, structured lending and trade. Manage and contribute to the processes, standards, procedures, controls, training, and implementation of transaction management. Lead the design and implementation of technology including testing and rollout. Lead and produce strategy around training and support to transaction management staff and business constituents. Constantly improve processes, controls and efficiencies within the group and front to back across loan lifecycle. Lead projects to enhance performance and efficiency. Leadership role and serve as escalation relevant to loan remediation and loan system testing / migrations. Actively manage and maintain a deal closing pipeline of loan transactions. Coordinate and close loan transactions/lifecycle events, capture data, store documentation, troubleshoot issues/roadblocks, perform quality control Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency, as well as effectively supervise the activity of others and create accountability with those who fail to maintain these standards. Qualifications: 15+ years of experience, including 8-10 years of managerial experience Individual must have excellent communication, organizational and interpersonal skills. title: AVP, Risk Manager – Wealth Management
description: Position Overview - The role is focused on establishing the Conduct & Control Department as a proactive business partner and a key contributor to the Bank’s business strategy. - Acts as the first line of defense to ensure risk-based, sustainable, and efficient strategies are implemented to address Operational, Business, and Conduct risks in the APAC region. - Works closely with the Wealth Management (WM) business in APAC to manage and mitigate risks. Primary Role Responsibilities - The candidate will support the Head of Governance, Advisory & Surveillance, WM APAC Conduct and Control, in maintaining a robust risk framework for Operational and Business risks in collaboration with WM in APAC (Hong Kong and Singapore). - The position is based in Singapore and operates within the first line of defense (1 LoD). Key Responsibilities 1. Incident Investigation and Analysis - Ensure timely lodging and escalation of incidents. - Lead and coordinate in-depth investigations of operational risk incidents across Wealth Management in Singapore and Hong Kong. - Conduct structured fact-finding and evidence analysis to determine root causes and contributing factors. - Assess downstream and upstream impacts, ensuring proposed actions address immediate, past, and future issues. - Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of remediation actions and preventive measures. - Perform trend analysis to identify opportunities to strengthen the control framework. 2. Escalation and Governance - Ensure timely escalation of incidents to Senior Management in accordance with defined thresholds and governance requirements. - Prepare clear and concise incident summaries, analyses, and reporting materials for management and governance committees (e.g., ICC, ORCC). 3. Stakeholder Coordination - Serve as a key coordination point between Front Office, Operations, Compliance, Legal, Finance, IT, and other stakeholders for incident matters. - Liaise with Head Office and regional counterparts to ensure consistency in incident handling and alignment with Group expectations. - Facilitate effective ownership and accountability for incident remediation. 4. Reporting and Continuous Improvement - Produce incident reports (e.g., quarterly reporting) and trend analyses for management. - Contribute to the review and enhancement of incident-related procedures, escalation matrices, and governance frameworks. - Support internal audits, inspections, and ad-hoc risk reviews related to operational incidents. - Assist in various risk reporting requirements for different forums and to Head Office. 5. Risk Assessment and Reporting - Participate in the review and collation of annual Enterprise Risk Assessments, such as Risk Cartography Self-Assessment and Risk Identification. 6. Ad Hoc Projects - Undertake ad hoc projects as assigned by the Head of Conduct and Control – Governance, Advisory and Surveillance. Requirements - Strong understanding of risk control frameworks and operational risk management. - Excellent stakeholder management and collaboration skills. - Structured, detail-oriented, and methodical approach to work. - Strong analytical skills with the ability to assess and articulate risk impacts. - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present investigation findings clearly and concisely to governance forums. - At least 5 to 8 years (or more) of relevant experience in Operational Risk, Incident Management, Internal Control, Audit, or related risk functions, preferably within private banking or wealth management. - Proven experience in operational incident investigation, including fact-finding, timeline reconstruction, root cause analysis, and remediation assessment. - Sound understanding of Operational Risk Management frameworks, internal controls, and governance within the private banking sector. - Strong data analysis skills, with the ability to work on various dashboard reporting for Management and investigative work.

description: Job Summary The Head of Global Markets Operations Business Risk Management is a senior leadership role responsible for the design, execution, and oversight of the operational risk, control, and governance framework across Global Markets Operations. The role ensures that operational risks are identified, assessed, mitigated, and managed in line with the firm’s risk appetite, regulatory expectations, and business strategy. This role acts as a line-of-defense–style partner embedded within Operations, providing independent challenge, oversight, and assurance while enabling safe, scalable, and compliant operations across all Global Markets products and regions. What Success Looks Like •    Strong, transparent operational risk and control environment across Global Markets Operations. •    Reduced severity and recurrence of operational incidents. •    Positive regulatory and audit outcomes. •    Effective partnership with Operations leadership enabling safe growth. •    High-performing, engaged global risk teams. RESPONSIBILITIES Business •    Own the Global Markets Operations Business Risk Management framework, aligned to enterprise operational risk standards. •    Identify, assess, and monitor operational risks across trading, post-trade, clearing, collateral, client, and regulatory operations. •    Ensure consistent execution of risk and control self-assessments (RCSAs), scenario analysis, and key risk indicators (KRIs). •    Provide independent challenge to Operations leadership on risk exposures, control effectiveness, and remediation plans. •    Act as a key Operations risk partner for regulatory exams, internal audits, and external reviews. •    Coordinate responses to regulatory findings and audit issues, ensuring sustainable remediation. •    Monitor regulatory developments impacting Global Markets Operations and assess operational risk implications. Processes •    Lead operational incident management, root cause analysis, and lessons-learned exercises. •    Oversee issue tracking, remediation, and closure in partnership with Operations and Technology. •    Provide risk oversight and challenge for operational change initiatives, including new products, new markets, and system changes. People & Talent •    Lead, develop, and retain a global regulatory operations organization. •    Build a strong leadership bench and succession pipeline. •    Foster a culture of accountability, control ownership, and regulatory excellence. •    Champion diversity, equity, and inclusion. Risk Management •    Oversee the design and effectiveness of preventative and detective controls across Global Markets Operations. •    Ensure timely identification, escalation, and remediation of control gaps, incidents, and near misses. •    Maintain strong governance, issue management, and management information frameworks. •    Support attestation processes and senior management certifications. •    Develop and maintain risk metrics, dashboards, and management information to support effective decision-making. •    Leverage data analytics to identify emerging risks, trends, and control weaknesses. •    Enhance transparency and consistency of risk reporting across regions and functions. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    Lead and develop a global Operations Business Risk Management team. •    Build strong risk talent pipelines and succession plans. •    Foster a culture of risk awareness, accountability, and continuous improvement. •    Champion diversity, equity, and inclusion. Our Ideal Candidate •	Education - Bachelor’s degree in finance, business, economics, or a related discipline required. •	Advanced degree (MBA or equivalent) preferred. •	15+ years of relevant experience in Risk Management Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Strong financial acumen and analytical capability. •    Expertise in performance measurement, management information, and data analytics. •    Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights. •    Experience supporting large-scale operational transformation •    Demonstrated ability to engage effectively with senior management and regulators. •    Strong judgment and ability to provide constructive, credible challenge. title: Business Analyst (Data Policy)
description: About the Role We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst (Data Policy) to support a Singapore Government Agency in developing, implementing, and maintaining robust data governance and data protection policies. This role involves policy analysis, development of data governance frameworks, regulatory compliance, and cross-agency stakeholder collaboration. You will help drive responsible data usage and ensure alignment with national standards, public sector regulations, and organisational objectives. Key Responsibilities 1. Data Policy & Standards Development Develop, review, and refine data governance policies, standards, frameworks, and guidelines. Translate Whole-of-Government (WOG) data policies into practical processes for internal adoption. Support policy areas including data access governance, data sharing, consent management, and third-party data management. 2. Governance Advisory & Compliance Provide advisory to internal teams on data protection legislation (e.g., PDPA) and public sector data governance requirements. Assess data governance implications for new programmes, digital initiatives, and data-sharing arrangements. Review agreements, contracts, and MOUs to ensure alignment with confidentiality and data protection obligations. 3. Stakeholder Engagement Partner with internal departments, government agencies, and external partners on data governance and policy matters. Serve as the primary liaison for data policy queries, submissions, and consultations involving WOG stakeholders. Facilitate discussions and align expectations with stakeholders across multiple functional areas. 4. Capability Building & Awareness Develop training materials and conduct engagement sessions to enhance data governance awareness across the organisation. Promote best practices in data management and support adoption of enterprise-wide data governance frameworks. 5. Operational Governance Support Manage, track, and report on data access, data-sharing requests, and related governance workflows. Support continuous improvement of data governance processes and compliance mechanisms. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Management, Engineering, Business Analytics, Law, or a related field. Experience Minimum 7 years of experience in one or more of the following areas: Data governance, dat protection, or data management Formulation of data policies, standards, or frameworks Information lifecycle management or records management Conducting risk assessments or business audits Reviewing data protection and confidentiality clauses in agreements Key Skills Strong analytical and critical-thinking abilities; able to synthesize complex information. High attention to detail with the ability to understand broader policy and organisational implications. Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain policy and technical governance concepts clearly. Strong stakeholder management, collaboration, and negotiation skills. Comfortable working in a dynamic and fast-paced environment. Programme or project management skills are an advantage. Preferred Skills Professional certifications in data governance, data protection, privacy, analytics, or related areas. Experience with Singapore Government IM8 policies. Experience in data or cybersecurity.

description: Luxoft has one of the world's leading Orchestrade practices. We are a top-tier Orchestrade Partner and a market leader in implementation, integration, upgrade, and migration. We provide end-to-end project services and have delivered Orchestrade projects across all major asset classes and sectors including investment banking, asset management, corporate treasury, commodities. The project is a Trading and Position platform Implementation for Commodity products. You will be part of a Top Initiative for this commodity client. This is a brilliant opportunity to work on several type of commodity products and learn a new trading platform. You will have the ability to showcase the work directly to the End users. The team is responsible for the integration of a trading application (Orchestrade) used for: • Pre-deal origination workflow and post execution monitoring and life-cycle management. • Dedicated workflow, configuration of the platform, supporting business • Integration with Operations, Finance • Reporting, Reconciliation • Accounting The application is Orchestrade: - Orchestrade is in .Net (C#): Natively connected to Excel - Database: Microsoft SQL - Delivered In-House or Hosted (Amazon Web Services) Also, you will join our Orchestrade Practice and become a member of our open minded, progressive and professional team. You will have a chance to grow your functional and soft skills and build a thorough expertise of the industry of our client. title: Examination Preparation Coordinator (Primary School)
description: Job Description – Examination Preparation Coordinators for English, Mathematics andScience Singapore International School Dili (SIS) Timor Leste Position Overview Singapore International School Dili (SIS) is an international primary school utilising the Singapore curriculum and located in the capital city of Timor-Leste. Having successfully completed our first year of operations, SIS has achieved its initial student enrolment goals and is now entering the next phase of growth and academic strengthening. As the school prepares its students for the International Primary School Leaving Examination (iPSLE) in Grade 6, SIS is establishing a dedicated Examination Preparation Department to systematically raise academic readiness and ensure students are well prepared for a range of examinations (not limited to iPSLE alone) that may be required by parents in the future depending on each child’s pathway and needs. We are therefore seeking experienced and enthusiastic educators from Singapore with a strong tuition and examination-focused background, who are deeply familiar with the Singapore curriculum and assessment standards, to join this pioneering department. This role is ideal for educators who have at least three years of tuition experience, are highly familiar with the Singapore syllabus, and are passionate about helping students achieve examination confidence and mastery. SIS is moving beyond conventional hiring based solely on formal teaching qualifications and is instead looking for educators with strong practical experience in preparing students for academic assessments. The Examination Preparation Department will work closely with the Academic Planning Team, classroom teachers, and the principal to realise the SIS educational philosophy: “Allowing your child to study at SIS will offer parents who are undecided today the luxury of choice to make the best decision for that child in the future.” Being part of this department involves: Designing structured exam-focused lesson plans aligned to the Singapore syllabus and focused on the three core principles of focus, content readiness and exam familiarisation. Working alongside subject teachers to strengthen students’ foundational understanding. Developing revision frameworks, practice systems, and assessment strategies. Establish and manage the school’s examination invigilation premises in full compliance with the guidelines and requirements set by the respective examination boards. Tracking student progress closely and providing targeted academic interventions. Preparing students not only for iPSLE, but for other examinations as required by parents’     future plans for their children. Cultural sensitivity, adaptability, patience, and creativity remain important, given the international context and diverse student backgrounds. Above all, we are looking for educators who are passionate about academic excellence and who believe in giving students strong foundations that keep future options open. DO NOTE THAT THIS IS A OVERSEAS ASSIGNMENT AND THE CANDIDATE IS REQUIRED TO RELOCATE TO TIMOR LESTE. Having said that, this is a rare opportunity to be part of a growing Singapore-standard school overseas, and to directly shape how students in Timor-Leste are prepared for international academic pathways. Reporting Accountability The Examination Preparation Coordinator reports directly to the principal of SIS Dili. Terms of Employment Fixed Term (number of days per year) Salary & Benefits An expatriate package which includes flight, accommodation and other allowances to be provided on top of a base salary. Qualifications and Experience Possess at least three years of teaching experience in a tuition center that delivers the Singapore primary school curriculum. Hold a minimum of a Diploma or Bachelor’s degree from a recognised institution. Teaching or curriculum development experience in English, Math and Science. Good interpersonal skills. Able to interact and work with diversity. Strong command of English. Below is the YouTube link to our school's interview by MediaCorp Channel 8: https://youtu.be/2kC4ivv7x8k?si=ygJbTayqiNeflzwc (The interview is in mandarin)

title: Senior Transaction Monitoring & Sanctions Specialist
description: Job Purpose You will be responsible for overseeing transaction monitoring activities, investigating suspicious transactions, and ensuring compliance with MAS regulations, AML/CFT requirements, and sanctions obligations. Exposure to transaction monitoring threshold or parameter tuning is an advantage. Strong analytical and investigative skills with excellent attention to detail. Good written communication skills, particularly in report writing. Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills.
