title: Motor Claims Executive
description: Singlife is a leading homegrown financial services company, offering consumers a better way to financial freedom. Through innovative, technology-enabled solutions and a wide range of products and services, Singlife provides consumers control over their financial wellbeing at every stage of their lives. In addition to a comprehensive suite of insurance plans, employee benefits, partnerships with financial adviser channels and bancassurance, Singlife offers investment and advisory solutions through its GROW with Singlife platform. It also offers the Singlife Account, a mobile-first insurance savings plan. Singlife is the exclusive insurance provider for the Ministry of Defence, Ministry of Home Affairs and Public Officers Group Insurance Scheme. Singlife is also an official signatory of the United Nations Principles for Sustainable Insurance and the United Nations-supported Principles for Responsible Investment, affirming its commitment to finding a better way to sustainability. The merger of Aviva Singapore and Singlife was announced in September 2020 and created one of the largest homegrown financial services companies in Singapore in a deal valued at S$3.2 billion. It was the largest insurance deal in Singapore at the time. Singlife was subsequently acquired by Sumitomo Life in March 2024, one of Japan’s leading life insurers, which valued Singlife at S$4.6 billion, making the transaction one of the largest insurance deals in Southeast Asia. We are looking for a Motor claims Executive to join the team. This role is responsible for assisting the Claims Manager in all GI claims operations which includes managing and reviewing reserves, risk frameworks, customer experience, cost management and audit across different lines of GI products. Emphasis is on resolving the most technically complex issues/activities within in the claims operation and providing day to day management of claims partners. The role also involves support, coaching and liaison to GI operations. The role holder is also required to participate in claim projects and in key internal activities such as monthly claim file reviews and audit. For all lines of GI business to assist the Claims Manager in areas including; technical processes, policies, standards, customer experience, expense framework, FNOL, settlement, subrogation, third party management and vendors Make the best use of finite technical resources to achieve the optimum balance between service & claims costs whilst maintaining technical excellence. Implement and manage with our claims partner defined processes and delivery performance levels against pre-determined benchmarks for costs, recovery performance, complaint levels, compliance, and leakage. In conjunction with our claims partners to continually improve the customer claims experience to ensure Singlife provides outstanding customer service through speedy claims settlement Audit, control, and performance manage third party vendors to ensure that process and customer experience standards are met. Responsibilities: Make the best use of finite technical resources to achieve the optimum balance between service & claims costs whilst maintaining technical excellence Implement and manage with our claims partner defined processes and delivery performance levels against pre-determined benchmarks for costs, recovery performance, complaint levels, compliance, and leakage. In conjunction with our claims partners to continually improve the customer claims experience to ensure Singlife provides outstanding customer service through speedy claims settlement Ensuring that all claims are managed within Claims SLAs and handled in accordance with PDPA guidelines Ensure compliance with all applicable laws and regulations relating to the above functional activities. Experience 1-5 years of relevant Motor Claims handling experience Education Diploma level or a recognized professional qualification CII or similar qualification (or significant progress towards) preferred Personal General Insurance and Commercial General Insurance Certification required (BCP, PGI and CGI certification)

title: Compliance Officer (Regulatory & Licensing)
description: 📖About Us 🔹We are a high-growth Web3 payment infrastructure company redefining how users and businesses interact with stablecoins and digital assets. 🔹Backed by licensed financial institutions, and trusted by leading Web3 partners, we provide crypto-fiat payment rails, card-issuing infrastructure, and CaaS (Cards-as-a-Service) solutions for exchanges, wallets, protocols, and global fintech innovators. 🔹Our team comes from top global financial institutions, tier-one tech companies, and leading fintechs across APAC and Europe — bringing deep expertise, a builder mindset, and a shared passion for shaping the future of compliant, scalable financial infrastructure. About the Role We are seeking a seasoned Compliance Officer (Regulatory & Licensing) to support the expansion of our payment services globally. You will be at the forefront of a global compliance team, conducting independent regulatory licensing research; diving into new areas, regions or jurisdictions; and collaborating with the internal team to develop solutions for obtaining and maintaining licenses in different jurisdictions. What You Will Do Research and analyze licensing requirements for DCS's expansion into international markets, such as Europe, Australia, Middle East, Japan and Latin America. Independently analyze the licensing process for new regions, ensuring compliance with local regulatory requirements. Assist in identifying expansion strategies, target opportunities and considering its impact to the business. Collaborate with legal, compliance, business and product teams to prepare documentations related to licensing in new jurisdictions. Work closely with external legal counsel, actively challenging and guiding them to develop the most effective and commercially viable regulatory arrangements that enable DCS’s business to go to market across different regions/jurisdictions. Manage regulatory submissions and respond to inquiries from regulatory agencies covering majority topics such as business proposals, compliance and AML/CFT matters, IT compliance, operational and outsourcing arrangements. Provide ongoing support for maintaining licenses in existing and new markets as regulations evolve. What You Bring Bachelor’s degree in Law, Banking, Finance, or related disciplines. Minimum 3 years+ of relevant experience in fintech, payments, regulatory compliance or licensing within the financial institution. Strong understanding of financial regulatory frameworks and licensing processes across multiple jurisdictions. Strong research skills with the ability to independently dive into new areas of licensing and regulatory requirements. Exceptional project management capabilities. Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with a focus on problem-solving. Detail-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment. Proficiency in English is a must

title: Compliance Officer
description: Job Summary This role is ideal for a professional with a proven track record in financial services and digital asset licensing who is passionate about applying that expertise to a dynamic, high-growth environment. You will act as a key partner to the CCO, assisting in the end-to-end management of our compliance frameworks and the scaling of our global licensing footprint. Responsibilities Lead the end-to-end process for regulatory license applications by developing strategy, preparing documentation, and overseeing implementation to ensure timely approvals. Prepare, submit, and manage all regulatory filings, correspondence, and meetings with financial authorities to maintain compliance across multiple jurisdictions. Track and manage licensing obligations and renewals for Group entities across regions including Australia, offshore locations, and other financial centers to ensure uninterrupted operations. Conduct research and analysis on licensing and regulatory strategies in global jurisdictions to support informed decision-making. Develop, implement, and maintain core compliance, AML/CFT, and risk management frameworks aligned with applicable jurisdictional regulations to uphold governance standards. Draft, review, and update compliance policies, procedures, and operational guidelines to ensure consistency and regulatory adherence across business units. Identify gaps in internal controls and propose actionable improvements to enhance the Group’s overall governance maturity. Monitor global regulatory developments and trends, translating new laws into actionable internal implementation plans to maintain compliance readiness. Oversee KYC due diligence, customer screening, and customer risk assessments to ensure adherence to internal AML/CFT policies and regulatory requirements. Manage AML-related legal services and ensure onboarding standards meet applicable regulatory requirements to mitigate compliance risks. Assess AML/CFT risks escalated by business lines and coordinate appropriate responses to safeguard the organization. Design and deliver compliance and AML/CFT training programs and awareness initiatives to build a strong compliance culture across the organization. Promote compliance awareness and foster a culture of integrity and accountability throughout the company. Preferred competencies and qualifications Minimum 5 years of relevant experience in regulatory compliance, AML/CFT, or governance within financial institutions, fintech, or digital asset platforms. Bachelor’s degree in Law, Finance, Business, or a related discipline. Proven experience with OTC trading, crypto exchanges, or payment platforms is highly valued. Demonstrated ability to work autonomously and create processes from the ground up in a high-growth environment (builder mentality). Proficiency in English (written and spoken) is required to facilitate effective coordination and collaboration across internal teams and stakeholders in the Greater China region.

Global Markets is part of our HSBC Corporate and Institutional Banking division, a markets-led, financing-focused business that provides investment and financial solutions. Our Markets business is one of the largest of its kind in the world. It combines sophisticated 24-hour global coverage with a detailed knowledge of local markets. Our services are offered both online and offline in 58 countries and territories. We specialise in foreign exchange, credits and rates, structured derivatives, equities and debt, equity and equity-linked capital markets. Experience in trading both bonds and derivatives markets is required. Bachelor's or equivalent University degree, or equivalent work-related experience. Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with very strong analytical skills. Ability to multi-task and to work well under pressure, strong problem-solving and decision-making skills, has attention to detail. Ability to understand and manage complex and diverse instruments. We are looking for a dynamic individual who thrives in fast-paced, multicultural, and innovation-driven environments. · Bachelor’s degree in Business, Innovation, Economics, or a related discipline. · At least 3 years of relevant experience in consultancy, project management, stakeholder coordination, cross-border business development, or projects operation. · Strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (written and verbal). · Strong interpersonal communication, project management, organizational, and stakeholder coordination skills. · Comfortable working across cultures and adapting to different business contexts. · Legally entitled to work in Singapore (Citizen, PR, or holder of a valid work pass). title: Technical Business Analyst for ServiceSG (Contract)
description: [2 years contract] ServiceSG aims to transform the service experience for citizens receiving public services. The Service Transformation and Planning team within ServiceSG leads the service transformation work for key citizen journeys. As a Data and Systems Business Analyst (Service Transformation & Planning), you will play a crucial role in overseeing and coordinating various aspects of a tech-ops data project within the business domain to support service delivery transformation. You will: • Data Governance & Data Sharing – Support cross-agency data sharing initiatives by defining clear data exchange processes, governance mechanisms, and technical standards – in compliance with government and privacy regulations. Drive the adoption, use, and alignment of data systems to enable data-ops-tech integration across government agencies and partner entities. Support the design and deployment of interoperable digital and data architectures that facilitate and align with the next bound of the public service vision — to know and serve citizens as one and with heart. • Shaping Service Experience through Insights & Requirements – Lead the engagement of agencies and stakeholders to capture strategic requirements and feedback that shape future digital and data service delivery systems. Design and conduct user research including surveys and focus group discussions to better understand user requirements. Translate operational needs and user behaviours into actionable system requirements and feature roadmaps. Ensure that day-to-day operational insights are elevated into long-term planning, enabling ServiceSG to anticipate demand and design future-ready solutions. • Programme Leadership & Governance – Oversee and steer multiple project workstreams to achieve service transformation outcomes. You will set direction, establish governance mechanisms, and monitor programme health across delivery milestones. Anticipate risks and craft mitigation strategies to ensure alignment with national digital and service strategies. Provide oversight of project reporting, executive briefings, and progress updates for senior leadership and decision-makers. • Cross-Agency Collaboration & Strategic Partnerships – Build and sustain strong partnerships with GovTech, public agencies, and cross-functional teams to co-create tech-enabled solutions that balance citizen experience, innovation, and operational efficiency. You will act as a strategic integrator, aligning diverse agency priorities and fostering whole-of-government coherence in service delivery. • Risk, Foresight & Continuous Improvement – Shape and deliver clear, concise, and compelling reports, dashboards, and presentations for senior management and policy-makers. Ensure that key project information and learnings are systematically captured, disseminated, and translated into strategic insights that inform broader WOG service transformation efforts. • Reporting and Documentation - You will prepare clear & concise reports, dashboards, and presentations for senior management, as well as ensure all key project information is accurately documented and easily accessible when needed. We are looking for motivated individuals who are self-starters in a fast-paced environment and enjoy large systems problem solving. You should be comfortable with the ambiguity in driving large system complex change, action-biased via experimentation and prototyping, and always seeking to bring in new ideas and best practices for the public service. Key Competencies Required • [ Working Effectively with Stakeholders ]: Able to work closely with senior management and other Public Service agencies to drive the service transformation initiative and achieve better coordination and seamless integration of technology and processes. Influence agencies to see beyond their individual agency’s missions, towards a collective purpose of seamless service delivery for the citizens. •[ Thinking Clearly and Making Sound Judgements ]: Strong analytical and critical thinking abilities. Able to work with ambiguity and loosely defined problem spaces and synthesize data points and ground realities to sense-make, propose and implement sound and timely solutions with stakeholders in mind. •[ Creative Thinking and Problem Solving ]: Contextualise and apply design methodologies and processes to uncover insights and solve specific service-related challenges. You would be required to demonstrate creativity in generating solutions to complex issues. •[ Technical and Data Acumen ]: Sound understanding of data systems, governance frameworks, and integration processes. •Prior experience in project management and projects involving using scrum and / or agile methodology / or technology projects will be useful. In addition, prior experience with data infrastructure or technology projects will be advantageous. Work Experience At least 5 years of work experience in at least two of the following areas in service delivery would be preferable: • Data Governance and Management • Service Design and Experience Management • Service Strategy, Policy and Planning • Service Operations and Management • Digital Transformation • Product Management • Project Management Personal Traits Excellent communication, presentation and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to bridge strategic thinking with practical implementation, and articulate complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences.

title: Global Financial Planning and Analytics Manager
description: The opportunity Hitachi Energy is seeking a Global Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) Manager for the Business Unit Transformers to join our innovative and forward-thinking team. In this role, you will drive financial excellence and strategic decision-making by providing insightful analysis, robust financial planning, and accurate forecasting. You will empower the organization with actionable financial insights, ensuring sustainable growth and profitability while collaborating with cross-functional teams to optimize financial performance. This is your opportunity to grow your leadership skills, contribute to the energy transition, and make a meaningful impact in a dynamic and fast-paced environment. How you will make an impact You will support the strategic direction of regions and GlobalProduct Groups by providing financial insights and conducting research on key industry trends. You will facilitate the preparation and analysis of financial reports for the global business unit organization, including business planning, budgeting, and forecasting. You will enhance business decision-making by crafting insightful profitability analysis for regions, factories, service units, customersegments, and product lines. You will collaborate with finance professionals and function leaders across regions and countries to analyze key financial performance indicators and trends. You will design and prepare advanced financial models to assess various "what if" scenarios and their potential impacts on the business unit. You will lead initiatives to continuously refine financial planning and analysis processes, improving efficiency and accuracy. You will standardize definitions and reporting metrics for productivity and efficiency, driving process improvements that enhance operational efficiency and profitability. You will lead financial audits and ensure compliance with internal control standards, assessing and applying automation to streamline processes across the business unit. Your background Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, ora related field. Minimum of 7 years of experience in financial management, with a proven track record in strategic planning, forecasting, and financial analysis. In-depth knowledge of IFRS, internal controls, audit processes, and risk management practices to ensure compliance and operational integrity. Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills to collaborate effectively across functions and locations. Advanced financial analysis, analytical and critical-thinking capabilities, with proven ability to synthesize and interpret complex financial data to inform strategic decision-making. Strong commitment to continuous improvement by identifying opportunities for process optimization and leveraging digital tools to automate and enhance financial workflows. Ability to inspire and guide teams, fostering a culture of growth and development. At Hitachi Energy, we believe in fostering a collaborative and inclusive work environment. We offer opportunities for growth and development, ensuring our team members can thrive and achieve their career goals. Join us and be part of a company that values innovation, excellence, and teamwork. We pride ourselves on offering a holistic range of competitive benefit programs to support your financial, physical and mental wellbeing and personal development. We want you to truly thrive with us – in work and out. For this role, depending on grade and experience, we offer the following employee benefits. Employer-sponsored medical plan (inc. dental care). Central Provident Fund (CPF). Group Term Life insurance, Group Personal Accident insurance, Group Business Travel insurance. Hospitalization Insurance. Leave programs (Annual leave, medical leave, Hospitalization leave, Exam leave etc.). *Benefits are subject to the respective plan rules. title: Business Analyst (Digital Operations)
description: About the Role: We are seeking a skilled and analytical Business Analyst to play a critical role in our digital transformation journey with regional responsibilities. As a key member of the digital operations team, you will be responsible for analyzing current business processes, identifying opportunities for digital transformation, and working closely with stakeholders to implement innovative solutions that optimize operational efficiency and enhance customer experience. Key Responsibilities: - Requirements Gathering: Work with stakeholders across various departments across the region to gather and document detailed business requirements for digital transformation initiatives. - Process Analysis: Analyze existing operational processes to identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and areas for improvement through digitalization. - Solution Design and Implementation: Collaborate with IT and operations teams to design and implement digital solutions that address business and operation’s needs, leveraging technologies such as automation, AI, and data analytics. - Data Analysis: Utilize data to provide insights on operational performance, identify trends, and recommend data-driven improvements to support digital transformation. - Stakeholder Communication: Serve as a liaison between business units, IT, Operations, and external vendors, ensuring clear communication and alignment throughout the transformation process. - Change Management: Support the adoption of new digital tools and processes by helping to develop training materials and conducting workshops for end-users. - Testing & Validation: Coordinate and participate in user acceptance testing (UAT) to ensure that digital solutions meet business and operation’s requirements and are ready for deployment. - Continuous Improvement: Monitor the effectiveness of implemented digital solutions and propose enhancements to drive ongoing operational efficiency. Qualifications: - 5+ years of experience as a Business Analyst, with a focus on operations or digital transformation. - Strong understanding of business process analysis and improvement methodologies. - Experience with digital tools and technologies, such as automation platforms and data analytics. - Proficiency in data analysis and visualization tools (e.g., Excel, Power BI). - Excellent problem-solving skills with a detail-oriented approach to identifying and addressing business and operation’s needs. - Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams across the region. - Experience with project management methodologies and tools (e.g., SDLC, Agile, Scrum). - Experience in the Financial/ Banking sector is a big plus

description: Role Overview The position required to assist Finance Manager in overseeing financial and costing operations. Key responsibilities include handling daily accounting tasks, preparing financial statements, reconciling accounts, supporting audits, and contributing to budgeting and forecasting processes. Job Scope: Perform closing activities, including journal entries and account reconciliations. Reconcile key accounts such as bank statements, accounts payable, and accounts receivable. Prepare financial statements, management reports, costing reports, and group consolidations. Analyze financial and cost data to identify trends and provide actionable insights. Work with non-finance teams to communicate financial performance and cost-saving measures. Provide guidance on financial and cost-related matters to improve profitability. title: Pan-Asia Equities Reporter - Singapore
description: Bloomberg News is one of the biggest financial and business news organizations in the world. We lead the way in breaking news and providing analysis on financial markets, from real-time coverage to in-depth features. We’re a truly global business with a truly diverse workforce. We pride ourselves on being open, inclusive and collaborative, and provide a work environment that inspires our employees to be their best. We offer competitive compensation, comprehensive healthcare coverage, and programs to support you and your family through different life stages, from parental leave to generous retirement savings plans. If this sounds like you: Apply if you think we're a good match. We'll get in touch to let you know what the next steps are, but in the meantime feel free to have a look at this: www.bloomberg.com/professional Learn more about our office and benefits: Singapore | www.bloomberg.com/professional www.bloomberg.com/singapore

description: Job Title: Investment & AI Strategy Advisor (Senior Associate / AVP, commensurate with experience) Reports To: CEO Location: Singapore Employment Type: Full-Time About Us SGMC Capital is a buy-side global wealth manager delivering discretionary portfolio management and investment advice to sophisticated investors worldwide. We prioritize clients’ objectives and act as a professional, performance-focused, and trusted partner. Our lean and agile team values intellectual rigor, independent thinking, and close collaboration. Headquartered in Singapore with a presence in Monaco, we are actively expanding our global footprint, including North America. Role Overview We are seeking a high-calibre professional to join our investment team in a role that sits at the intersection of investment advisory, advanced data analytics, and applied AI . This position will play a central role in shaping how the firm leverages data, machine learning, and AI technologies and couples it with a stringent fundamental analysis to develop investment strategy, portfolio construction, and market insight generation . The role is suitable for candidates ranging from Senior Associate to AVP , depending on experience and demonstrated capability. Embedded within our global markets function, you will work closely with senior leadership to develop both fundamental and AI-enabled investment intelligence , enhance portfolioanalytics, and translate complex, data-driven insights into clear, compelling perspectives for both internal and external stakeholders. This is not a sales role . It is an investment-facing role that forms a critical foundation for how we understand markets and communicate that understanding to clients, as well as how we source, structure, analyze, and operationalize data and AI in support of investment decisions and client communication . Key Responsibilities • Conduct in-depth research on global financial markets, sectors, and individual securities • Design, develop, and maintain data-driven and AI-enabled analytical frameworks to support investment research, portfolio strategy, and market insights • Apply AI, machine learning, and advanced analytics techniques to financial market data, macroeconomic indicators, and portfolio-level datasets • Support portfolio analysis, monitoring, performance attribution, and reporting • Develop investment memos, presentations, research notes, and client-facing materials • Analyze global macroeconomic and market developments and contribute to the firm’s investment framework • Translate analytical and market insights into clear, structured narratives for clients and internal audiences • Collaborate with investment, advisory, and operational teams to strengthen the firm’s global investment capability • Work closely with senior leadership to convert market understanding into actionable investment perspectives • Collaborate with investment, advisory, technology, and operations teams to strengthen the firm’s data and AI infrastructure for investing What We’re Looking For • Master’s Degree in Business or Data Analytics • Approximately 3–5+ years of relevant experience , depending on level • Experience with data pipelining, complex datasets and software tools including Databricks, Tableau and Snowflake • Exposure and understanding of Foundry platform is an added advantage • Strong interest in global financial markets and investment strategy • Solid analytical skills with comfort working with market data and portfolio analytics • Familiarity with Bloomberg; strong proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint • Ability to synthesize complex market information into clear, structured insights • Excellent written and verbal communication skills – As a number of our clients are European proficiency in these languages is a plus • Self-driven, intellectually curious, and comfortable operating in a lean, high-accountability environment • Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work independently while collaborating closely with senior stakeholders What We Offer • Direct exposure to senior leadership and investment decision-making • A steep learning curve with meaningful responsibility and visibility • Broad exposure to global markets and client engagement across regions • A flat, collaborative structure with genuine opportunities for advancement • The ability to grow into more senior investment or leadership roles as the firm expands • Competitive compensation, calibrated to experience, with performance-based incentives

What You Will Do: ​ Developing, managing and executing the category strategy managing policies, agreements, source catalogs, and special procurement programs. Recognizing the potential for cost decreases, inventory reductions and quality improvement, as well as responsibility for soundness of business proposals and risk assessment. Acting as the liaison between the organization and the supplier to communicate information explicitly, while considering production demands and suppliers' conditions. Documenting the suppliers' performance on time sensitiveness and production; maintains a strong relationship with suppliers. What You Have: Data Analytics: Knowledge of techniques and processes for inspecting, cleansing, transforming, and modeling data; ability to discover useful information, draw logical conclusions, and support decision-making. Collects required data through various data sources and documents them for evaluating trends and activities. Recognizes and assesses problems, opportunities, and methods for improving existing business performance. Utilizes designated data analysis software to analyze the data. Effective Communications: Understanding of effective communication concepts, tools and techniques; ability to effectively transmit, receive, and accurately interpret ideas, information, and needs through the application of appropriate communication behaviors. Delivers helpful feedback that focuses on behaviors without offending the recipient. Looks for and considers non-verbal cues from individuals and groups. Interpersonal Relationships: Knowledge of the techniques and the ability to work with a variety of individuals and groups in a constructive and collaborative manner. Explains impact of interactions with individuals and groups. Collaborates with departmental associates and management. Negotiating: Knowledge of successful negotiation concepts and techniques; ability to negotiate successfully across the organization and with external vendors and clients in a constructive and collaborative manner. Describes qualities of effective and ineffective negotiations. Accesses organizational policies and practices for negotiating. Utilizes techniques for establishing rapport and building trust. Contracts Management and Administration: Knowledge of policies, standards and processes of how to manage and administer contracts; ability to use proper processes and practices for managing and administering a variety of contractual agreements. Discusses organizational protocols, standards, and procedures for contracts management and administration. Identifies and interprets regular contracts administration reports. Purchasing Tasks and Activities: Knowledge of policies, regulations and processes of purchasing; ability to use practices and procedures for procurement of materials, components, equipment and services. Uses available procurement systems and tools. Ensures compliance with procurement regulations and contract specifications. Supplier Relationship Management: Knowledge of supplier relationship management concept and strategies; ability to address critical issues to meet supplier needs and maintain engaged, partnering relationship with suppliers . Explains the benefits of establishing and maintaining supplier relationships. Identifies the main objectives and functions of supplier relationship management. Strategic Sourcing: Knowledge of strategic sourcing methods, technologies and processes; ability to analyze, improve and re-evaluate organizational procurement process continuously. Explains key concepts and steps of strategic sourcing. Identifies major initiatives, key issues and benefits of strategic sourcing. Additional Info: ​ This position is offered on a one-year renewable contract , subject to business requirements and individual performance. This position requires working onsite five days a week. Caterpillar is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status; however, as a global company, Caterpillar offers many job opportunities outside of Singapore which can be found through our employment website at www.caterpillar.com/careers . International Assignment is not available. What You Will Get: Our goal at Caterpillar is for you to have a rewarding career. Our teams are critical to the success of our customers who build a better world. Here you earn more than just wage, because we value your performance, we offer a total rewards package that provides day one benefits along with the potential of a variable bonus. Additional benefits include paid annual leave, flexi leave, medical and insurance (prorated based upon hire date). Final Details: Please frequently check the email associated with your application, including the junk/spam folder, as this is the primary correspondence method. If you wish to know the status of your application – please use the candidate log-in on our career website as it will reflect any updates to your status. If you are interested in joining our team, please apply using an English version of your CV. We look forward to meeting you! About Caterpillar Caterpillar Inc. is the world’s leading manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, off-highway diesel and natural gas engines, industrial gas turbines and diesel-electric locomotives. For nearly 100 years, we’ve been helping customers build a better, more sustainable world and are committed and contributing to a reduced-carbon future. Our innovative products and services, backed by our global dealer network, provide exceptional value that helps customers succeed.

title: PROJECT ENGINEER
description: Job Responsibilities: Project Engineer to support the migration of our Automatic Fare Collection (AFC) system to a cloud based platform. The Project Engineer will coordinate project activities, track progress, manage budgets, and ensure smooth collaboration across internal teams, contractors, and external stakeholders. This role requires strong independence, technical awareness of AFC and cloud systems, and excellent organisational skills to ensure timely and successful project delivery. • Plan, organise, schedule, and facilitate project meetings with internal teams, contractors, and stakeholders. • Prepare agendas, capture minutes, and follow up on action items to ensure accountability. • Monitor project performance, identify potential risks and issues, and implement mitigation strategies. • Produce and communicate accurate, timely project progress reports for management forums, steering committees, and stakeholders on a regular basis • Ensure project deliverables meet established quality standards, compliance requirements, and customer expectations. • Collaborate with the Finance team to administer project budgets and expenditures. • Facilitate and document design and technical review sessions with contractors, cloud architects, and AFC system specialists. • Track review outcomes to ensure incorporation into the project deliverables. • Promote continuous improvement by conducting post-project evaluations and implementing lessons learned. Required Experience and Skills:- • Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Computer Science, Project Management, or a related discipline. • Proven experience as a Project Engineer or Project Coordinator in technology or system migration projects. • Relevant project management or project experience in the area of Automatic Fare Collection system in Singapore transit environment will be a plus. • Proficient in Microsoft Office for Windows. • Strong organisational and time management skills with ability to work independently. • Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills. • Organizational, leadership, analytical, problem-solving and decision-making skills. • Ability to work under pressure, manage competing priorities, and adapt to changing requirements.

title: Regulatory Reporting – Legal Entity Controller, Assistant Vice President
description: Who we are looking for State Street Corporation’s Finance division is currently seeking an AVP for Legal Entity Reporting mainly being responsible in the Regulatory Reporting Area. As Legal Entity Controller you will take responsibility of financial and regulatory reporting as well as monitoring of various legal entities within the ASEAN region. You will develop an in-depth understanding of various entities within the financial industry, including their business strategies as well as associated technical accounting and disclosure requirements. We are looking for a proactive and self-motivated team player to join our Legal Entity Controller team based in Singapore who is willing to take over responsibilities in a wide range of accounting and reporting requirements in a fast-paced banking environment. Why this role is important to us The team you will be joining is a part of State Street Global Services (SSGS). SSGS gives asset owners and managers access to the essential financial tools they need to deliver effective investment solutions. From core custody, accounting, fund administration and shareholder recordkeeping, to complete operations solutions and servicing for alternative assets like OTC derivatives, private equity and real estate, SSGS’ tools help our clients make better investment choices and act on growth opportunities. Join us if making your mark in the financial services industry from day one is a challenge you are up for. What you will be responsible for As Regulatory Reporting – Legal Entity Controller you will Reporting into Regional ASEAN Controller. Supporting various aspects of legal entity and BU Controllership within the ASEAN region (spanning Singapore, Thailand, Malaysia, and Brunei) to ensure accuracy of financial results and that all internal controls are appropriately designed and executed. Coverage for Global Services, Global Delivery, Global Markets, Global Exchange and Global Advisors in region for Controllership impacts. Regulatory reporting to MAS and implementation of the new MAS 610 framework - Ensure all regulatory returns, statistics and surveys data are prepared accurately and timely, and in compliance with regulatory and statutory requirements which includes maintain and write up of respective procedures and documentation. Collaborate within a small team based out of Singapore and partnering with a wide range of stakeholders including Businesses, the local management team, Lead Financial Officers (LFOs), outsourced functions team (Global Accounting Operations, Billing, Regulatory Reporting) and Finance /functional leadership team. Working closely with Business Controls teams to improve control environment. Coordinate with Global Close and Consolidation team on monthly close cycle process for accuracy and efficiency. Ensure financial controls are operating effectively and issues reported/ resolved and appropriately escalated. Responsibility for Internal Audit results and remediation for Singapore entities. Actively cooperate with the external auditors to finalize statutory reporting for various entities in the ASEAN region advisor including query resolution. Interface and close collaboration with LFO’s and FP&A organization to ensure alignment and accuracy or monthly, Quarterly/ Annual results and alignment to Business Unit initiatives and correct treatment of key events (Impairment, deferrals etc). Support and execution of transformation initiatives, ensure robust control and knowledge transfer for activities migrated to Hub Locations, proper implementation of systems which impact the external & internal financial & regulatory reporting in the ASEAN region. Execute on initiatives that impact accounting, reporting or tax including capital management Support tax compliance (direct and Indirect) including Transfer Pricing scheme. Any other ad-hoc tasks as required by the APAC controller from time-to-time. What we value These skills will help you succeed in this role Ability to work in a fast paced banking environment managing multiple priorities Fast learner, proactive in nature and has excellent attention for details Committed, self-motivated and proactive, with a desire to succeed Team player Ability to think holistically and make connections between diverse issues/tasks Education & Preferred Qualifications Experience with local banking MAS (610, 649) reporting requirements will be required. Qualified Accountant with approximately 10 years of relevant experience. Good knowledge of Singapore GAAP / IFRS preferred Good knowledge of Accounting System (e.g. Oracle, SUN) will be an advantage Excellent written and oral communication skills in English required Strong control awareness Proficient level of Excel knowledge (e.g. Macro-handling) will be an advantage

title: Senior Manager, Ops Finance
description: JOB SUMMARY CIB Operations supports Bank’s Corporate and Investment Banking (CIB) business, delivering a wide range of client support activities across trade, payments, client onboarding and risk services across Bank’s global footprint. With presence in 50+ markets and deep process expertise, the function play a critical role in enabling seamless client delivery and operational excellence. Standard Chartered is undergoing a bold transformation under the Fit for Growth (FFG) program, aimed at simplification, standardization and digitisation keeping client at the centre. This role is instrumental in shaping how CIB Operations delivers on its FFG commitments through effective partnering with FFG initiative owners and CIB Ops leadership team. We are looking for a high-impact finance leader to partner with CIB Ops MT to lead end to end financial planning, forecasting, and cost analysis for CIB Operations, providing challenge and insight to deliver sustainable savings and enable business outcomes through the Fit for Growth journey for the function. RESPONSIBILITIES Strategy •    Strengthen the finance partnership for CIB Operations & Drive delivery of agreed budgets and targets and ensure alignment with the strategic objectives of the CIB Operations & Contribute to build out a centre of expertise finance partnering delivery capability in the Business Performance Operations. Business •    Be the key finance partner to CIB Operations in steering the cost performance agenda for the function. •    Demonstrate a deep-rooted understanding of the CIB Operations. Partner with the CIB Operations to provide deep dive analytics on Performance to drive consistent and sustainable above and beyond business performance. •    Supporting the CIB Operations FFG agenda to deliver the efficiencies and growth opportunities by tracking and reviewing on a regular basis. •    Drive robust cost-challenge conversations and manage performance via agreed metrics. Processes •    Partnering with CIB Operations, business CFO and Regional teams, to drive insight into business and operations performance. Conceptualize and deliver high impact, executive-ready presentations to support the cost performance narrative for engagement across the Group and Business. •    Supporting the formulation and coordination budget, Strategic Plans, Forecasts, and reviews against actual performance with the Product, Group and Segment teams. •    Monitor and evaluate the implementation of key strategic business plans, deep dive and other requested analysis (e.g.cost savings initiatives) for Operations. •    Prepare and coordinate information for presentations to the Board, investor communications, and external presentations by the business heads, finance heads and Investor Relations teams •    Understanding key opportunities for simplifying, standardising, and digitising the Bank’s processes and overall capability •    Be predictive and proactive so we have performance management updates readily available. •    Drive a culture of process excellence permeating through all levels in the organization. •    Strive for innovation – i.e., new ideas to be generated across the team. BAU team to tap their time and deliver Change. •    Ensure compliance with accounting standards, regulatory requirements and management reporting and policies People & Talent •    Lead by example, develop and embed a high-performance culture with specific focus on capability, effectiveness, and productivity. •    Foster a culture of collaboration and a mindset of change and growth Risk Management •    Ability to interpret the firm’s financial information, identify key issues based on this information and put in place appropriate controls and measures. •    Ensure compliance with Group Accounting Governance •    Have an awareness and understanding of the regulatory framework in which the Group operates, and the regulatory requirements and expectations relevant to the role. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate, and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters Key Stakeholders •    CIB Operations •    T&O Management •    T&O and Functions CFO •    Operations CFO •    Group FP&A team. •    Business CFOs Our Ideal Candidate •    Chartered Accountant/CPA/CMA/MBA with 12+ years of finance experience in Finance Partnering, Financial Planning & Analysis, Performance management, quantitative analysis, and market share analysis and/or competitive analysis. •    Deep understanding and strong competence in managing costs and Operations units in the industry. Strong analytical and strategic mindset coupled with a thorough understanding of the Banking Industry. •    In-depth knowledge of the drivers of businesses performance and relevant key performance measures of Banking business •    Ability to understand and connect business drivers relevant to the Performance management processes. •    Ability to work effectively under pressure, multi-task, lead through ambiguities and influence, work with global teams. •    Effective communication skills, including presenting and influencing senior management. •    Make sound decisions/influence making sound decisions based on business priorities. •    Comfortable dealing with incomplete information, uncertainty, change and dealing with ambiguities. •    Excellent Excel & PowerPoint skills •    Excellent written and oral communication skills. •    Relationship and stakeholder management skills is important. •    Initiative and proactive thinking Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Financial Analysis •    Strategy and Planning •    Data Analysis and Visualisation •    Financial Forecasting and Modelling •    Communication/Storytelling •    Influencing and Advisory About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. Recruitment Assessments Some of our roles use assessments to help us understand how suitable you are for the role you've applied to. If you are invited to take an assessment, this is great news. It means your application has progressed to an important stage of our recruitment process. Visit our careers website www.sc.com/careers

Understanding of financial assets (FX, Money Market, cash products) and trade lifecycle. Experience with regulatory reporting (e.g. HRF, NORIA, MMSR). Experience on Back Office systems. title: Risk Partnerships Manager
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies - from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups - use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team Stripe continues to develop innovative products that serve businesses of all sizes around the world, risk will increase in complexity and scale. Joining the Risk Partnerships team, you will play a critical role in the company’s financial and partnership health, enabling Stripe’s growth while partnering with internal and external stakeholders to understand, manage and mitigate against key risks related to financial partners. What you’ll do Stripe is seeking a Risk Partnerships Manager covering our most important financial partners including bank sponsors, Local Payment methods and other key partners. This role will be instrumental in enabling new products and payment methods to help Stripe scale for the future. It’s expected that the Risk Partnerships Manager will be a subject matter expert in card network and bank sponsor programs and requirements. You will develop expertise on Stripe’s risk policies in areas including fraud, credit, and brand risks. You will be relied upon to keep pace with emerging trends in the payments and fintech industries. You will build strategic relationships with Risk counterparts at major financial partners to help drive Stripe’s risk and commercial initiatives. It is equally crucial for you to develop trusting and collaborative relationships with internal stakeholders that will provide subject matter expertise, and be able to execute across multiple programs and initiatives in parallel. Your work will be highly visible to Risk leadership and will be instrumental to driving our future growth. Responsibilities Manage key risk relationships with financial partners including bank sponsors, local payment method, and key partners that enable new products and payment methods. Build relationships, understand financial partner requirements, and advocate for Stripe’s users and product teams with external stakeholders. Effectively communicate complex financial partner risk requirements to Stripe leadership. Ensure these are understood and correctly prioritized by Risk and the broader Global Partnerships team, as well as other cross-functional teams within the organization. Represent Stripe’s risk controls and processes to bank partners, card networks, regulators and other external parties. Develop expertise in Stripe’s risk management systems and maintain a strong understanding of Stripe’s risk posture and evolving technical foundations. Contribute to Stripe risk strategy objectives by proposing process improvements, new risk policies, capabilities, and other areas of thought leadership to drive the company vision. Collaborate with our Financial Partnerships team to drive and advocate for risk objectives in balance with business objectives. Cultivate strong working relationships with colleagues across Risk, Product, Engineering and Operations teams to prioritize, scope and roll out product launches and enhancements. Manage top priority user escalations and incident remediations, as needed. Who you are We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. Minimum requirements 8+ years of professional experience, including 2+ years in the payments industry (Risk Management experience preferred). Partner-facing experience with APAC Payment methods, acquiring bank sponsors, or additional relevant payment methods in APAC. A strong sense of ownership, resilience, and a drive to have an impact. Experience in managing day to day risk initiatives, requirements and incidents across a variety of internal and external partners. Strong stakeholder management skills including navigating difficult situations, negotiating timelines, and influencing internal and external stakeholders across organizations and borders. An analytical, data-driven approach to decision-making – you understand how to partner with Data Science to build metrics and track progress across various monitoring programs. Demonstrated project/program management skills and a proven track record for building and scaling programs from the ground up. Excellent collaboration, communication, and relationship building skills. Must have the ability to convey complex ideas succinctly to all audiences. Ability to prioritize work appropriately and stay focused on the most important and most impactful initiatives at any given time. Preferred qualifications You have an in-depth understanding of product and payment risk platforms. Effective program management skills and a proven track record for building and scaling programs from the ground up. Familiarity with SQL and working with large datasets is a plus.

title: Desk Quant Analyst
description: Squarepoint is a global investment management firm that utilizes a diversified portfolio of systematic and quantitative strategies across financial markets that seeks to achieve high quality, uncorrelated returns for our clients. We have deep expertise in trading, technology and operations and attribute our success to rigorous scientific research. As a technology and data-driven firm, we design and build our own cutting-edge systems, from high performance trading platforms to large scale data analysis and compute farms. With offices around the globe, we emphasize true, global collaboration by aligning our investment, technology and operations teams functionally around the world. Overview of Desk Quant Analyst Position: Maintain and upgrade the codebase and configuration of strategies within the firm’s automated trading framework. Manage large datasets used in both research and production environments. Perform live as well as historical monitoring of trading activities. For instance, do some post-trade analysis or production reconciliation. Work in close collaboration with quant researchers and traders to understand the constantly evolving needs of the trading desks. Typical Day of Desk Quant Analyst: Before market open, check that all required data and related processes are ready for the trading day. During market hours, sporadically monitor behavior and performance of strategies. Work on various longer-term projects required for expanding our systematic trading activities and ensuring their robustness. Skill Set Required For Position: Degree in Engineering, Computer Science or related technical subject. Programming proficiency with at least one major programming or scripting language (e.g. C++, Java, Python). Great attention to details. Strong communication skills and ability to work well with colleagues across multiple regions. Ability to work well under pressure. Additional Desirable Qualifications: Knowledge of Q/KDB+. Knowledge of Linux and Bash. Advanced quantitative skills.

title: Credit Risk Manager (Retail, Credit Cards)
description: Responsibilities: Own and continuously enhance retail credit risk frameworks for credit card underwriting and portfolio management, ensuring alignment with regulatory standards and internal risk appetite. Lead the drafting, review, and implementation of credit card–specific credit policies, underwriting criteria, and risk controls; assess policy effectiveness and recommend enhancements. Partner closely with risk modelling and analytics teams to drive the development, validation, implementation, and ongoing monitoring of credit risk models (e.g. scorecards, Basel, ECL), including review of model outputs and assumptions. Conduct in-depth credit card portfolio analytics, covering approval trends, risk grade migration, delinquency and loss performance, vintage analysis, early warning indicators, and concentration risks. Manage risk appetite and limit frameworks for credit card portfolios, including limit calibration, stress testing analysis, breach monitoring, and escalation. Provide risk input and challenge to business, underwriting, and product teams on credit card strategy reviews, policy changes, new product initiatives, and portfolio optimisation proposals. Produce and review recurring and ad-hoc risk reports for senior management, synthesising complex portfolio data into clear risk insights and actionable recommendations. Prepare and present materials for governance forums, committees, and internal stakeholders, highlighting portfolio performance, underwriting outcomes, key risk metrics, and emerging risk themes. Support junior team members through guidance and review of analytical work, contributing to team capability building and knowledge sharing. Drive process improvements and automation initiatives to enhance efficiency, data quality, and scalability of credit risk monitoring and reporting. Requirements: Bachelor’s degree or above in a quantitative discipline (e.g. Statistics, Mathematics, Financial Engineering, Quantitative Finance, Data Analytics) from a reputable institution. Minimum 3 years of relevant working full-time experience in retail credit products, such as credit cards and unsecured personal loans, or other financial risk domains. Proficiency in data and analytical tools such as SQL is a plus. Experience with additional analytics or BI tools is an advantage. Familiarity with credit card–relevant credit risk concepts, including scorecards, Basel, ECL, and stress testing. Demonstrated ability to independently conduct complex analyses and translate results into clear risk recommendations for decision-makers. Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to provide constructive risk challenge to business and product partners. Detail-oriented, well-organised, and able to operate effectively in a fast-paced, structured environment. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Note: Kindly note that you can only be considered for one role at a time with any of the companies within our group. If you have applied for other jobs with the group, you will be considered for roles in the order of your application

title: Sales Support Specialist
description: About MIJ Hub My Inspiring Journey (MIJ) Hub is a leading and rapidly growing private school and non-governmental organization (NGO) dedicated to transforming the lives of individuals aged 4 to 40 with diverse learning needs. Through our comprehensive educational programs, vocational training, and community integration initiatives, we have been creating positive change locally for over 13 years. As we strive to be the ultimate one-stop center for the special needs community in the region, we are proud to open our first international centre, MIJ International in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia., At MIJ Hub, we are pioneers in innovation. By managing multiple for-profit ventures, including a food and beverage (F&B) arm, we generate sustainable income streams that allow us to provide subsidies, ensuring parents and students have access to affordable, high-quality education. Join MIJ Hub and become part of a vibrant, dynamic team committed to driving financial sustainability, fostering inclusivity, and expanding our reach to make a real difference in the community. Job Title: Sales Support Specialist (Part-time) At MIJ Hub, the Sales Support Specialist plays a vital role in driving our sales function. This position offers an exciting opportunity to make a meaningful impact as an individual contributor. It’s ideal for someone who thrives in a customer-focused environment and is eager to bring their extensive retail sales experience to a dynamic and innovative team. In this role, you will drive sales across all MIJ brands, including student enrollment for MIJ Hub, securing donations, and promoting product sales from Ashraf Café and Inspo. You will engage with customers via calls, emails, and WhatsApp to close sales, upsell, and retain customers. Additionally, you will collaborate closely with our marketing team to ensure a seamless experience between our e-commerce platforms and sales efforts. You’ll also be responsible for utilizing HubSpot to manage customer data, track sales activities, and maintain accurate records. Key Responsibilities ● Support Sales Specialist to drive sales revenue across all MIJ brands, focusing on donations and product sales ● Utilize HubSpot to manage customer data, track sales activities, and maintain accurate Records. prepare sales materials, and ensure a seamless customer journey. ● Coordinate with other departments to support smooth and efficient sales operations. ● Address customer inquiries, resolve issues, and maintain high levels of satisfaction. ● Hands-on experience with CMS platforms, including Payload, to support smooth integration between marketing and sales workflows. ●Strong experimentation mindset, comfortable testing variations in messaging, offers, and channels to identify what drives higher engagement and conversions. Requirements ● Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Communications, or a related field. ● Exceptional interpersonal skills with a talent for building rapport and fostering long-term customer relationships. ● Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, adept at engaging with customers across calls, emails, and WhatsApp. ● Proven ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. ● Strong problem-solving skills with a proactive approach to addressing customer needs and concerns. ● Experience with CRM systems (preferably HubSpot), and a good grasp of digital tools to track and manage customer interactions. ● Ability to work closely with cross-functional teams, particularly marketing, to enhance customer engagement and drive sales. Able to commit as 3 days in a week from 9am to 4pm.

title: Senior Data Scientist
description: Who We Are Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact. To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive. We Are BCG X We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. What You'll Do Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions. As a Data Scientist, you'll be part of our rapidly growing team. Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X. Successful candidates are intellectually curious builders who are biased toward action, scrappy, and communicative. Successful candidates possess the following: Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner Love building things and are comfortable working with modern development tools and writing code collaboratively (bonus points if you have a software development or DevOps experience) Significant experience applying advanced analytics to a variety of business situations and a proven ability to synthesize complex data Deep understanding of modern machine learning techniques and their mathematical underpinnings, and can translate this into business implications for our clients Have strong project management skills What You'll Bring Master’s Degree or PhD with significant relevant experience providing advanced analytics solutions. The degree should be in computer science, applied mathematics, statistics, machine learning, or a related data centric field. Demonstrated deep technical and data science expertise, acute strategic and analytical skills, ability to lead and persuade, drive and energy, and desire to work in a project-based environment on strategic issues. Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner Strong record of professional accomplishment and leadership. Fluency in Python Additional info Ability to travel based on client and business needs.

description: About Us 🔹We are a high-growth Web3 payment infrastructure company redefining how users and businesses interact with stablecoins and digital assets. 🔹Backed by licensed financial institutions, and trusted by leading Web3 partners, we provide crypto-fiat payment rails, card-issuing infrastructure, and CaaS (Cards-as-a-Service) solutions for exchanges, wallets, protocols, and global fintech innovators. 🔹Our team comes from top global financial institutions, tier-one tech companies, and leading fintechs across APAC and Europe — bringing deep expertise, a builder mindset, and a shared passion for shaping the future of compliant, scalable financial infrastructure. About the Role We are seeking a Business Operations Manager to serve as the strategic engine of our commercial organization. This is a high-impact leadership position designed for an operational architect. You will be responsible for the end-to-end efficiency of the sales lifecycle—from drafting complex commercial contracts and designing high-stakes sales collateral to leading the project management of merchant onboarding. The ideal candidate is a master of documentation, a data-driven storyteller, and a meticulous project lead who can navigate high-growth ambiguity to build scalable, zero-error processes. Sales Enablement & Saleskit Strategy Collateral Creation: Lead the drafting and design of high-impact Saleskit slides (PPT) . Translate complex technical product features and TPV data into clear, compelling value propositions for executive-level prospects. Market Intelligence: Partner with Product and Marketing to develop "Battlecards" and competitive pitch decks, equipping the sales team to handle objections and close deals in a competitive market. Asset Management: Own the centralized sales asset library, ensuring version control so that global sales teams are always utilizing current, compliant, and on-brand materials. Trainings: Arrange regular trainings for sales team, enabling sales team with latest industry insight, product releases, marketing strategies and internal polices/processes. 3. Strategic Onboarding Management Implementation Leadership: Serve as the Lead Project Manager for the onboarding journey, orchestrating a seamless transition from "Closed-Won" to "Go-Live" by coordinating between Sales, Tech, and Customer Success. Process Engineering: Continuously audit and optimize the onboarding platforms to reduce "Time-to-Live." Identify and remove friction points within the KYC, AML, and Technical API integration phases. Performance Monitoring: Establish and monitor KPIs for the onboarding pipeline, providing visibility into implementation velocity and ensuring a "white-glove" experience for new partners. What You Bring Core Requirements Documentation Mastery: Proven track record in drafting complex commercial documents and creating executive-level PPT presentations that drive high-level stakeholder decision-making. Onboarding Management: 3–5+ years of experience in Sales Ops or Onboarding Management, specifically managing multi-stakeholder onboarding or implementation projects (Fintech/PSP experience is a significant advantage). Analytical Aptitude: Expert-level Excel modelling skills . Ability to build financial models to calculate TPV forecasts, revenue shares, and multi-tranche incentive structures. Attention to Detail: A meticulous approach to documentation and contract drafting where a "zero-error" mindset is applied to ensure operational excellence. Education: Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, or a related field. Why Join Us & Our Culture Impact: Drive meaningful BD strategy and influence the growth of the next-generation Web3 payments. Innovation: Work on cutting-edge crypto-fiat card infrastructure used by leading Web3 companies. Global Exposure: Collaborate across teams in APAC, Middle East, and Europe. Growth: Learn fast, access strategic decision-making, and scale your career in a high-growth startup. Culture: A flat, collaborative, high-ownership environment where your ideas directly influence product direction. We value people who are: Builders who thrive in 0→1 and 1→100 environments Owners who take initiative and move fast Learners who grow through feedback and curiosity Collaborators who elevate the team Visionaries who care about shaping the future of stablecoin DeCard — Product Highlight Spend Stablecoins Seamlessly, Anywhere DeCard is a next-generation crypto-fiat card powered by DCS Card Centre, a MAS-licensed financial institution with 50+ years of payment expertise. Top up stablecoins and spend instantly at millions of merchants worldwide — bridging Web3 with real-world payments. Learn more on LinkedIn Decard & website: DCS Card Centre and DeCard How to Apply Excited about shaping the future of Web3 payments? Join us and redefine digital finance.
