title: Senior Manager, Content & Digital Strategy (34-month contract), OCCM
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION Singapore Management University is a place where high-level professionalism blends together with a healthy informality. The 'family-like' atmosphere among the SMU community fosters a culture where employees work, plan, organise and play together - building a strong collegiality and morale within the university. Our commitment to attract and retain talent is ongoing. We offer attractive benefits and welfare, competitive compensation packages, and generous professional development opportunities - all to meet the work-life needs of our staff. No wonder, then, that SMU continues to be given numerous awards and recognition for its human resource excellence. RESPONSIBILITIES This position is for Office of Corporate Communications and Marketing (OCCM). Content Strategy and Editorial Oversight Drive the development and execution of a coherent, audience-led content strategy across SMU's owned digital platforms, ensuring coherence with institutional narratives and brand positioning. Lead editorial planning and governance for content across English and Chinese platforms, ensuring consistency of tone, accuracy of translation, and strategic alignment. Partner with internal stakeholders, and agency partners to shape content that effectively supports reputation-building, engagement, and acquisition goals. Search-Optimised and Discoverability-Driven Strategy (SEO, AEO, AIO, GEO) Drive content optimisation to enhance visibility across search engines, AI-generated responses, and geo-targeted platforms. Integrate keyword, entity, and schema strategies in both English and Chinese content. Guide best practices in content architecture, metadata, and analytics-informed optimisation. Stay abreast of emerging SEO trends, including multimodal search, entity-based optimisation, and voice or chatbot interfaces—translating these into actionable content strategy. Multi-Format Content Creation and Channel Adaptation Develop and oversee the production of compelling content across short-form, long-form, and multimedia formats for web, email, and social platforms. Adapt messaging across different channels to ensure platform-specific resonance, audience relevance, and content cohesion. Provide expert copywriting for campaign messaging, social captions, ad copy, and email flows with clear calls-to-action and brand alignment. Community Engagement and Monitoring Engage with online communities across the region, responding to audience queries and comments in a culturally appropriate manner. Monitor brand mentions and address any emerging issues, escalating potential risks to the Social Lead when needed. Performance Analytics and ROI Evaluation Drive content visibility and discoverability through integrated SEO, AIO (AI Optimisation), AEO (Answer Engine Optimisation), and GEO (Geographical/Global Engine Optimisation) strategies across English and Chinese platforms. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure metadata, structured data, and content architecture support both search and AI-driven discovery. Track and analyse performance across search, social, and owned channels using tools such as GA4, Search Console, SEMrush, and other analytics platforms. Translate insights into actionable recommendations for content optimisation, user engagement, and lead conversion. Stay abreast of evolving search, AI, and platform algorithms to inform ongoing optimisation and content planning. Cross-Functional Collaboration and Alignment Partner with colleagues across brand, corporate communications, university events, admissions, alumni, and faculties to ensure content supports cross-functional campaign and reputational goals. Guide and mentor junior team members or freelancers to deliver high-quality content that meets editorial standards and marketing priorities. Engage external writers, editors, or agencies when needed, ensuring alignment with SMU's tone, brand, and strategic goals. Any other duties as assigned. QUALIFICATIONS Bachelor's degree in Journalism, Mass Communications, Marketing, Digital Media, or a related field. Effectively bilingual with proficiency in Chinese equivalent to ‘HSK Level 6' or ‘Business Chinese Test (BCT) Advanced or higher'. Proven experience in content strategy, SEO/AIO/AEO/GEO optimisation, and digital storytelling. Strong editorial and copywriting capabilities in both English and Chinese, with a clear, engaging, and adaptable writing style, and across formats—blog articles, web copy, email campaigns, and social media content—with a clear, audience-first approach. Proven ability to craft compelling narratives and adapt messaging for different platforms, while maintaining brand voice and institutional credibility. Solid understanding of modern SEO principles, including keyword targeting, metadata optimisation, and content structuring for search engines and social platforms. Familiarity with how content is discovered across traditional search, generative AI tools (e.g. ChatGPT), and social platforms (e.g. TikTok, LinkedIn). Working knowledge of tools like Google Search Console, SEMrush, or similar, with ability to translate insights into action. Experience using tools like Google Analytics, HubSpot, or equivalent to assess content impact across traffic, engagement, and conversion metrics. Ability to derive content insights from data and translate them into optimised content strategy and reporting narratives. Hands-on experience creating and adapting content across web, email, and social channels (LinkedIn, Instagram, YouTube, TikTok). Working understanding of platform trends and engagement formats, with the ability to optimise content for different devices and audiences. Proven ability to collaborate with internal teams, freelancers, and agency partners to deliver timely and effective content. Strong project management and organisational skills, with the ability to juggle multiple content streams and campaign deadlines. OTHER INFORMATION #LI-XL1 Candidates who do not possess the stipulated qualifications but have relevant work experience may still apply. Remuneration and appointment terms shall commensurate with qualifications and experience. SMU reserves the right to modify the appointment terms where necessary. title: Business Development Executive / Senior Business Development Executive
description: About OA Group of Companies (OA GOC) OA Group of Companies (OA GOC) is a regional professional services group headquartered in Singapore, with an expanding presence across Malaysia and the region. We partner with growth-minded business owners, CFOs, and leadership teams to build strong corporate foundations, enhance governance and compliance, and enable sustainable growth. Our multidisciplinary platform spans Audit & Assurance, Accounting & Outsourcing, Corporate Secretarial & Compliance, Tax Compliance & Advisory, Business Advisory, and Digital Transformation . By integrating compliance, financial insight, and advisory thinking, we help clients move from operational stability to strategic scale. At OA GOC, we believe that strong growth is built on professionalism, ownership, and long-term partnerships — both with our clients and our people. Why This Role Exists As OA GOC enters its next phase of growth, we are intentionally building a high-calibre Business Development team to drive long-term client partnerships and group-wide revenue expansion. This is not a transactional sales role. The Business Development function at OA GOC exists to work alongside business owners and finance leaders, understand their challenges early, and shape integrated solutions across the OA GOC platform. The role plays a direct and visible part in the firm’s growth trajectory. Role Overview We are hiring Business Development Executives and Senior Business Development Executives to support and accelerate OA GOC’s growth. This role is suitable for individuals who are commercially driven, disciplined, and motivated by measurable outcomes. You will be responsible for originating opportunities, building trusted relationships, and converting prospects into long-term clients. You will operate in a professional services environment where credibility, clarity, and relationship quality matter. Key Responsibilities Identify, qualify, and convert business opportunities across SMEs and mid-market clients Build trusted relationships with founders, CFOs, finance leaders, and senior executives Drive outreach through targeted campaigns, networking, events, referrals, and partnerships Prepare and present structured proposals and presentations aligned to client needs Own and manage your sales pipeline with discipline, forecasting accuracy, and accountability Work closely with internal delivery teams to shape solutions and support client success What Success Looks Like in This Role Building a consistent and high-quality opportunity pipeline within the first 3–6 months Developing repeat and referral relationships with clients Demonstrating strong conversion discipline and commercial judgement Progressing from opportunity execution to owning key accounts, sectors, or growth initiatives Making a visible contribution to group-level revenue growth Who We Are Looking For We welcome candidates from different professional backgrounds, particularly individuals who are: Former accountants, auditors, tax agents, or corporate secretarial professionals seeking a transition into business development Business development professionals looking for a more strategic, professional services-oriented role The ideal candidate will demonstrate: Strong drive, ownership mentality, and coachability Professional communication and relationship-building skills Confidence engaging with senior stakeholders Comfort being measured by outcomes and results Discipline in pipeline management and follow-through For Professionals Transitioning from Accounting, Audit, Tax or Corporate Secretarial This role is well-suited for professionals who understand financial, compliance, and governance frameworks and want to apply that knowledge commercially. OA GOC provides structured support to help you develop sales and client-engagement skills while retaining the professionalism and credibility you have built in your technical career. You will not be expected to “hard sell”, but to position value, ask the right questions, and build trust with clients. Career Development High performers in this role can progress into senior business development leadership, key account ownership, sector-focused growth roles, or group-level commercial functions . Advancement is merit-based and tied to performance, capability, and leadership potential. What We Offer Clear and structured career progression Mentorship and ongoing professional development Competitive base salary with uncapped incentive compensation A professional, performance-focused team environment A meaningful role in shaping the growth of OA GOC Why Join OA GOC This is an opportunity to build a long-term career in business development within a respected professional services group that values professionalism, ambition, and impact. This role is suited for individuals who take ownership of outcomes, are comfortable with accountability, and want to grow alongside the firm.

title: Business Analytics and Data Specialist
description: At #TeamCPF, you’re not just joining a team; you are embracing a culture of excellence, collaboration, and meaningful impact. We thrive on sharp minds and insightful decisions. Your ability to analyse and think critically isn't just valued; it's essential. Every choice you make contributes to our collective success. Collaboration is our way of life. We believe in the power of effective partnerships and seamless communications across teams. Together, we amplify each other’s strengths and achieve remarkable results. Our learning never stops. We encourage your inquisitiveness and courage to embrace new challenges head-on. Your agility, readiness to challenge conventions, embrace of data-driven strategies, dedication to learning and applying new skills fuels our innovation and progress. At the core of everything we do lies a genuine desire to make a difference. We serve our community and support each other with compassion, empathy, and unwavering dedications. Every action we take is guided by a deep sense of purpose and a commitment to those we serve. Join us at #TeamCPF! Together, let's redefine possibilities and leave a legacy that echoes for generations. What you will be working on As a Business Analytics and Data Specialist, you will be instrumental in transforming data into actionable insights that drive strategic decision-making and operational excellence across the Agency Services Group. You will analyse data trends to project future operational needs, detect shifts in citizen behaviour to anticipate policy implications, design automation workflows and explore emerging technologies to achieve cost, resource and manpower efficiency. In this role, you will: Analyse data and conduct research studies to support policy reviews and optimise business processes. Implement data initiatives and programmes across the Agency Services Group and build staff capabilities through training and knowledge transfer. Set up and maintain data marts with proper data governance framework and sharing guidelines. Explore and implement automation solutions to reduce manual effort and streamline work processes. Perform environmental scans to identify emerging technologies and automation opportunities to achieve greater operational efficiency. What we are looking for We value the diverse talents and experiences that each individual brings to the table. While mastery of every requirement may not be necessary, familiarity and expertise in some of the following areas will position you for success within this team. Relevant experience in business analytics, data analysis, or related fields with understanding of citizen disbursement schemes to support and drive business process optimisation and sustainability. Have experience in data analysis, critical thinking and problem-solving. Have experience in organisational and project management. Knowledge and prior experience with Databricks, Python, UiPath and/or other programming software is an added advantage. Keen interest in leveraging emerging technology to enhance data analytics capabilities. Communicates effectively, both in writing and speaking. Works independently and manages priorities effectively. Adaptable and resourceful in handling different tasks and challenges. The seniority of appointment and actual corporate job title will commensurate with individual work experiences. We welcome fresh graduates in any discipline and candidates without working experience to apply. To help us better understand your academic background, please provide an official or unofficial copy of your academic transcript, as well as any other relevant documents or certificates that you believe would support your application. Position is on 1-year full time contract directly under the payroll of CPF Board with potential for emplacement into permanent position. What you can expect Being part of #TeamCPF means embarking on a challenging and rewarding career in a progressive workplace that values productivity and growth. Here’s what awaits you: Opportunities to engage in a mix of formal and informal training, keeping your skills sharp in our ever-evolving technological landscape. Promotion opportunities based on your capability and on-the-job performance. A vibrant community of like-minded and friendly colleagues, where collaboration and creativity thrive. A hybrid work model that offers flexibility for remote work, subject to exigencies of service. Flexible dress code that empowers you to choose your appropriate outfit for the day. A comprehensive rewards package that includes annual leave, pro-family leave, medical and dental benefits, and access to recreational activities.

title: Sales Operations Manager (Web3 Payment Fintech)
description:
About Us 🔹We are a high-growth Web3 payment infrastructure company redefining how users and businesses interact with stablecoins and digital assets. 🔹Backed by licensed financial institutions, and trusted by leading Web3 partners, we provide crypto-fiat payment rails, card-issuing infrastructure, and CaaS (Cards-as-a-Service) solutions for exchanges, wallets, protocols, and global fintech innovators. 🔹Our team comes from top global financial institutions, tier-one tech companies, and leading fintechs across APAC and Europe — bringing deep expertise, a builder mindset, and a shared passion for shaping the future
of compliant, scalable financial infrastructure. About the Role We are seeking a Sales Operations Manager to serve as the strategic engine of our commercial organization. This is a high-impact leadership position designed for an operational architect. You will be responsible for the end-to-end efficiency of the sales lifecycle—from drafting complex commercial contracts and designing high-stakes sales collateral to leading the project management of merchant onboarding. The ideal candidate is a
master of documentation, a data-driven storyteller, and a meticulous project lead who can navigate high-growth ambiguity to build scalable, zero-error processes. What You Will Do Contract Management & Drafting Excellence Template Architecture: Lead on drafting business requirements and maintenance of standardized sales contract templates, ensuring they remain agile and updated with current commercial terms, pricing tiers, and legal requirements. Operational Governance: Manage the end-to-end Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) process. Ensure that every executed agreement is mapped correctly to the CRM for accurate revenue attribution and audit readiness. Legal Liaison: Act as the primary interface between Sales and Legal teams to facilitate the redlining process, ensuring speed of execution without compromising compliance. 2. Sales Enablement & Saleskit Strategy Collateral Creation: Lead the drafting and design of high-impact Saleskit slides (PPT) . Translate complex technical product features and TPV data into clear, compelling value propositions for executive-level prospects. Market Intelligence: Partner with Product and Marketing to develop "Battlecards" and competitive pitch decks, equipping the sales team to handle objections and close deals in a competitive market. Asset Management: Own the centralized sales asset library, ensuring version control so that global sales teams are always utilizing current, compliant, and on-brand materials. Trainings: Arrange regular trainings for sales team, enabling sales team with latest industry insight, product releases, marketing strategies and internal polices/processes. 3. Strategic Onboarding Management Implementation Leadership: Serve as the Lead Project Manager for the onboarding journey, orchestrating a seamless transition from "Closed-Won" to "Go-Live" by coordinating between Sales, Tech, and Customer Success. Process Engineering: Continuously audit and optimize the onboarding platforms to reduce "Time-to-Live." Identify and remove friction points within the KYC, AML, and Technical API integration phases. Performance Monitoring: Establish and monitor KPIs for the onboarding pipeline, providing visibility into implementation velocity and ensuring a "white-glove" experience for new partners. What You Bring Core Requirements Documentation Mastery: Proven track record in drafting complex commercial documents and creating executive-level PPT presentations that drive high-level stakeholder decision-making. Onboarding Management: 3–5+ years of experience in Sales Ops or Onboarding Management, specifically managing multi-stakeholder onboarding or implementation projects (Fintech/PSP experience is a significant advantage). Analytical Aptitude: Expert-level Excel modelling skills . Ability to build financial models to calculate TPV forecasts, revenue shares, and multi-tranche incentive structures. Attention to Detail: A meticulous approach to documentation and contract drafting where a "zero-error" mindset is applied to ensure operational excellence. Education: Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, or a related field. Why Join Us & Our Culture Impact: Drive meaningful BD strategy and influence the growth of the next-generation Web3 payments. Innovation: Work on cutting-edge crypto-fiat card infrastructure used by leading Web3 companies. Global Exposure: Collaborate across teams in APAC, Middle East, and Europe. Growth: Learn fast, access strategic decision-making, and scale your career in a high-growth startup. Culture: A flat, collaborative, high-ownership environment where your ideas directly influence product
direction. We value people who are: Builders who thrive in 0→1 and 1→100 environments Owners who take initiative and move fast Learners who grow through feedback and curiosity Collaborators who elevate the team Visionaries who care about shaping the future of stablecoin DeCard — Product Highlight Spend Stablecoins Seamlessly, Anywhere DeCard is a next-generation crypto-fiat card powered by DCS Card Centre, a MAS-licensed financial institution with 50+ years of payment expertise. Top up stablecoins and spend instantly at millions of merchants worldwide — bridging Web3 with real-world payments. Learn more on LinkedIn Decard & website: DCS Card Centre and DeCard How to Apply Excited about shaping the future of Web3 payments? Join us and redefine digital finance. Apply here We are an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are
committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

title: Head, GMO Business Performance Management & Communication
description: JOB SUMMARY The Head of Global Markets Operations Business Performance Management is a senior leadership role responsible for driving performance discipline, transparency, and continuous improvement across Global Markets Operations. The role owns the business performance management framework, including financial management, capacity and productivity analytics, service delivery metrics, and strategic performance reporting. This role enables Operations leadership to make data-driven decisions, optimize cost and capacity, improve service outcomes, and align operational performance with Global Markets strategy. What Success Looks Like •    Clear, consistent, and trusted performance insights across Global Markets Operations. •    Improved cost efficiency, productivity, and service outcomes. •    Strong alignment between operational performance, strategy, and investment decisions. •    High-performing, analytically strong global teams enabling Operations leadership. RESPONSIBILITIES Business •    Design, implement, and own the Global Markets Operations performance management framework. •    Establish consistent KPIs, metrics, and scorecards across trading, post-trade, clearing, collateral, client, and regulatory operations. •    Ensure alignment between operational performance metrics, business strategy, and risk appetite. Processes •    Oversee operations financial management, including budgets, forecasts, cost allocations, and expense transparency. •    Partner with Finance to manage headcount, run-rate, productivity, and cost efficiency initiatives. •    Provide clear insights into cost drivers, unit costs, and return on investment for operational initiatives. •    Lead capacity planning, demand forecasting, and workforce optimization across regions and functions. •    Develop productivity and throughput metrics to improve utilization and scalability. •    Support location strategy, sourcing decisions, and operating model optimization. •    Define and monitor service delivery metrics, SLAs, and quality indicators across Operations. •    Identify performance gaps impacting client experience, revenue enablement, or risk outcomes. •    Partner with Operations leadership to drive remediation and continuous improvement initiatives. •    Deliver high-quality management information, dashboards, and executive reporting for senior leadership. •    Provide actionable insights, trend analysis, and scenario modeling to support strategic decision-making. •    Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and transparency across performance reports. •    Support Operations transformation initiatives by defining performance baselines and success measures. •    Track benefits realization for automation, digitization, and process re-engineering initiatives. •    Drive a culture of continuous improvement and performance accountability. People & Talent •    Lead, develop, and retain a global regulatory operations organization. •    Build a strong leadership bench and succession pipeline. •    Foster a culture of accountability, control ownership, and regulatory excellence. •    Champion diversity, equity, and inclusion. Risk Management •    Establish governance forums to review performance, cost, and capacity outcomes. •    Partner with Operations, Finance, Technology, HR, and Risk to align performance objectives. •    Represent Operations Business Performance Management in senior leadership and strategy forums. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    Lead and develop a global Business Performance Management team. •    Build strong analytical, financial, and data capabilities within the organization. •    Foster a culture of ownership, transparency, and continuous improvement. •    Champion diversity, equity, and inclusion. Our Ideal Candidate •	Education - Bachelor’s degree in finance, business, economics, or a related discipline required. •	Advanced degree (MBA or equivalent) preferred. •	15+ years of experience in Global markets operations Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Strong financial acumen and analytical capability. •    Expertise in performance measurement, management information, and data analytics. •    Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights. •    Experience supporting large-scale operational transformation •    Demonstrated ability to engage effectively with senior management and regulators. •    Strong judgment and ability to provide constructive, credible challenge. About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. Recruitment Assessments Some of our roles use assessments to help us understand how suitable you are for the role you've applied to. If you are invited to take an assessment, this is great news. It means your application has progressed to an important stage of our recruitment process. Visit our careers website www.sc.com/careers

title: Senior Principal Data Scientist
description: Get to Know the Team The Fulfilment Tech Family is an integral part of Grab, connecting our wide-ranging marketplaces across Southeast Asia. We develop real-time distributed systems and machine learning solutions to process hundreds of millions of requests daily, ensuring efficient allocation of supply, pricing, and seamless order matching. Our mission: Build reliable and impactful products for driver-partners. Optimize the ability to fulfil consumer needs in any conditions. Develop sustainable marketplaces, balancing user experience and operational costs across stakeholders. We're hiring a Senior Principal Data Scientist to join the Fulfilment team. You're an individual contributor whom will lead strategic optimization initiatives that elevate pricing, dispatch, and supply management strategies. You'll combine in-depth data-driven methodologies with a strategic mindset to drive automation and marketplace efficiency. Get to Know the Role As a Senior Principal Data Scientist, you'll shape and advance cross-system Fulfilment strategies, accounting for how passengers and driver-partners respond to platform interventions. You'll leverage machine learning and operations research methodologies to optimize marketplace-level operational outcomes. Through joint optimization across systems—pricing, dispatch, and supply positioning—you'll design interpretable, adaptable, and multi-objective-aware Reinforcement Learning decision agents. You'll model marketplace behaviour and develop policies to automate key aspects of platform operations, contributing lasting impact for customers, driver-partners, and Grab. You'll report to the Head of Data Science and work onsite at Grab's One North Singapore office. The Critical Tasks You Will Perform You will: Develop ML and DL models to improve generalisation and operational efficiency Create unified Reinforcement Learning architectures that coordinate multiple elements, including pricing, dispatch, and supply positioning, while balancing diverse marketplace objectives. Design frameworks for joint optimization of marketplace systems through hierarchical or multi-agent RL approaches that push the operational Pareto frontier. Explore representations of data that consider supply-demand signals, behaviour elasticity, and factors like weather, traffic, and user intent. Build interpretable models to capture passenger and driver decision-making across diverse scenarios and operational conditions. Devise feedback loops to respond to passenger and driver behaviour, considering geographical differences and behavioural patterns over time. Collaborate with data engineers and backend teams to refine and integrate optimization models into real-time production systems. Work with the platform and experimentation teams to validate models in live environments and drive iterative improvements. Guide technical teams by leading efforts in data science while fostering collaboration and excellence. What Essential Skills You Will Need You have a Master Degree in Computer Science, Operations Research, Applied Mathematics, or related field with at least 10 years of relevant experience. You have experience with Reinforcement Learning, MDPs, stochastic control, or behavioural modelling under uncertainty. You have experience deploying ML models in online learning systems, sequential decision frameworks, or simulation-based techniques for optimization tasks. You have fluency in Python and experience with ML libraries such as PyTorch or TensorFlow for developing robust learning algorithms. You are familiar with tools such as Spark or Ray to build scalable workflows for data-driven optimization and training. You can bridge business challenges into concrete modelling problems with actionable outputs. You have advanced skills to interpret platform data and understand marketplace elasticity, user behaviour, and external environmental influences, contributing to actionable insights for real-world applications. You may also apply directly via https://smrtr.io/wRtCm

title: Research Associate (Singapore Literature)
description: The English Programme at Nanyang Technological University hosts a dynamic community of students and professors engaged in a wide variety of literary passions and projects. For more details, please view https://www.ntu.edu.sg/soh/about-us/english . We are looking for a Research Associate to provide research and administrative support for an MOE Tier 1-funded project, “Smart Nation: A Literary Atlas”. The project is a scholarly intervention that sets out to provide a fresh perspective on the complex negotiations between state, society, and science and technology in Singapore in order to address the vital and urgent questions pertaining to the necessary re-evaluation of these relationships in the twenty-first century and the role that culture plays in this task. Key outputs include a monograph on cultural representations of science and technology in Singaporean literature and a seminar series featuring prominent researchers working at the intersection of science and technology and literature. Key Responsibilities: Collate annotated bibliography of Singaporean literary works that engage with science and technology as well as related themes Close read Singaporean literary works that engage with science and technology as well as related themes Assist with archival research, both online and on-site Assist with the preparation of manuscripts Assist with administrative tasks pertaining to the organization of a seminar series Oversee a small team of undergraduate student research assistants Job Requirements: Master qualification degree in English Literature or related field Good written and oral communication skills Ability to work independently under strict timelines with an eye for detail Familiarity with Singaporean literature and/or literature engaging with science and technology-related issues is an advantage. title: Full Professor (Tenured) in Environment and Society
description: Asian School of the Environment Nanyang Technological University, Singapore Full Professor in Environment and Society The Asian School of the Environment (ASE) ( https://www.ntu.edu.sg/ase ) is an interdisciplinary school at Nanyang Technological University (NTU) that uses Earth and environmental science, engineering, technology, and the social sciences to address key environmental challenges. Many ASE researchers work closely with the Earth Observatory of Singapore ( https://earthobservatory.sg/ ), and/or Singapore Centre for Environmental Life Sciences Engineering ( https://scelse.sg/ ), and/or Centre for Climate Change and Environmental Health, CCEH ( https://www.ntu.edu.sg/cceh ). The Asian School of the Environment (ASE), College of Science (CoS) at the Nanyang Technological University (NTU) in Singapore seeks to appoint an accomplished and visionary scientist as Full Professor in Environment and Society. We welcome applicants with research interests including but not limited to environmental governance and decision-making, political ecology, environmental or ecological economics, risk and resilience in socio-environmental systems, and coupled human–environment systems. Candidates in relevant fields may also be considered for additional appointment at the Earth Observatory of Singapore (EOS). Qualities and Leadership Candidates should have a strong track record of impactful, interdisciplinary research, demonstrated leadership in securing major grants and building collaborative research networks, supervising graduate students, experience mentoring junior faculty, and contributions to institutional or programmatic development, especially within interdisciplinary environments. The successful candidate will have opportunities and will be expected to convene and inspire diverse teams, fostering collaborations across disciplines and institutions to develop, pursue, and lead large-scale, high-impact research initiatives that address pressing environmental and societal challenges. Applicants must have a PhD in a relevant field and would normally already hold a tenured Professorial position in a university (or equivalent institution). The successful candidate will: • Build and lead a pioneering research programme in environment and society • Convene and lead large collaborative projects and initiatives at regional and global scales • Teach at undergraduate and postgraduate levels in ASE • Contribute to mentoring and academic leadership within NTU • Develop collaborations with researchers across NTU, Singapore, and internationally ASE and CoS seeks a diverse and inclusive workforce and is committed to equality of opportunity. We welcome applications from all and recruit based on merit, regardless of age, race, gender, religion, marital status and family responsibilities, or disability. Emoluments The commencing salary is competitive and will be commensurate with the candidate's qualifications and experience. The University offers a comprehensive fringe benefit package. Application Procedure Interested applicants are invited to submit through the NTU career portal a completed application package which include: 1. Cover letter describing experience and interests in research, teaching, and leadership. 2. A full Curriculum vitae 3. Publication list 4. A summary of the future research, teaching and service plans, and 5. The names/emails of four individuals willing to provide letters of reference. References will only be contacted if you are shortlisted for the position. All applications and materials submitted will be held in strict confidence. The Search Committee will begin reviewing applications on 3 November 2025. For further enquiries, please contact Search Committee Chair, Professor Steve Yim ( steve.yim@ntu.edu.sg ) Please click here to apply.

title: Middle and High School Social Studies Teacher
description: REPORTS TO: Middle & High School Principal JOB GOAL: The successful Social Studies teacher at ICS must have a strong understanding of standards based assessment as well as concept based teaching and learning.  Experience closing the achievement gap in a variety of contexts would be considered an asset as well. They must also develop engaging and innovative lessons that allow our students to grow academically, socially and emotionally. The teacher should be confident in incorporating technology (including emerging technologies such as GenAI) and hands-on learning experiences into the classroom with enthusiasm and also believe in collaboration with other colleagues, promoting understanding and communication in a diverse learning and cultural environment.  While we are a US curriculum school, the teacher should be able to deliver the curriculum through a wider lens of perspectives and cultures. PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: Plan, prepare, and execute appropriate lessons, utilizing appropriate resource materials and activities. Follow the prescribed scope and sequence of curriculum and assessment as scheduled. Employ a variety of instructional aides, methods, and materials that will promote best practices in order to reach the whole child as stated in the ICS mission statement. Assess student learning against the appropriate standards on a regular basis and provide progress reports as required. Identify the needs of students through diagnostic procedures and align instructional objectives to these needs. Effect student learning through mastery of the subject material by utilizing valid teaching techniques to achieve curriculum goals within the framework of the school’s philosophy. Eager to explore and learn about emerging technologies, such as Generative AI, with a commitment to integrating them effectively into the classroom to foster critical thinking and guide students in developing ethical and responsible use. Ensure Biblical Worldwide Integration (BWI) is mapped throughout the curriculum. Complete lesson/unit plans, Atlas, ESO documentation, and PLC expectations within specified time frames. Maintain student records as required by the school. Communicate with parents, students, and other professional staff regarding student progress (behavioral and academic). Follow school policies, procedures, rules, regulations, and guidelines and the provisions of the contract. Maintain proper discipline in the classroom and on the school premises utilizing teacher created discipline plans in conjunction with a school-wide discipline plan. Inform the administration if/when unable to fulfill any duty assigned. Prepare adequate information and materials for a substitute teacher. Plan and lead a student Chapel when called upon. Supervise extracurricular activities, organizations, and outings as assigned. Complete the Christian Philosophy of Education in-service program within the first three years of teaching at ICS. Attend and participate in devotions, teachers’ meetings, evening programs (if involving his or her students or as requested by the administration), and other meetings as assigned. Carry out supervisory duty as assigned (before school, break, lunch, after school). Maintain a classroom in a clean and educationally stimulating environment. Maintain regular and accurate attendance and grade reports that provide comprehensive information for each student’s learning progress. Keep students, parents, and the administration informed of students’ progress or deficiencies, and provide sufficient and timely notice to parents when students fail to meet learning standards. Submit all appropriate reports to the administration, guidance office, and business office as required by school regulations or as requested. Work with the administration in implementing all policies, procedures, and directives governing the operation of the school. Complete professional development programs as required by the administration and/or ICS Board. Maintain teaching credentials as required through sponsoring agencies (state/provincial education department, ACSI, etc.). Acquire and maintain teaching credentials as required by the laws of Singapore, NICS, and/or ICS. Demonstrate the character qualities of enthusiasm, courtesy, flexibility, integrity, kindness, self-control, perseverance, and punctuality. Exemplify strong interpersonal and communication skills. Meet everyday stress with emotional stability, objectivity, and optimism. Develop and maintain rapport with students, parents, and staff by treating others with friendliness, open-mindedness, dignity, and consideration. Respectfully submit to and demonstrate loyalty to constituted authority. Maintain a personal appearance that models cleanliness, modesty, good taste, and is in agreement with school policy. QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor’s Degree Teaching credential from a US state, Canadian province or from ACSI (exceptions will be approved by the Academic Board) Expertise in working with students and teaching Advanced Placement (AP) courses Knowledge of specific subject(s)

Strong communication and presentation skills.

title: Finance Controller (Construction & Manufacturing)
description: About our client: Our client is a Singapore based Design & Architecture company. title: Director, Business Risk Management
description: Job Summary Strategy •    Support in the latest WRB strategy in pivoting to Affluent market and the execution in Retail Product •    Align to WRB and COO strategic agenda in acquiring client at scale economically through Partnerships. Business •    Work with Group Process Owner of the Product Management Process (For Retail Product Management) / Group Head Partnerships (For Partnerships) and relevant Functional Heads to drive the design, implementation, and maintenance of Product Management related Process / Partnership Governance Framework •    Drive business Operational Risk governance by working with Business Heads and Risk Owners to identify and resolve existing operational risks and horizon risks that is unique to retail products and partnerships. •    Take the lead in identifying and driving initiatives designed to increase business operating efficiency and strengthen first line operational risk management in the Product Management and Partnership process. •    Drive or support transformational change initiatives to align with the latest WRB business strategy in pivoting to Affluent and enable business to grow/scale. Processes •    Work with WRB business members to deliver and maintain optimal global design standards for Product Management Process / Partnership Process that are fit for business purpose and growth. •    This includes end-to-end assessment of operational risk, establishing and maintaining KCIs, CSTs and KRIs to manage operational risk, act on breaches, and approve or remediate deviations from global standards raised by the countries. •    Assess the impact of Group Risk Owner policy changes on the process, amend the relevant governance documents and ensure that the same is cascaded to countries and embedded with proper gap analysis and approved dispensations. Key Responsibilities People and Talent •    Lead through example and build the appropriate culture and values.  Set appropriate tone and expectations from their team and work in collaboration with risk and control partners. •    Ensure the provision of ongoing training and development of people, and ensure that holders of all critical functions are suitably skilled and qualified for their roles ensuring that they have effective supervision in place to mitigate any risks. Risk Management •    Provide business input and oversight of the development of end-to-end scalable infrastructure (where appropriate) and processes to execute business processes pertaining to Product Management and Partnerships.  This includes establishing and maintaining training, advice and support, to assure the processes, policies and standards are effectively implemented in the business. •    Reinforce an effective and exemplary risk and control culture and further strengthen the control environment. •    Act to minimize operational loss and audit failures and take proactive measures to respond to matters arising and identify and manage forward looking risks. •    Overall responsible for the implementation of ERMF/ORF for the process. •    Simplify processes and controls. Governance •    Drive effective governance and oversight of risks arising from Product Management and Partnerships through effective Governance forums and committees, including Retail and SME Banking Product Committee (RS PC) and Partnerships Centre-of-Excellence (Partnerships COE). •    Enhance an integrated governance structure for the robust and efficient governance processes in the business. •    Reinforce an effective and exemplary risk and control culture and further strengthen the control environment. •    Support countries on issues and risk resolutions arising from BAU activities, audits, risk reviews etc within the processes. •    Responsible to ensure that key stakeholders and governance forums are kept up to date on key issues and resolution plans and overall progress on initiatives. Regulatory and Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key stakeholders •    WRBMT and business (mainly Group Head of CCPL, Group Head Partnerships and respective Group Heads of Products) •    WRB COO Top Team •    Key functional Heads o    Head, CFCC WRB o    Head of Legal WRB o    Head, OTCR, WRB o    Head, ESGR, WRB •    Country WRB Heads, and respective partnerships / retail product business heads Other Responsibilities •    Embed Here for good and Group’s brand and values in WRB COO team •    Perform other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures. Our Ideal Candidate •	10+ years relevant risk management experience and/or business experience in Wealth and Retail Banking. •	Demonstratable prior experience in risk identification and control design will be a key advantage. •	Knowledge of the Banks Enterprise Risk Management Framework, Policy and Standards is a plus. •	Familiarity with Product Management and Partnership Business. •	Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal. •	Strong interpersonal, relationship building and stakeholder management skills. •	Ability to think strategically as well as be able to execute. •	Collaborative and creative problem solver, while being outcome focused. •	Ability to make decisions in an unstructured environment and to work under pressure to meet tight deadlines. •	Ability to multitask and work independently in a fast-paced environment Role Specific Technical Competencies •	Knowledge of Non-Financial Risk Management (ERMF, Risk and Control Self-Assessment) •	Knowledge of Retail Business and products, including Partnership business. •	Knowledge of Product Management •	Experience of running any Committees or Forums would be a plus About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. title: BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP MANAGER
description:
BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP MANAGER We are seeking a Business Relationship Manager to manage our corporate customer portfolio. The incumbent will report to the Joint General Manager in charge of business and will be responsible for marketing, business development, relationship management and risk management for new and existing customers in the Asia-Pacific region. Applicants should possess a degree in Economics, Law, Accounting and Finance or Business Administration, with about 10 years' experience in corporate banking. Knowledge in specialised lending such as project finance, object finance and commodity finance would be an advantage. Familiarity with and a good understanding of the business culture and processes of Japanese organizations is required. Due to regular communication with the Head Office, a high level of proficiency in both spoken and written Japanese is essential. Strong analytical, communication and interpersonal skills are necessary. Overseas travel may be required. Remuneration will commensurate with qualifications and experience. title: GC13374551 - Senior Treasury Manager (Shipping MNC)
description: The Company Join a Global Leader in the marine industry, headquartered in Singapore with global presence. Due to evolving business needs, we are actively recruiting for a Senior Treasury Manager to be based in the Singapore office. The Role Reporting to the CFO, you will be responsible for end-to-end liquidity management, financial modelling, and primary bank engagement for the Group. The role integrates treasury execution with forward-looking capital planning to ensure strong cash visibility, disciplined funding decisions, and a single, credible financial narrative with lenders and stakeholders. This role sits at the intersection of cash management, forecasting, modelling, and banker communication, supporting the CFO on funding strategy, refinancing, and covenant management. The key responsibilities include daily and medium-term liquidity management across the Group. Develop and maintain integrated financial models (cash flow, funding, covenant, stress scenarios). Support refinancing, restructuring, and funding proposals through robust modelling. Ensure modelling assumptions are aligned with actual cash behaviour and treasury realities. Act as the primary point of contact for relationship banks and lenders. Prepare and present cash flow forecasts, funding updates, and covenant reporting to banks. Support negotiations on facilities, terms, waivers, and renewals in collaboration with the CFO. Coordinate bank information requests, ensuring consistency and credibility of data shared. Monitor compliance with financial covenants and reporting obligations. Prepare covenant calculations, headroom analysis, and early-warning indicators. Proactively flag risks and recommend mitigating actions to the CFO. Strengthen treasury governance, controls, and reporting discipline. Drive automation and process improvements (e.g. ERP / Treasury systems) to reduce manual effort. Ensure clear audit trails and documentation for treasury and funding decisions. Partner with Finance, FP&A, and Accounting teams to align forecasts and assumptions. Support the CFO with materials for Board, lenders, and internal steering committees. Contribute to capital structure strategy and long-term funding planning discussions. Your Profile To qualify for this role, you must have a Bachelor Degree in Accountancy with minimum 10 years of relevant experience in a MNC with experience in treasury, corporate finance and capital planning. This role requires hands-on experience in financial modelling with practical cash-flow orientation. We are looking for a meticulous, analytical, technically competent Treasury professional with solid understanding of debt instruments, covenants and liquidity management. RESPONSIBILITIES Strategy •    Define and own the Global Markets Operations continuous improvement and small change strategy. •    Establish standardized methodologies (e.g., Lean, Agile, Kaizen) for incremental improvement delivery. •    Prioritize initiatives based on business impact, risk reduction, and feasibility. •    Embed a culture of continuous improvement across Operations. Business •    Design, implement, and own the Global Markets Operations performance management framework. •    Establish consistent KPIs, metrics, and scorecards across trading, post-trade, clearing, collateral, client, and regulatory operations. •    Ensure alignment between operational performance metrics, business strategy, and risk appetite. Processes •    Lead the end-to-end delivery of small change initiatives, including process enhancements, system configuration changes, automation, and control improvements. •    Partner with Technology and Operations teams to deliver changes outside of large transformation programs. •    Ensure rapid, low-risk delivery with clear ownership, governance, and controls. •    Track benefits realization and sustainability of delivered changes. •    Identify process inefficiencies, waste, rework, and control gaps across Global Markets Operations. •    Drive simplification, standardization, and automation of operational processes. •    Improve straight-through processing (STP), reduce manual interventions, and lower operational risk. •    Deliver targeted improvements to address operational risk, audit issues, and regulatory findings. •    Enhance preventative and detective controls through process and system improvements. •    Partner with Business Risk Management to ensure risk considerations are embedded in change delivery. People & Talent •    Lead, develop, and retain a global regulatory operations organization. •    Build a strong leadership bench and succession pipeline. •    Foster a culture of accountability, control ownership, and regulatory excellence. •    Champion diversity, equity, and inclusion. Risk Management •    Establish governance forums to review performance, cost, and capacity outcomes. •    Partner with Operations, Finance, Technology, HR, and Risk to align performance objectives. •    Represent Operations Business Performance Management in senior leadership and strategy forums. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    Lead and develop a global Business Performance Management team. •    Build strong analytical, financial, and data capabilities within the organization. •    Foster a culture of ownership, transparency, and continuous improvement. •    Champion diversity, equity, and inclusion. Our Ideal Candidate •	Education - Bachelor’s degree in finance, business, economics, or a related discipline required. •	Advanced degree (MBA or equivalent) preferred. •	15+ years of experience in Operation optimization in Global Markets operations Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Strong financial acumen and analytical capability. •    Expertise in performance measurement, management information, and data analytics. •    Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights. •    Experience supporting large-scale operational transformation •    Demonstrated ability to engage effectively with senior management and regulators. •    Strong judgment and ability to provide constructive, credible challenge. About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential.
