title: Assistant Manager, Finance
description: COMPANY DESCRIPTION The Salvation Army has been touching lives in Singapore since 1935 and we remain committed in serving the underprivileged in the community without discrimination. To us, every life is precious and we actively help those in need regardless of age, race or religion. Throughout the years, we have established a comprehensive network of social services to meet a wide range of needs in our community. Our diverse programmes reach out to children who need care and protection, families in material need, ailing aged requiring quality nursing care and other needy segments in our society. Our spiritual ministry also reaches out to the people in the community to provide counsel to those in need. We are there to help guide those in need towards finding their identity and purpose in life. RESPONSIBILITIES ACCOUNTABILITY You will be accountable to the Manager, Finance. In the absence, you will be accountable to the Head, Finance. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES Financial Management and Reporting Responsible for assisting in budgeting, financial planning including cash flow management and analysis of the financial performance of the group. Ensure accurate and timely completion in all areas of financial and management accounting activities including but not limited to monthly, year-end closing of accounts, audited financial statements and accounts schedules. Assist in preparation of financial reports and necessary materials for presentation, as needed. Stakeholder engagements Establish collaborative business partnering relationships with various business units and lead the team in improving existing policies and processes and transform it through the active adoption of digital tools for better efficiency and costs savings. Finance operations, systems and processes streamlining Assist to provide supervision over the Finance operations, to review and ensure its quality and accuracy. Develop and implement processes and procedures to enhance workflows of the department to drive continuous productivity improvement on accuracy, efficiency and simplicity. Support and contribute to systems implementation projects and its change management tasks. Support new and on-going projects to enhance Finance process and controls. Ensure compliance with organisation policies, boards, councils' decisions, and government statutory legislations. Others To carry out any other duties as assigned by Management from time to time. QUALIFICATIONS Degree in Accountancy with ACCA/CPA or other professional accounting qualification 5 - 8 years of relevant experience, with at least 3 years in a supervisory role Preferably with knowledge of charity and/or non-profit finance experience Proficiency in Oracle Fusion, MS Office applications (Advanced Excel skills) and data visualisation tools will be advantageous Good internal and external stakeholder management skills Possess strong sense of responsibility, initiative with good interpersonal and communication skills Positive attitude with a strong desire to learn Resilient and confident in management of ambiguity Fast learner who can work independently and as a team Good organisational skills with the ability to multi-task and prioritise projects/task OTHER INFORMATION DUTY HOURS The total work hours is 44 hours per week. Under normal circumstances the hours of duty will be as follows: 8.30am to 5pm - Monday to Friday (1 hour lunch break) It will be at the discretion of your Centre/Department in charge or his/her assigned representatives to determine whether you will be required to work any of the unutilised hours according to the operational needs of the Centre/Department and within the legitimacy of the employment laws where applicable. Working Location Singapore Regional Headquarters - Finance Department 20 Bishan Street 22, Singapore, 579768 By submitting this job application, I affirm that I have given to The Salvation Army true and complete information on this application. No requested information has been concealed. I agree that The Salvation Army (TSA) may collect, use and disclose your personal data, as provided in this application form, or (if applicable) obtained by TSA as a result of your employment, for the following purposes in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 and our data protection policy (available at our website at https://www.salvationarmy.org.sg/data-protection-policy/ ): a) the processing of this employment application; b) the administration of the employment with TSA; and c) sharing of personal information to third party, including but not limited to government agencies, hospitals, vendor(s)/supplier (s), working in collaboration with TSA to support the employment. If you are providing third party's personal data, you hereby declare that you have obtained consent from the named individual(s), for the collection, use and disclosure of his/her personal data by you to TSA, TSA business partners and other third-party service providers. I authorised the organisation to contact references provided for employment reference checks. Please visit our website at https://www.salvationarmy.org.sg/data-protection-policy/ for further details on our data protection policy, including how you may access and correct your personal data or withdraw consent to the collection, use or disclosure of your personal data. If any information I have provided is untrue, or if I have concealed material information, I understand that this will constitute cause for denial of employment or immediate dismissal. Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - The Salvation Army Singapore

title: BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT MANAGER
description: The trainee will be guided directly by the Director and will be exposed to day-to-day business development operations, partnership coordination, outlet performance support, and compliance matters relevant to nightlife and Izakaya-style establishments. Specific Responsibilities: 1. Assist the Director in identifying and evaluating new business opportunities related to nightlife and Izakaya operations. 2. Conduct market observation on customer preferences, entertainment trends, and competitor activities within the nightlife industry. 3. Support the development of promotional concepts, themed events, and collaboration ideas suitable for club and Izakaya settings. 4. Coordinate with external vendors, performers, and service providers for events, entertainment programs, and outlet activities. 5. Prepare business proposals, concept briefs, and presentation materials for management review. 6. Monitor outlet operations from a business development perspective to identify areas for improvement or expansion. 7. Assist in planning outlet expansion, renovation concepts, or new entertainment offerings. 8. Support the Director in managing partnerships with alcohol suppliers, food partners, and entertainment providers. 9. Ensure business activities align with licensing requirements, entertainment regulations, and operational guidelines. 10. Compile reports on business initiatives, industry developments, and outcomes for internal discussion. 11. Participate in management meetings to provide input on operational improvements and customer engagement strategies. Technical Skills and Competencies: 1. Strong understanding of nightlife, entertainment, and Izakaya-style business operations. 2. Ability to analyze market trends and customer behavior within hospitality and entertainment environments. 3. Competency in preparing business proposals, concept notes, and operational reports. 4. Knowledge of basic licensing, compliance, and regulatory requirements for nightlife establishments. 5. Good coordination and communication skills for working with vendors and external partners. 6. Ability to plan and organize entertainment activities and promotional initiatives. 7. Practical problem-solving skills applied to real operational challenges. 8. Proficiency in basic office software for documentation, reporting, and presentations. 9. Ability to follow management direction closely and execute tasks according to business needs. Duration of Traineeship/Attachment Offered - 6 months Mature Mid Career Individuals - $3,300 This Position is open for mid career individuals (Mature, 40 years and above). Mid Career individuals from any qualifications level can apply.

title: Account Executive, Startups (Greater China)
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team The Startups team is dedicated to winning all top and up and coming startups in Asia. We achieve this by combining subject matter expertise with robust collaboration within the startup ecosystem. Our focus is on identifying and partnering with high-potential startups, assisting them in monetizing their products, scaling globally, and creating new revenue streams. You have the opportunity to sell the entire suite of Stripe products and run the full sales cycle through to onboarding and revenue realization. What you’ll do In this role you will create partnerships between Stripe and founders of the most innovative and fastest-growing startups in the Greater China Region by helping them to understand how Stripe’s financial infrastructure and solutions can support their growth ambitions and create a competitive advantage for their businesses. As an Account Executive on the Startups team, you’ll identify new opportunities for startups to get the most out of Stripe’s growing products suite by thoroughly understanding their needs and advising them on how to leverage market-leading financial solutions to their own advantage. You will create and drive campaigns to build new sales pipelines and work with our users on increasing their Stripe adoption. Responsibilities Identify high-potential prospective startups from inbound leads and outbound prospecting Own, develop and implement full sales cycle strategies for driving product adoption and net new product sales for startups Represent Stripe in the local startup ecosystem and and help us grow our footprint further Work together with the investment partnership team on portfolio analyses and founder-focused gatherings Work with the other got-to-market teams on shared initiatives and represent the Startups team at regional events Solve complex client needs and work across product, sales, risk, and operations teams to improve our product Generate your own leads through cold calling, blitzing, research, networking, and driving your territory Make every potential Stripe user happy with every interaction, regardless of deal size Identify and understand users’ pain points to propose Stripe solutions Make sure that internal teams are set up for users’ success by coordinating with other Stripe teams such as Solution architects, SDRs, Marketing, Product and many more Who you are We’re looking for a well-rounded strategic seller who can build strong relationships with Stripe’s users and manage high velocity deal cycles. Our startup users are some of the most ambitious and demanding customers and a successful candidate will match their curiosity, speed and passion to build things by overcoming challenges. You are solution-oriented, adaptable, have a keen interest in further developing yourself and know how to get things done. You enjoy a highly-technical sales cycle and working with a diverse group of internal stakeholders. We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. Minimum requirements 3+ years experience in a customer facing, revenue related role, preferably at a high-growth SaaS company, bank, consultancy or venture capital firm, with a track record of top performance, or alternatively a background in product Track record of overachieving against ambitious sales targets Expertise in building territory plans to identify high payoff activities and initiatives in your market Ability to understand different business models and build great relationships with highly technical founders. Ability to understand complex technical ecosystems and build great relationships with technical customers Analytical ability to manage complex sales cycles Strong analytical and quantitative capabilities including comfort with basic modeling. Ability to operate in a highly ambiguous and fast-paced environment. Experience managing projects with input from cross-functional team: A knack for working well with a wide range of stakeholders, both internally and externally Passion for the  startup ecosystem. Superior verbal and written communication skills in both English & Mandarin is required for this role, as it involves working primarily with companies from the Greater China region Preferred requirements Prior experience in the startup ecosystem, either as founder or operator in a fast-scaling company Knowledge and understanding of existing and developing payments technologies

title: Digital Fundraising Executive
description: Company Overview Glyph Community is a Singapore-registered IPC charity dedicated to supporting children from lower-income or vulnerable households, aged 5 to 12, through holistic weekend enrichment programs. Beyond fostering social, emotional, and intellectual growth through structured activities like arts, STEM, life skills, and wellness initiatives, Glyph Community now also focuses on uplifting the living standards of the families we serve. Role Overview We are hiring a Digital Fundraising Executive (Contract) to grow our online fundraising and donor engagement efforts starting January 2026. You will plan and manage digital campaigns, apply for fundraising grants, and support the charity in meeting its fundraising goals. Roles and Responsibilities Plan and run digital fundraising campaigns on social media, crowdfunding sites, and email independently Write and design fundraising content to attract and retain donors Apply for relevant fundraising grants and assist in donor reporting Track and report campaign and donor data using Excel or other tools Support other fundraising or administrative tasks as assigned Key Performance Indicators Growth in total online donations and number of active donors Number of campaigns and grant applications completed on time Accuracy and timeliness of reporting and documentation Exact metrics to be aligned on if shortlisted for interview Requirements Diploma or Degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a related field Non-degree holders with relevant fundraising experience and demonstrated proficiencies will be considered 1 to 3 years of experience in digital marketing, fundraising and/or content creation Strong  writing and communication skills Proficient in design tools (Adobe PS / AI, Canva, Davinci Resolve, Capcut), social media tools (meta business suite), Microsoft Office (Words, Excel, PowerPoint) and CRM (HubSpot). Organised, creative, and passionate about helping children and families Application Process As part of our selection process, shortlisted applicants are required to participate in a case study to assess their suitability for the role.

title: BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT MANAGER
description: ey Responsibilities Develop and lead business development strategies for international sourcing and import operations Identify, evaluate, and onboard overseas manufacturers and exporters to support business growth Build and manage long-term commercial relationships with suppliers, distributors, and key accounts Lead pricing, contract negotiations, and commercial agreements Conduct market analysis and demand forecasting to guide sourcing and expansion decisions Coordinate with internal teams on logistics planning, import documentation, customs clearance, and regulatory compliance Ensure compliance with Singapore import regulations and food trade requirements Prepare business plans, revenue forecasts, and management reports Represent the company in strategic meetings, negotiations, and trade discussions Requirements Master’s Degree in Business Administration or any related field. Minimum 1 – 10 years of relevant experience in Business Development, International Trade, or Import & Export Proven experience managing cross-border trade operations and supplier relationships Strong knowledge of FMCG or grocery product supply chains , logistics, and trade documentation Demonstrated ability to negotiate complex commercial agreements independently Bilingual communication skills (English and at least one additional business language) to support coordination with overseas suppliers and partners Strong leadership, stakeholder management, and execution capabilities Willing to travel overseas if required

title: Sr. Capital Category Supplier Manager II
description: As a Sr Capital Category Supplier Manager II, you will be the primary category point of contact for supplier interactions, performance management, and negotiations globally. You will lead in the development and implementation of category strategies and projects related but not limited to cost reduction initiatives, supply base optimization, and pricing/contract negotiation! Category Management & Strategic Sourcing Develop and implement Category Strategy for Capital Equipment Perform Market analysis including supplier landscape, capabilities, and technology roadmaps Participate in supplier segmentation and strategic goal setting to improve value such as high revenue, spend reduction, purchasing efficiency, customer services, and supplier management Recommend resource levels across procurement sourcing categories, projects, and functions Drive sourcing requirements and supplier selection through decision matrix in support of technology development, cost reduction, performance improvement efforts, and sustainability Facilitate co-development activities and collaboration with suppliers Implement processes to manage partner well-being with key suppliers Cost Management & Analysis Identify and evaluate category-specific savings and revenue levers Drive category savings and revenue and scorecard metric targets Provide market intelligence and category price trends to relevant internal partners Use cost modeling techniques to negotiate for assigned categories based on global benchmarks. Negotiation Lead and partner with collaborators in negotiations with suppliers for strategic sourcing events/RFQ process into bundled negotiation Lead or participate in negotiations for contract extensions and renewals, renegotiation of prices, transfers, etc. Incorporate savings targets and contract requirements into negotiations Build and negotiate supplier contracts in assigned categories, monitoring and implementing terms to improve cost performance and drive successful business engagement. Business Partnering Partner with Customers to deliver Strategic Solutions for the assigned categories Develop and maintain expertise in the assigned spending categories through relationships with key partners Interact with Micron and Supplier executives Drive customer-centric approach to solving issues and representing Procurement Minimum Qualifications: Minimum 5-8 years of experience in Procurement and Engineering A bachelor’s degree in engineering business Proficiency in Probe and Test Processes and Capital Procurement Experienced in a Category Management role, preferably in the semiconductor or high-tech industry Demonstrate experience in Strategic Sourcing Strong in Negotiation and Communication skills Preferred Qualifications: Certified Purchasing Manager or CPSN Certification

title: Vice President, Securitization Finance (Job ID: 10074712)
description: The successful candidate will be responsible for supporting and promoting the advancement of our Securitization Finance ("SEC") business operations within the Asia Pacific region (excluding Japan) by focusing on strategic planning, operational readiness, research, and reporting including communicating with the Head Office in Japan. Responsibilities: Strategic Involvement: Assist the SEC team with the origination and execution of deals, collaborating effectively with MUFG branches and relevant divisions. Prepare, maintain, and update various internal reports pertaining to SEC transactions. Conduct Borrower Rating processes and credit analysis for existing and prospective clients. Develop credit applications for potential transactions. Aid in transaction documentation tasks, liaising with internal and external stakeholders as needed. Uphold operational solidness and adherence to internal process for transaction bookings. Assist in the business planning and daily management for AIBD. Financial Management: Support the SEC team in the origination and execution of deals, cooperating closely with respective MUFG divisions to meet the team's financial goals and fulfil Key Performance Indicators. Customer relationship: Cultivate and manage relationships with existing and prospective SEC clientele with a goal to originate mandates for leading debt issuance and arrangement of transactions. Serve as the primary contact for Japanese clients and Tokyo Head office, managing communication, transaction, discussion and mandate-related coordination to support origination and execution of securitization transactions Teamwork: Provide guidance and facilitate the transfer of knowledge to junior team members. Actively liaise with numerous departments and collaborate closely with other regions to foster a cohesive global securitization team. Risk & Control: Ensure transactions align with the Bank's risk profile requirements and perform thorough analysis on transactional risks or related aspects, acquiring necessary credit approvals. Comply with all applicable MAS regulations and MUFG group policies. Job Requirements: Minimum JLPT N1 Certification (or equivalent business-level Japanese proficiency) with demonstrated experience communicating with Japanese stakeholders and senior management, including acting as a key point of contact for securitization finance transactions across Tokyo Head Office and APAC. Minimum 5 years of work experience in solution finance business. Demonstrated experience in banking operations with solid understanding of banking operations complemented by exceptional communication, written and interpersonal skills. Familiarity with legal principles and demonstrable knowledge in documenting securitization finance transactions. Robust business insight with a proven knack for closing deals. Demonstrated collaborative skills for internal and customer-facing communication relating to SEC transactions. Self-starter attitude, cooperative team player while capable of operating independently with a customer-centric approach. We regret to inform that only shortlisted applicants will be notified. Job ID: 10074712

title: Senior Distribution Consultant
description:
To support the business especially our front-office dealers in the area of wholesale cash and vault operation. To assist the Vault Manager in ensuring that the operational
processes are properly executed. To oversee and ensure timely and accurate distribution of value to and from our customer base while adhering to internal and external compliance and regulatory requirements. Ensure proper accountability of all cash movements – including shipments in and out of Singapore Ensure proper reconciliation of Stock Balances and physical store products Work with internal and external parties to address corrections where required and reconcile discrepancies To give efficient support to the business especially our front-desk dealers Deliver excellent customer service to customers Key Duties and Responsibilities A) Cash Operations Ensure customer orders are properly fulfilled through accurate picking, packing and dispatching of foreign & local currency. Ensure MSBs transactions are executed in a timely manner and accurately. Oversee that all cash purchases are properly verified and counted with discrepancy detected validated. Manage and monitor all purchases and excess stock and update the front desk for timely stock clearance. Banknotes Shipments Responsible for arranging local shipment movement and to ensure customer’s expectations are met. Responsible for arranging inbound shipment clearance through authorized security vendor(s) Responsible for securing cargo space for all outgoing shipment through authorized security vendor(s) Ensure properly handing/taking over of banknotes shipment to/from authorized security crew members of respective vendor(s). Ensure incoming banknotes shipments are properly bulk-check before sending for detailed processing by the team. Accurately prepare packing list against deal tickets and ensure all outgoing shipments are properly packed and sealed before releasing to our authorized Security vendor(s). Stock Inventory & Requirements Responsible for the accountability of all cash inventory and end of day balancing. Accuracy/ Daily Balance and Reconciliation Ensure all cash inventory are physically checked and properly reconciled against BLISS reports at the end of each business day. Liaise and confirm all discrepancies reported by Distribution Consultant and working with the dealers till issue is resolved. Archiving Manage the archival of all electronic and physical records as per AML and business requirements. Investigations Investigating problems and retrieving paperwork as required by internal and external customers Security / Operating Procedures Adhere and ensure all staff abide by all security
procedures and Vault SOPs Process improvement & Compliance Ensure compliance with AML requirements and Travelex’s Policies and Procedures Identify where there are problems being incurred as a result of current processes and bring to the attention of management Identify opportunities for process improvement and bring to the attention of management Take ownership for the resolution of problems as reported Communications & Reporting Ensure all daily reports are completed in a timely and accurate manner. All instructions or reporting concerning matters relating to banknotes cash operation must be promptly handled once received / communicated via official email(s). Work Premises Ensure Vault premises and the surrounding areas are
tidied and all cash inventory properly secured and armed before leaving. Security Controls and Premises Assist the Vault Manager in ensuring that all the
security controls such as CCTV recordings and the Chubb monitoring systems are functioning properly. Upkeep and ensure all the facilities in and around the
premises are well maintained and in good working condition. Administration Conduct meetings as and when necessary to discuss and
update relevant business specific communication. To ensure that staff are properly trained and equipped
with the necessary skillset to detect “counterfeits” banknotes. Manage record keeping of banknotes volume and costs of operating. Verify all invoices received. Ensure that staff and customers’ daily movement records are properly managed and maintained. Assume the responsibility of Vault Manager/Team Leader during their absence. B) People Workplace Foster professional relationships with customers and staff members Establish strong relationships with management of internal departments, customers and suppliers/vendors Point of contact for external parties raising issues Contribute to the success of the team. Ensure vault premises properly secured and armed for the day before leaving.

title: Assistant Manager/Manager - Portfolio & Asset Management (Real Estate)
description: This is a newly created position and working very closely with an established senior management team that embrace an excellent work culture of performance. They are a multi-asset firm with real estate as one of their biggest portfolios. With their value-add strategies, you will be looking to be very hands-on in managing their asset enhancement initiatives. Their portfolio will comprise of logistics, living and healthcare. You will be responsible for a wide scope of work including portfolio management, asset management, lease, project and development management. This will include formulating and executing overall fund strategy, including overseeing asset allocation and asset management plans. You will oversee, prepare and manage annual asset plan / budgets and financial reports, with the assistance of the asset management and finance teams. You will recommend asset enhancement, redevelopment, divestment opportunities and managing the sale process. You will participate in any potential development/ investment transaction process. You will be managing and work closely with the appointed property agents to ensure the assets are well-managed. You will liaise with various stakeholders in the conceptualization, formulation and implementation of asset enhancement. You will be supporting the investment team in property acquisitions, fund raising, fund management, general corporate finance work too. You will be responsible for carrying out financial modelling and assist in feasibility studies to support projects identified for major real estate asset enhancements, redevelopment and transformation. You will assist in analysing, planning of real estate assets within the portfolio to determine its highest and best use. As part of the Lease Management, you will maintain good tenant relationships and oversee in lease negotiations to ensure high occupancy rate including setting leasing strategy, re-gearing leases and negotiate surrender/re-letting opportunities. You will be responsible for driving the global leasing of properties within the portfolio, including attending to securing tenants. You will be analysing and reviewing rental, renewal options and prepositions as well as negotiation. This will include reviewing and recommending lease terms, negotiating with prospective tenants to achieve desired rent and occupancy. You will also be involved in Project and Development Management such as managing the budget, resources, scheduling etc. You will oversee site entitlement and due diligence effort for new construction and re-modelling projects across portfolio of real estate. You will participate in project scoping and budgeting as well as managing all aspects of the due diligence process including but not limited to feasibility studies, geotechnical evaluations, environmental assessments, and surveys. Requirements: Degree in Finance, Real Estate, or equivalent. Preferably at least 5 years’ experience in similar capacity either Portfolio or Asset Management or Development management Strong financial skills, analytical mind, and creative thinking Excellent verbal and written communication skills Fast learner, ability to work independently, multi-task, and execute efficiently in a fast-paced dynamic environment All applications will be treated in the strictest confidence. Personal data provided will be used for recruitment purposes only. Please apply with your CV in word document format. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. For other positions related to real estate, please go to https://careers.tri-cap.com.sg/. EA Licence: 24S2282 EA Personnel: R1109270

title: Manager, Business & Program Development
description:
The Manager, Business & Program Development, will provide strategic leadership to enhance the
organization’s overall growth.  This role involves spearheading business development efforts, creating and executing impactful programs, and fostering collaboration to drive revenue and achieve organizational objectives. Additionally, the Manager will lead the business development and program team in diversity and SMART MedTech, and Sustainability Initiatives in Southeast Asia to raise awareness and attract talents for the industry. This is a full-time position and reports directly to the Director, Business & Program
Development at SEMI SEA Office. THE IDEAL CANDIDATE: The ideal candidate can communicate internally and externally to meet the company’s overall target goal. The candidate is self-driven and collaborates closely with other senior leaders to drive the organization’s overall success. CORE RESPONSIBILITIES: · Increase programs relevancy and value to members – Develop new content for programs. Work closely with program committees, as they set program objectives and develop content. Assist in preparing meetings and project plans to meet program needs. Ensure quality of speakers and presentation materials. Create and develop new digital programs –increase values to members. Provide strategies for SEMI Southeast Asia – Play a vital role in contributing to strategizing for the expansion of the SEA region market, encompassing budget management and performing ROI analysis.  Take the lead in Key Account Management to ensure sustained growth and success. Manage, develop and execute the business growth and Go-to-market strategies for new service. Conduct and execute business plans for new SEMI products - services and platforms to support SEMI’s larger span of the global electronics manufacturing supply chain in areas such as semiconductor equipment
and materials, semiconductor manufacturing, automotive electronics, medical electronics, flexible and hybrid electronics, MEMS, Advanced Packaging (+ other IoT markets). Develop strategic partnerships (industrial, Public, Associations)
for new segments (horizontally and vertically). Assess and prioritize SEMI’s targeted engagement in vertical and horizontal/adjacent supply chain segments. Strategic Planning – Contribute to the development of organizational strategies to drive sustainable growth. WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE: Successful implementation of impactful programs. Work closely with senior management to align business and program
development initiatives with overall company objectives. Identify new business opportunities and partnerships to expand market
presence. CRITICAL SKILLS: Working with regional offices and partners to identify target client markets and developing outreach strategies. Ability to create and communicate a long-term vision while balancing short term goals Provide effective leadership to the business and program development teams. Demonstrate sound judgement in evaluating creative ideas and suggestions and adeptly manage the processes with effectiveness and efficiency. PREFERRED KNOWLEDGE OR PRACTICAL EXPERIENCE: Education - B.S./B.A. degree in semiconductor or related field or an equivalence in working experience. Minimum 5 years of experience in business development or related roles. Knowledge of the semiconductor industry is mandatory with good contact points in E&E ecosystem. Strong collaboration and communication skills as demonstrated by ability to work cross-functionally with internal stakeholders. Ability to collaborate effectively and articulate clear organizational vision. ABOUT SEMI SEMI is the global industry association representing the electronics manufacturing supply chain, connecting over 3,000 member companies and 1.3 million professionals worldwide. SEMI members are responsible for the innovations in materials, design, equipment, software, devices, and services that enable smarter, faster, more powerful, and more affordable electronic products. Since 1970, SEMI has built connections that have helped its members prosper, create new markets, and address common industry challenges together.

title: Claims Assessor
description: about the company Our client is a prominent insurer in Singapore with a strong focus on health and life protection. You will ensure that each case is handled according to policy terms while maintaining consistent service standards. title: Director of Finance
description: JOB SUMMARY Functions as the property’s strategic financial business leader. The position champions, develops and implements property-wide strategies that deliver products and services to meet or exceed the needs and expectations of the brand’s target customer and property employees. The position provides the financial expertise to enable the successful implementation of the brand service strategy and brand initiatives while maximizing the return on investment. In addition, creates and executes a business plan that is aligned with the property and brand’s business strategy and focuses on the execution of financial activities and the delivery of desirable financial results. CANDIDATE PROFILE Education and Experience • 4-year bachelor's degree in Finance and Accounting or related major; 3 years experience in the hotel finance and accounting industry. OR • Master's degree in Finance and Accounting or related major; 1 year experience in the hotel finance and accounting industry. CORE WORK ACTIVITIES Engaging in Strategic Planning and Decision Making • Develops means to improve profit, including estimating cost and benefit, exploring new business opportunities, etc. • Analyzes information, forecasts sales against expenses and creates annual budget plans. • Compiles information, analyzes and monitors actual sales against projected sales. • Analyzes differences between actual budget wages and forecasted wages for more efficient budget planning. • Identifies the underlying principles, reasons, or facts of information by breaking down information or data into separate parts. • Thinks creatively and practically to develop, execute and implement new business plans • Creates the annual operating budget for the property. • Provides analytical support during budget reviews to identify cost saving and productivity opportunities for property managers. • Implements a system of appropriate controls to manage business risks. • Ensures a strong accounting and operational control environment to safeguard assets, improve operations and profitability. • Analyzes financial data and market trends. • Leads the development and implementation of a comprehensive annual business plan which is aligned with the company’s and brand’s strategic direction. • Provides on going analytical support by monitoring the operating department’s actual and projected sales. • Produces accurate forecasts that enable operations to react to changes in the business. Leading Finance Teams • Utilizes interpersonal and communication skills to lead, influence, and encourage others; advocates sound financial/business decision making; demonstrates honesty/integrity; leads by example. • Communicates the strategic goals, the focus and the owner priorities to subordinates in a clear and precise manner. • Leverages strong functional leadership and communication skills to influence the executive team, the property's strategies and to lead own team. • Oversees internal, external and regulatory audit processes. • Provides excellent leadership by assigning team members and other departments managers' clear accountability backed by appropriate authority. • Conducts annual performance appraisals with direct reports according to standard operating procedures. Anticipating and Delivering on the Needs of Key Stakeholders • Attends meetings and communicating with the owners, understanding the priorities and strategic focus. • Understands and meets the needs of key stakeholders (owners, corporate, guests, etc.). • Advises the GM and executive committee on existing and evolving operating/financial issues. • Communicates financial concepts in a clear and persuasive manner that is easy to understand and drives desired behaviors. • Demonstrates an understanding of cash flow and owner priorities. • Manages communication with owners in an effective manner. • Manages property working capital and cash flow in accordance with brand standard operating procedures and owner requirements. • Facilitates critique meetings to review information with management team. Developing and Maintaining Finance Goals • Ensures Profits and Losses are documented accurately. • Monitors all taxes that apply, ensuring that taxes are current, collected and/or accrued. • Submits reports in a timely manner, ensuring delivery deadlines. • Develops and supports achievement of performance goals, budget goals, team goals, etc. • Improves profit growth in operating departments. • Reviews audit issues to ensure accuracy. • Monitor the purchasing process as applicable. Managing Projects and Policies • Generates and provides accurate and timely results in the form of reports, presentations, etc. • Reconciles balance sheet to ensure account balances are supported by appropriate documentation in accordance with standard operating procedures. • Ensures that the P&L is accurate (e.g., costs are properly matched to revenue, costs are recorded in the proper accounts). • Ensures compliance with management contract and reporting requirements. • Ensures compliance with standard and local operating procedures. • Ensures compliance with standard operating procedures. Managing and Conducting Human Resource Activities • Ensures team members are cross-trained to support successful daily operations. • Ensures property policies are administered fairly and consistently. • Ensures new hires participate in the department’s orientation program. • Ensures new hires receive the appropriate new hire training to successfully perform their job. • Creates appropriate development plans which develop team members based on their individual strengths, development needs, career aspirations and abilities. • Conduct performance review process for employees. • Participates in hiring activities as appropriate.

title: Solutions Architect – Transaction Banking (Contract)
description: Solutions Architect – Transaction Banking (Contract) Location: Singapore Contract Type: 12-months Contract (extendable & convertible) Keywords: Solutions Architecture, Transaction Banking, Payments, Liquidity Management, Trade Finance, Enterprise Architecture, SDLC, Agile, Financial Services Technology Company / Role Overview This role sits within a global technology architecture function supporting a large-scale corporate and transaction banking platform within a leading financial services organisation. The team provides strategic technology direction to ensure alignment between business objectives, enterprise architecture standards, and long-term platform roadmaps. Operating across multiple regions, the role offers exposure to a diverse portfolio of initiatives across payments and transaction services, working closely with both business and technology stakeholders in a fast-paced, globally distributed environment. Key Responsibilities Define and own end-to-end solution architectures that align business objectives with enterprise architecture principles, technology standards, and approved platform strategies. Translate business requirements into clear architectural intent, including system landscapes, integrations, interfaces, and operating environments, while embedding non-functional requirements. Partner closely with business leaders, product owners, engineering teams, and external vendors to ensure solutions are fit for purpose and scalable. Rapidly shape high-level architectures that evolve as requirements mature, ensuring designs remain modular, flexible, and future-proof. Support delivery teams by clarifying architectural decisions, resolving design constraints, and proposing solution options during implementation. Conduct architecture and design reviews to ensure solutions meet standards for security, performance, scalability, reliability, maintainability, and usability. Work with product leadership to plan and prioritise architecture runway and technology-focused backlog items. Promote shared services, reuse, automation, and a culture of continuous improvement across products, processes, and tools. Job Requirements Core must-haves: 10+ years of experience within banking or financial services technology environments. Strong hands-on experience in solution and/or enterprise architecture roles. Proven track record delivering solutions across the full SDLC, including Agile and Waterfall methodologies. Broad technical expertise across financial applications, middleware, databases, and distributed systems. Ability to interpret complex business needs and translate them into clear, actionable architectural designs. Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present architectural concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical thinking capabilities. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related discipline (or equivalent professional experience). Nice-to-have: Domain knowledge in one or more transaction banking areas such as payments, liquidity management, receivables, trade finance, or commercial cards. Experience driving shared services strategies, automation initiatives, and common solution frameworks. Exposure to performance-driven delivery environments with a strong continuous improvement mindset. If this role aligns with your experience and career goals, please send your application to AviralBhargava@argyllscott.sg .

title: General Manager, Pan-Asia Strategy
description:
J OB PURPOSE This position is responsible for formulating and promoting management strategies in NX based on marketing across the entire Asia region, covering both East Asia and Southeast Asia. In particular, the primary mission is to accurately capture the opportunities arising from the “China Plus One” shift in
supply chains, driven by geopolitical risks and the slowdown of the Chinese economy, and to link these opportunities to the growth strategy of NX Group. In formulating and promoting management strategies, a deep understanding of our core business in logistics is essential, particularly experience in strategy development for the forwarding business, which is a
network-based operation, as well as extensive knowledge of its operational processes. In addition,
experience in marketing and analysis, as well as knowledge related to the target key industries—particularly products, manufacturers, and sales companies in the semiconductor, technology, and mobility sectors—are also essential. In promoting these initiatives, it is essential to lead and coordinate among the countries in the East Asia region, including China, Taiwan, Hong Kong, and South Korea, as well as the ASEAN nations, our Group
headquarters, and Japan, which is our mother market. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Strategy Formulation & Deployment Formulate medium-term and annual plans for the Sales, Air Freight, Ocean Freight, and Logistics business based on the overarching Pan-Asia Strategy policy, create execution roadmaps and support to set targets. Progress Management of Various Initiatives and PDCA Progress management for sales projects across the Asia Region including China, Asean, and Japan. Strengthening the air forwarding business in the Pan-Asia region and managing the progress of various initiatives related to volume expansion. Strengthening the ocean forwarding business in the Pan-Asia region and managing the progress of various initiatives related to volume expansion. Progress management of initiatives for platform development and business expansion based on industrial expertise related to warehouse and distribution operations. Administrative duties related to the Pan-Asia strategy, etc. Organization and management of meetings for strategy execution, progress monitoring and reporting to senior management. Planning for talent deployment and personnel exchanges to leverage talent in the Asia region. Planning and management related to the formalization and sharing of business knowledge. Marketing, research, and analysis related to logistics businesses. Promoting communication with relevant regions including India, Americas, and Europe and planning projects to advance the Pan-Asian strategy QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE Bachelor’s degree in business administration or relevant field. Minimum 20 years’ experience in freight forwarding / multimodal sales or account management. Experience in regional or global marketing and sales promotion. Demonstrable experience in developing strategic and business plans. Thorough knowledge of market changes and forces that influence the company. Ability to communicate in Japanese language with Japanese account clients and Japan Headquarters. ATTRIBUTES OF THE CANDIDATE Excellent organizational and leadership skills. Excellent communication, interpersonal and presentation skills. Outstanding analytical and problem-solving abilities. Strong verbal and written communication skills. Strong interpersonal, supervisory, team player and customer service skills required. Possess attributes of respectfulness, honesty, integrity and flexibility. Ability to multi-task, work under pressure and meet deadlines required.

title: Trustee & Custody Operations Manager
description: About CapBridge CapBridge, a member of FOMO Group, is a leading digital investment platform headquartered in Singapore. As a Capital Markets Services licensee, CapBridge is regulated by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) to deal in capital markets products, including securities and collective investment schemes, and to provide custodial services. It is also an exempt financial adviser licensed to issue or promulgate analyses and reports on investment products. CapBridge enables HNWIs and institutional clients to invest in traditional and digital assets via its one-stop digital investment platform, providing highly curated, top-quality, and institutional-grade opportunities to meet clients' diverse asset allocation needs. Through CapBridge's associated company, FOMO Pay Pte Ltd, a regulated MAS Payment Services Act (PSA) Digital Payment Token (DPT) entity, qualified clients can also invest into CapBridge curated products using DPTs, providing a seamless bridge between Traditional Finance (TradFi) and Web3 Finance. About the Role! As the cornerstone of our fiduciary framework, you will spearhead the end-to-end safeguarding of cash, securities, and digital assets. You will provide critical oversight for SPV and trust structures, ensuring every transaction and trustee decision is defensible, compliant, and audit-ready. This role is designed for a seasoned operations leader who can navigate complex MAS regulatory landscapes (SFA, FAA, Trust Companies Act) while driving the evolution of our custody controls and digital asset integration. Key Responsibilities: ● Custody & Asset Safeguarding: Own the end-to-end lifecycle for traditional and digital assets, overseeing reconciliations, corporate actions, and the establishment of robust segregation frameworks. ● Fiduciary Oversight: Lead trustee administration for SPVs and trusts, interpreting complex deeds to monitor issuer obligations, covenants, and formal trustee actions. ● Regulatory & Risk Governance: Act as the first-line owner for MAS compliance (SFA, FAA, TCA); partner with Compliance on inspections and maintain rigorous incident remediation plans. ● Onboarding & AML Oversight: Direct the due diligence and KYC/KYB assessments for issuers and intermediaries, ensuring all counterparties align with internal risk appetite. ● Reporting & Audit Readiness: Serve as the primary contact for auditors and regulators, ensuring all records are inspection-ready and presenting periodic governance reports to senior management. ● Strategic Transformation: Lead workflow automation and UAT for new systems, translating regulatory requirements into scalable operational designs for new asset classes. Requirements: ● Experience: 8–10+ years in Custody Operations, Trustee Administration, or Asset Servicing. ● Regulatory Expertise: Deep knowledge of MAS frameworks (SFA, FAA, Trust Companies Act, and relevant Notices). ● Technical Breadth: Proven experience with SPV structures and capital markets; exposure to Digital Assets or Tokenized Securities is a strong advantage. ● Operational Mindset: Demonstrated ability to act as an independent control owner with a high degree of "audit-ready" documentation discipline. ● Leadership: Experience coaching junior team members and managing complex stakeholder relationships across Legal, Tech, and Senior Management. ● Industry Background: Prior experience in regulated FinTech, Trust Companies, or Fund Administration is highly preferred. Our Perks and Benefits: ● Competitive Compensation and Benefit Package ● Group Hospitalisation and Medical Insurance ● Fun and Dynamic Environment with plenty of learning and growth opportunities ● Career Advancement ● Professional Development Training Workshops ● Team Building Sessions

title: Talent & Learning Manager (Fixed Term Contract)
description: The Opportunity… At Moët Hennessy, We Don't Just Create Exceptional Experiences – We Live Them. For generations, our 27 Maisons, including Dom Pérignon, Veuve Clicquot, and Hennessy, have defined luxury. Now, it's your turn. Imagine a career fueled by passion and creativity, where you redefine what's possible. We seek individuals who thrive in collaborative environments, sharing our love for craftsmanship, elegance, and integrity. We celebrate diversity and the pursuit of excellence. Ready to drive learning and talent innovation at a global luxury brand? As Talent and Learning Manager for Moët Hennessy in Asia Pacific, your role will involve partnering with regional and global teams to design and implement strategic L&D initiatives. Your work will directly empower our people, build essential capabilities, and cultivate an engaging and inclusive culture that is critical to our ongoing success. If you're passionate about fostering continuous growth, enhancing critical skills, and nurturing an engaging work environment, this is your opportunity to contribute to our success. This is a 9-momth fixed-term contract. How you will contribute… Learning & Development Strategy and Execution Strategic L&D Leadership: Support the development and implementation of the regional Talent & Learning strategy, aligning with regional and global objectives and leverage existing tools and platforms to foster a culture of continuous learning and critical capability enhancement across Asia Pacific. Capability & Needs Assessment: Conduct thorough skills assessments and needs analyses to identify critical skill gaps and development opportunities, directly informing the design of targeted training solutions that support the regional SOAR strategy. Program Design & Delivery: Lead the end-to-end design, development, and facilitation of a diverse learning calendar, encompassing engaging workshops, digital learning, and experiential programs, ensuring high-quality content delivery. Stakeholder Collaboration & Curation: Act as a strong people partner, collaborating effectively with global and regional teams, and engaging with external vendors to curate and deliver high-quality, relevant learning content. Performance Measurement & Improvement: Establish and implement robust evaluation methods for all learning programs and initiatives, utilizing data and feedback to drive continuous improvement and demonstrate impact. Learning Culture & Engagement: Champion a dynamic, feedback-driven learning culture, actively promoting participative learning and growth initiatives throughout the organization. Talent Development & Management: Strategic Talent Advancement: Drive regional talent investment strategies and programs, focusing on upskilling, leadership advancement, and overall capability building to support the long-term talent agenda. Talent Pipeline & Mobility: Collaborate with HR teams to actively build a robust and diverse talent pipeline within the region, ensuring talent mobility and readiness for future organizational needs. High-Potential Development: Identify and nurture high-potential talent, providing targeted development opportunities to accelerate their growth and prepare them for future leadership roles. Other: Budget Management & Operational Excellence: Manage the L&D and Talent Development budget effectively, ensuring efficient allocation of resources, and overseeing administrative processes to support the smooth execution of all programs and initiatives. Cross-Functional Support & Ad-Hoc Initiatives: Provide responsive support for various HR and business initiatives, including contributing to ad-hoc projects, preparing reports, and handling other duties as assigned to advance organizational talent objectives We are looking for unique and amazing talent. Join us if you have… 8+ years of experience in Learning & Development, Talent Management, or a similar HR function, with a strong focus on training and program delivery. Proven experience in designing, implementing, and evaluating learning programs with experience using LMS platforms Strong understanding of adult learning principles and instructional design methodologies. Excellent communication, presentation, and facilitation skills. Ability to work effectively in a multicultural and fast-paced environment. Proactive, results-oriented, and a strong team player. Proficiency in English; additional regional languages are a plus When you join Moët Hennessy… You embark on a career journey with the leaders of luxury wines, champagnes, and spirits, within the prestigious LVMH Group. You enter a world of possibility where career development opportunities across our 5 global regions and within the entire LVMH Group are unmatched. You join a passionate workforce within our internationally diverse and inclusive teams, exploring the rich heritage of our Maisons and shaping the next chapter of our story You discover our essential relationship with our terroirs and the soils that define our products. Through our Living Soils Living Together program, we are committed to regenerating soils, reducing climate impact, and empowering communities for a sustainable future. You will share in our passion for hosting, the art of living, and creating products that celebrate life’s most memorable milestones, always experienced through responsible tasting. Ready for a world of possibilities? Discover Moët Hennessy today!

Work with the Product Owner (PO) if any on this project to prioritize requirements and decompose them into detailed user stories, ensuring a clear roadmap for development. Translate requirements into comprehensive functional specifications, including detailed descriptions of user interactions, data flows, and system behavior. Collaborate with development teams to ensure that the software design aligns with the functional specifications and meets the defined requirements. Provide functional expertise to the development team, ensuring they have a clear understanding of the business processes and user needs. Research existing solutions, both internal and external, to determine if they can be leveraged to meet the business requirements. Understand technical constraints and participate in software design discussions, ensuring that the solution is feasible and aligns with the overall system architecture. Ensure the functional quality of the software, including participation in functional testing and providing functional support during production. Analyze the impact of functional changes on current production Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and Operational Level Agreements (OLAs). Contribute to Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) activities by providing user scenarios and supporting functional testing efforts. Contribute to deployment activities by providing functional pre-requisites and performing post-deployment checks. Support team members in their functional activities, train new joiners, and help resolve functional issues. Ensure the seamless integration of the software into the existing IT infrastructure, working closely with the Functional Architect. Specifications, workload estimates, planning, test scenarios, documentation. Requirements: Top 3 MUST have skills: Strong hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP, including secure setup, configuration, and administration of compute, storage, and networking resources. Proficient in implementing security controls and automation using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools such as Terraform, ensuring compliance and secure deployments. Solid understanding of cloud security principles, identity and access management (IAM), encryption, network security (VPC, subnets, VPN), and monitoring tools (e.g., AWS GuardDuty, Security Hub, Inspector) to protect workloads and data. Qualification: Experience with container security and orchestration platforms (e.g., Kubernetes security hardening). Knowledge of DevSecOps practices and integrating security into CI/CD pipelines. Familiarity with compliance frameworks and cloud security benchmarks (CIS). Ability to implement cloud-native threat detection and response solutions. Exposure to vulnerability management and remediation in cloud environments. Basic scripting skills in Python, Bash, or PowerShell for security automation. Strong troubleshooting skills and ability to work in cross-functional teams. Experience with disaster recovery and secure backup strategies.

title: Marketing & Growth Lead
description: We are a small but fast-growing tuition centre in Singapore, built on strong academic outcomes and modern, content-driven student acquisition. As a lean and ambitious team, we move fast, experiment often, and expect real ownership. We are looking for a Marketing & Growth Lead who will own the end-to-end marketing and sales process, from campaign ideation to student conversion. Role Overview This is a high-ownership role. You will be responsible not just for creating content, but for designing, championing, and executing marketing campaigns that directly drive enrolments. You will plan the full funnel — awareness, engagement, trial, conversion — and work closely with tutors to turn teaching content and TikTok Live sessions into real business growth. This role is ideal for someone who thinks like a founder or growth operator, not a task-based marketer. Key Responsibilities End-to-End Marketing & Sales Ownership Design and own the entire student acquisition funnel Plan how leads are generated, nurtured, and converted into signups Champion marketing campaigns from concept to execution and post-campaign review Take responsibility for outcomes (e.g. enquiries, trial sign-ups, enrolments) Marketing Strategy & Campaign Planning Craft a clear, actionable marketing plan aligned with enrolment targets Plan and launch specific campaigns (e.g. crash courses, exam-season pushes, TikTok Live series, free trial funnels) Identify target segments (students vs parents, level-specific strategies) Track performance metrics and continuously iterate Content Creation & Social Media Plan/design/shoot/edit/create short-form videos, images, captions, and posts Manage posting schedules and content calendars Repurpose TikTok Live content into clips for sustained reach Ensure consistency in branding, messaging, and credibility TikTok Live & Tutor-Led Marketing Plan TikTok Live teaching and engagement strategies as a key growth channel Guide tutors on structuring live lessons for engagement and conversion, communicating value clearly to students and parents, and handling basic sales messaging naturally during live sessions Coordinate live schedules and coach tutors on basic on-screen presence and messaging Beneficial Prior Experience Candidates with experience in one or more of the following will be highly valued: Marketing or growth roles in education, tuition, or enrichment centres Running or scaling TikTok, Instagram, or short-form video accounts Planning and executing lead-generation or conversion-focused campaigns Creator-led marketing, influencer marketing, or live commerce Early-stage startup or small business environments Graphic design, video-editing for social media posts Work Arrangement & Compensation Part-time, flexible hours Hybrid / remote-friendly (some in-person coordination required) Highly competitive pay, with performance-based incentives Clear path to expanded responsibility or full-time role as the centre scales Candidates are required to commit for at least six months

title: Transaction Monitoring Officer
description: Responsibilities: Investigate and assess transaction monitoring alerts to identify potential Money Laundering or Terrorism Financing Activities Investigate alerts escalated to the transaction monitoring team Communicate with relevant business units in the investigation of cases and to provide transaction monitoring advisory Establish, review and update policies and procedures relating to transaction monitoring Review and clearance of sanctions alerts Handling of production order and seizure orders from local authorities Assist to monitor daily alerts handling and documentation activities of the team and STR filing Review/follow up on investigation reports conducted by analyst for completeness and timeliness Communicate with relevant business units in the investigation of cases and to provide transaction monitoring advisory Review and preparation of documentation for STR filing Periodic review and update of policies and procedures relating to transaction monitoring Conduct periodic sample checks and prepare reports on the quality of alerts clearance and documentation with proposals for improvement. Review of TM threshold/parameters. Providing training/guidance to the analysts and business units. Handling of queries from MAS, auditors and other external parties. Conduct gap analysis on new/existing MAS Notices ,Guidelines, Circulars etc. Any other tasks or duties assigned by Team Lead/Head of Department or Company from time to time Requirement: Recognized bachelor degree or above, preferably in Business or related discipline At least 4 years experience in banking industry, and 2 years in Transaction Monitoring Proficiency in Mandarin to liaise with internal/external stakeholders A good understanding of Transaction monitoring and the fund flows relating to banking industry Knowledge of sanctions and trade finance enhanced due diligence Previous experience in investigation and preparation of cases for STR filing Previous experience in TM threshold/parameters tuning is an advantage Excellent eye for details Strong analytical mind-set Good writing skills Able to work independently and within a team Strong interpersonal and communication skills at all organizational levels

title: Regional Account Manager (Freight Forwarding / East) [EL]
description: The Regional Account Manager serves as the primary representative of the company, engaging with clients to strengthen partnerships, enhance customer retention, and drive business growth. This role requires a strategic approach to account management, business development, and customer relationship management across the South-East Asia and Oceania regions. Key Responsibilities: Business Development: Build and maintain strong relationships with key customer contacts, including sourcing, operations, supply chain, and project stakeholders at the regional level. Analyze customer supply chain requirements to identify opportunities for company services (air, sea, third-party logistics, etc.). Develop and implement strategies to ensure business continuity and expansion, working closely with Regional/Country Sales & Marketing Heads and Executive Sponsors. Account Management: Engage with key decision-makers and influencers to strengthen regional partnerships. Oversee multiple Global Key Accounts within the assigned region, ensuring alignment with business objectives. Achieve and exceed budget targets, including net sales and volume goals. Lead in-country Key Account Teams to improve collaboration and operational efficiency. Maintain detailed customer profiles, including air and sea target volumes by trade lane. Keep customer relationship management (CRM) systems updated with account activities and insights. Co-manage contracts with the Global Account Manager, including Master Service Agreements (MSA), Statements of Work (SOW), and Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA). Organize and oversee key client engagements, such as meetings, RFQs, and business implementations (SOPs, training, projects, and reports). Work closely with in-country Key Account Managers (KAMs) to develop customized solutions that address cost, quality, and value-added services based on customer needs. Address major service failures, claims, KPI issues, and accounts receivable (AR) concerns, ensuring smooth resolution. Contribute to profit & loss (P&L) improvement by providing relevant financial insights to the account management team. Drive innovation and strategic solutions, fostering a partner-oriented approach with both internal and external stakeholders. Qualifications & Experience: Experience: Minimum 10 years in account management, with at least 5 years in regional account management. Industry Expertise: Must have a strong background in logistics or supply chain management. We regret only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be handled confidentially. By submitting your application, you agree to the collection, use, retention, and disclosure of your personal information to prospective employers. Wecruit Pte Ltd EA License No: 20C0270

title: Risk Developer Specialist (Murex)

Work will also include development using UNIX shell scripts, SQL and other languages to automate the interfaces between Murex and other application systems. The candidate is expected to work closely, and communicate effectively and independently with project managers, business analysts and business users, as well as external vendors Key requirements: Murex Market Risk Management modules & Murex Datamart End of Day process SQL & Shell scripting Control-M Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Systems, or a related discipline Minimum 7 years of overall IT experience, with at least 5 years of hands-on experience in Murex Market Risk Management module. Proven track record in implementing and supporting market risk functionalities in Murex. Strong proficiency in SQL/Oracle (including stored procedures). Expertise in Unix Shell scripting. In-depth knowledge of Murex Market Risk Management concepts and configurations. Experience in Murex simulation views, formulae, and datamart development. Hands-on experience with Market Risk module functionalities such as: Value-at-Risk (VaR), Sensitivities, Stress Testing, Back Testing Development of Market Risk MRE and MRA objects in Murex, including: Revaluation, Raw and logical sources, Aggregation views, Node formula configuration Strong understanding of Market Risk configuration (e.g., static data, physical feeders). Proficiency in MRA scripting and automation, including ant-script development for aggregation automation, tagging, and housekeeping. Additional Competencies: Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. Strong problem-solving and analytical skills. Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

title: Trade Finance/ Accounting – Oil & Gas Trading
description:
Description This is one of the oil trading companies in the world, involved in the production and global distribution of refined petroleum products. An exciting opportunity has arisen for a Trade Finance/ Accounting to join the trading business in Singapore. Role Reporting to the Trade Finance Manager, the Trade Finance Executive is responsible for managing receivables and payables for physical product trading, handling LC issuance and negotiations, reviewing trade contracts, coordinating with operations on trade and shipment details, and processing payments. title: Chief Technology Officer (IT)
description: X Star is a Singapore-based AI innovator transforming the automotive finance industry. Since our founding in 2023, we have expanded our operational footprint across Asia Pacific, Latin America, and the Middle East. We leverage proprietary artificial intelligence to provide consumers with cutting-edge financial services while delivering powerful technology solutions that enhance value for our partners. Our global network now includes over 2,000 car manufacturers, dealers, and financial institutions. Backed by a dedicated team of 200 professionals and a financing facility of $150 million as of H1 2025, X Star is accelerating its global expansion to bring intelligent, seamless mobility solutions to a worldwide consumer base. Headquartered in Singapore, X Star operates across key markets, including Japan and Mexico. Our Singapore business alone has facilitated over SGD 100 million in cumulative loans, demonstrating strong market traction and scalability. As a result of continuous growth of business, we are looking for an experienced Chief Technology Officer (IT) to join us and play a critical role in shaping our future success. Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership Define and implement the company’s IT vision and roadmap. Align technology initiatives with overall business goals. Identify opportunities for digital transformation and innovation. Technology Management Oversee IT infrastructure, applications, and enterprise systems. Ensure high availability, scalability, and performance of technology platforms. Evaluate and adopt emerging technologies to maintain competitive advantage. Cybersecurity & Compliance Establish and enforce robust cybersecurity policies and practices. Ensure compliance with industry regulations and data protection standards. Manage risk assessments and disaster recovery planning. Team Leadership Build, mentor, and lead a high-performing IT team. Foster a culture of collaboration, innovation, and continuous improvement. Manage vendor relationships and technology partnerships. Operational Excellence Optimize IT processes for efficiency and cost-effectiveness. Implement best practices in IT service management. Monitor KPIs and report on IT performance to executive leadership. Qualifications Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field. Proven experience (10+ years) in IT leadership roles, with at least 5 years in a senior executive position. Strong knowledge of enterprise IT systems, cloud computing, cybersecurity, and software development. Demonstrated ability to align technology with business strategy. Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills.

title: Data Analyst Lead
description: Role Overview: The Senior Assistant Director (Data Analyst) leads the design, development, and continuous improvement of enterprise dashboards and analytical products that deliver actionable insights across HQ and Research Institutes. This role ensures analytical outputs are trusted, well-governed, and aligned with organisational priorities, working closely with Business Analysts, Data Engineers, and AI teams to translate data into measurable outcomes. Key Responsibilities: A. Enterprise Analytics Delivery Lead the design and development of interactive dashboards and data visualisations using Power BI and Snowflake. Define and maintain consistent data modelling and data transformation logic, metrics, and KPI definitions across analytical products. Collaborate with Data Engineers to ensure datasets used are certified and discoverable in Alation, with clear business definitions and lineage. Partner with Business Analysts to ensure dashboard outputs meet stakeholder needs and strategic objectives. Oversee dashboard code version control. B. Data Analysis & Insight Generation Conduct deep-dive analyses to uncover trends, correlations, and drivers of business performance. Translate findings into concise insights and actionable recommendations for leadership. Integrate predictive or diagnostic analytics into dashboards in collaboration with the Data Science and AI teams. C. Quality & Governance Validate the accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of data powering dashboards. Provide feedback on data quality and metadata accuracy to support Alation certification and stewardship processes. Contribute to establishing and maintaining enterprise standards for dashboard design, refresh frequency, and version control. D. Collaboration & Capability Building Mentor junior analysts and uplift analytical standards across the team. Lead training or sharing sessions under the Analytics Centre of Excellence to build data literacy organisation wide. Act as a bridge between business users and technical teams, ensuring clarity and alignment on analytical goals. Qualifications & Experience: Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Business Analytics, Information Technology, or a related discipline. 8–10 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or performance analytics. Advanced proficiency in Power BI, SQL, and data modelling using Snowflake. Strong understanding of data pipelines, metadata management, and governance frameworks (e.g., Alation). Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and data-visualisation skills. Proven ability to engage senior stakeholders and communicate technical findings in plain language. Experience leading or mentoring junior analysts is preferred.
