title: Research Fellow (Operations Research & Analytics)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description Role Summary: Lead and manage multi-stakeholder research projects in operations research, analytics, and optimization, including collaborations with NTUC FairPrice (Plug-in Flow Routing Optimization Engine; Store Friendly replenishment packing) and PSA (data analysis to improve efficiency in the container logistics ecosystem), as well as other industry and academic projects. Drive end-to-end project delivery—from problem framing and model formulation to implementation, evaluation, and dissemination—while coordinating milestones and deliverables with collaborators. Key Responsibilities: Act as project manager for assigned projects, developing work plans, timelines, risk registers, and progress reports; ensure on-time delivery of milestones and industry-facing deliverables. Work closely with external collaborators (e.g., NTUC FairPrice, Store Friendly, PSA) and internal stakeholders to define objectives, align expectations, and translate operational needs into rigorous research questions. Design and formulate models for routing, packing, replenishment, scheduling, and network optimization; develop algorithms and decision-support tools using appropriate techniques (e.g., mathematical programming, stochastic optimization, simulation, machine learning). Lead data acquisition, cleaning, integration, and analysis; establish data pipelines and quality assurance processes; conduct experiments, benchmarking, and sensitivity analyses to validate models. Prototype and, where applicable, support pilot deployment of solutions; document methodologies, assumptions, reproducible workflows, and user guides. Prepare high-quality publications, technical reports, and presentations; contribute to dissemination through conferences, seminars, and industry briefings. Guide and mentor PhD students, research assistants, and interns; provide technical supervision, review work outputs, and foster a collaborative research culture. Coordinate compliance with NUS and partner data governance, ethics, and security requirements; manage project documentation and IP considerations in line with agreements. Contribute to grant proposals, project scoping, and resource planning; assist with budgeting and procurement related to project needs. Support broader IORA initiatives, including knowledge sharing, seminar organization, and collaborations across the University and with external partners Requirements PhD in Industrial Engineering/Operations Research/Management Science (or closely related), with a strong research record in stochastic modeling and optimization. Hands-on experience solving real-world routing, packing, replenishment, scheduling, and network optimization problems. Proficiency with large-scale decision methods (e.g., approximate dynamic programming/rollout, primal–dual or penalty controls, Lagrangian decomposition/index policies, queueing/fluid models). Strong programming skills in Python and/or Java (MATLAB a plus), with experience in data pipelines, simulation/digital twins, and reproducible experimentation. Proven project management and industry collaboration experience, delivering milestones and translating operational needs into deployable solutions. Strong communication and mentorship: publications, technical reporting, presentations, and guidance of PhD students/RAs.

title: Lead Cybersecurity Specialist (Governance)
description:
​ Our Client is an established company in
Singapore, who is seeking to recruit a Lead Cybersecurity Specialist (Governance). Lead Cybersecurity Specialist
(Governance) You will be the primary
architect of the security governance and risk management framework., with the mission
to transform GRC from a compliance-heavy exercise into a strategic enabler. You
will ensure that risk management is deeply integrated into the lifecycle of
every digital system, from web applications to critical Operational Technology
(OT) environments. 1. Enterprise Risk
Governance & Management ·       Dynamic Risk Registers: Establish and oversee the security
risk register. You will ensure that registers are not static documents but
"living" tools that accurately reflect the current threat landscape
and project status across all agencies. ·       Senior Management Facilitation: Lead and facilitate
high-level risk conversations with Senior Management. You must be able to
translate complex technical risks into clear business impacts to drive informed
resource allocation and prioritisation. ·       Risk Analysis Framework: Develop a robust framework to guide
agencies in performing consistent, high-quality risk analysis. This framework
should empower agencies to take calculated risks for innovation rather than
defaulting to "no" due to risk aversion. 2. Threat Risk Assessment
(TRA) & Standards ·       Unified TRA Framework: Establish and maintain standards for
conducting Threat Risk Assessments across diverse domains, including Cloud
(GCC), Web Applications, and OT/ICS systems. ·       Crown Jewel Identification: Develop SOPs to guide project
teams in identifying "Crown Jewels" (Critical Information Assets) and
mapping comprehensive threat vectors. ·       Standardisation of Controls: Define common security
configuration standards and ensure that controls are technically effective in
mitigating identified risks, rather than just meeting baseline requirements. 3. Zero Trust &
Architecture Governance ·       Zero Trust Roadmap: Lead the establishment of a Zero Trust
Framework, setting the standards for identity-based security,
micro-segmentation, and "never trust, always verify" architectures. ·       Architectural Advisory: Provide expert GRC input during the
design phase of high-impact systems to ensure security-by-design and alignment
with standards. ·       Technology Application: Evaluate and recommend security
technologies that effectively mitigate specific risks, ensuring that defensive
layers remain relevant against modern threats. 4. Supply Chain &
Ecosystem Risk Management ·       Third-Party Risk Strategy: Establish the framework for
managing risks across the software supply chain and IT vendors. ·       Dependency & Vendor Risk: Develop standards for assessing
the cyber-resilience of third-party partners and managing risks associated with
software dependencies (e.g., Open Source libraries). 5. Audit Excellence &
Systemic Improvement ·       Proactive Readiness: Shift agencies from "reactive"
audit preparation to a state of continuous compliance and readiness. ·       Root Cause Rectification: Oversee the closure of audit
findings, ensuring agencies implement substantive, effective technical fixes
rather than surface-level measures. 6. Stakeholder Management
& Threat Intelligence ·       Education & Advocacy: Partner with key stakeholders to
inculcate a proactive risk management mindset. ·       Threat & Tech Foresight: Keep abreast of evolving Actor
TTPs (Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures) and technology changes. Periodically
review the relevancy of existing defences against the latest threats. Requirements Experience ·       Years of Experience: 10 to 12 years in Cybersecurity GRC,
Information Security Risk Management, or Security Architecture. ·       Domain Breadth: Proven experience in managing risks across IT
and Cloud environments; exposure to OT (Operational Technology) systems is a
significant advantage. ·       Regulatory Knowledge: Deep familiarity with security policies
(e.g., Instruction Manual on IT Management) and international standards (e.g.,
NIST, ISO 27001). Technical Skills ·       Risk Methodologies: Mastery of risk assessment methodologies
(e.g., TVRA) and the ability to translate technical vulnerabilities into
business risk. ·       Security Technologies: Strong technical understanding of
various Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA) components and cloud security
technologies. Such as Firewalls, EDR, IAM, SIEM, CSPM, CWPP, CASB and secrets
management etc. ·       Threat Awareness: Ability to map technical controls to the
MITRE ATT&CK framework to ensure defensive coverage. ·       Offensive Security: Proficiency in manual and automated
testing tools; deep understanding of the MITRE ATT&CK framework and common
TTPs. ·       Certifications: Professional certifications such as CISM
(Certified Information Security Manager), CRISC (Certified in Risk and
Information Systems Control), CISSP, OSCP or OSWE (Offensive Security Web
Expert) are highly preferred. Soft Skills ·       Strategic Influence: Ability to educate and persuade senior
stakeholders (CIOs/Project Owners) on the importance of rigorous risk
governance. ·       Critical Thinking: Ability to look past surface-level audit
compliance to find and fix underlying systemic issues. ·       Lifelong Learner: A genuine passion for staying updated on
the latest security technologies and evolving cyber threat landscapes. ·       Risk Articulation: Exceptional ability to
"translate" deep technical issues (e.g., zero-day vulnerabilities,
configuration drifts) into business risk for non-technical senior executives. JJ Consulting Services EA Licence No.: 12C6207 Applicants are invited to send in a MS Word resume to jobs@jjconsulting.com.sg stating
position applying for/present/expected salaries and earliest available date. We thank all applicants in advance and regret that only short listed candidates
will be notified.

title: Executive, KYC
description: We invite highly motivated, dynamic professionals to join our growing Quality & Risk Management Team. Our Quality & Risk Management Department disseminates timely and relevant information on developments in legislation and regulations, executes initiatives to manage our risk in a challenging environment. The position involves operating as part of a risk management and compliance team. The AML Monitoring & Compliance function is part of Quality and Risk Management supporting the Firm in customer due diligence process to evaluate the KYC screening results, identify any potential risks prior to accepting or continuing business relationships, ensuring they falls within the regulatory and compliance expectations. Job Description and Responsibilities: Evaluate, perform validation of completeness and accuracy of the KYC screenings, evaluate and document the assessment of the results according to relevant regulations, guidance and procedures Handle internal queries on regulatory requirements and compliance policies and procedures on AML-CFT Provide support in the development and maintenance of in-house training content for periodic trainings on AML-CFT Participate in establishing, implement and provide oversight AML/CFT policies, procedures, systems and controls Manage and engage with internal stakeholders, setting service standards and resolving issues in accordance with organizational procedures, promote understanding and compliance with risk management policies and procedure Job Requirements: A recognized degree, preferably in Accounting, Banking & Finance or equivalent Minimum 2 years of relevant experience in compliance and regulatory matters with a sizable financial institution/regulatory body, legal, brokerage and investment firm preferred Candidates with Certified Fraud Examiner (CFE) or Association of Certified Anti-Money Laundering Specialists (ACAMS) will be an added advantage Working knowledge of local regulatory guidelines, KYC and AML guidelines is essential Possess strong analytical and problem-solving skills with excellent verbal/written communication and interpersonal skills Ability to exercise sound judgement, escalate issues and propose solutions appropriately Familiar with the use of research databases such as Lexis Nexis, World Check, Factiva, Corporate Registries and related research tools Team player, self-motivated, resourceful and has an eye for detail ⠀ Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted by KPMG Talent Acquisition team, personal data collected will be used for recruitment purposes only. At KPMG in Singapore we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. We believe that diversity of thought, background and experience strengthens relationships and delivers meaningful benefits to our people, our clients and communities. As an equal opportunity employer, all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment regardless of age, race, gender identity or expression, colour, marital status, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or other non-merit factors. We celebrate the different talents that our people bring and support every staff member in their journey to achieve personal and professional growth. title: Accounts Assistant

title: Head, GM Client & Sales Operations
description: Job Summary The Head of Global Markets Client and Sales Operations is a senior leadership role responsible for the end-to-end operational support of Global Markets client coverage and sales activities. This role oversees Markets Client Implementation, Client Services, Channel Management, and Sales Support, ensuring a seamless, scalable, and compliant client experience across all regions and asset classes. The role partners closely with Global Markets Sales, COO, Coverage, Product, Compliance, Risk, Technology, and Operations to enable revenue growth while maintaining strong controls, regulatory compliance, and operational excellence. What Success Looks Like •    Seamless, compliant, and scalable client onboarding and servicing model. •    Improved client experience and Sales productivity. •    Strong regulatory outcomes and effective risk management. •    High-performing, engaged global teams supporting Global Markets growth. RESPONSIBILITIES •    Fostering and maintaining dialogue across the SCB global on-boarding network to develop and enhance global client relationships and responding to client needs. •    Develop and implement a forward-looking transformation strategy for Markets client and sales support, focusing on scalability, efficiency, and enhanced client experience. •    Identify key opportunities for process redesign, automation, and digitalization to improve operational outcomes. Markets Client Implementation •    Oversee end-to-end Markets client implementation, including product provisioning, documentation and regulatory requirements. •    Ensure timely, risk-appropriate onboarding aligned with revenue priorities and client experience expectations. •    Partner closely with Compliance, Legal, Risk, and Front Office to manage onboarding complexity and regulatory change. •    Continuously enhance onboarding processes through automation, digitization, and data quality improvements. Markets Operational Client Relationship Management •    Lead global Markets Client Services teams responsible for post-onboarding client support across products and regions. •    Ensure effective resolution of client inquiries related to account setup, trading enablement, static data, and service issues. •    Establish service standards, SLAs, and performance metrics aligned with client and business expectations. •    Drive a client-centric culture while maintaining strong controls and risk awareness. Markets Channel Management •    Own operational oversight of client channels, including electronic platforms, portals, and communication tools. •    Ensure effective enablement and support of digital and electronic trading channels in partnership with Technology and Product teams. •    Oversee client access entitlements, permissions, and controls across channels. •    Support the rollout of new channels and enhancements with appropriate operational readiness and governance. Markets Sales Support •    Provide operational support to Sales and Coverage teams, including data, reporting, client analytics, and enablement tools. •    Ensure effective coordination of client requests, product onboarding, and issue resolution across internal teams. •    Support Sales productivity initiatives through process simplification, automation, and improved information flow. Processes •    Ensure alignment between global standards and local execution, managing regulatory divergence where required •    Ensure consistent, efficient, and scalable operational processes across regions while allowing for local market requirements. •    Drive continuous improvement, automation, and digital transformation initiatives across trading operations People & Talent •    Lead, develop, and retain a global, multi-regional team. •    Build strong leadership capability and succession planning across the organization. •    Foster a culture of accountability, continuous improvement, and client focus. •    Champion diversity, equity, and inclusion. Risk Management •    Own the control environment for Client and Sales Operations, including operational risk, conduct risk, and regulatory compliance. •    Ensure adherence to global policies, local regulations, and internal governance standards. •    Lead responses to audits, regulatory exams, and control reviews related to client and sales operations. •    Establish robust governance forums, metrics, and management reporting. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    Act as a key partner to Global Markets COO and front office leadership, providing operational insight and enabling business growth. •    Collaborate closely with Technology on platform stability, change delivery, and strategic investment priorities. •    Work with Finance, Treasury, Risk, Compliance, and Legal to ensure integrated decision-making and execution. •    Represent Trading Operations at senior management committees and forums. Our Ideal Candidate •	Education - Bachelor’s degree in finance, business, economics, or a related discipline required. •	Advanced degree (MBA or equivalent) preferred. •	15+ years of relevant experience in leading Global Markets Client and sales operations. Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Strong financial acumen and analytical capability. •    Expertise in performance measurement, management information, and data analytics. •    Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights. •    Experience supporting large-scale operational transformation •    Demonstrated ability to engage effectively with senior management and regulators. •    Strong judgment and ability to provide constructive, credible challenge. About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. Recruitment Assessments Some of our roles use assessments to help us understand how suitable you are for the role you've applied to. If you are invited to take an assessment, this is great news. It means your application has progressed to an important stage of our recruitment process. Visit our careers website www.sc.com/careers

title: Strategic Commodity Manager (Semiconductor Industry)
description: Responsibilities Drive and support the execution of the regional commodity strategy in alignment with global procurement goals, ensuring the development of a strong and sustainable supplier base across Asia. Build and maintain a robust supplier portfolio by identifying potential vendors, participating in onboarding decisions, managing supplier data, and overseeing supplier classification throughout the full lifecycle. Strengthen supplier relationships through regular engagement, business reviews, and international visits, including negotiating annual volumes, capacities, and supporting supplier audits. Identify cost-reduction opportunities, develop savings plans, track implementation, and prepare savings reports aligned with annual targets. Lead supplier transfer or relocation projects by assessing strategic, cost, performance, and risk considerations, ensuring smooth transition and continuity of supply. Support procurement process improvements by contributing to digitalization and standardization initiatives, assisting in supplier selection and performance enhancement, and handling escalations for supplier complaints as required by operations. Minimum 3 years of experience in commodity management, strategic sourcing, or supplier management within a regional or global procurement environment. Strong negotiation, stakeholder management, and relationship-building skills, with the ability to manage suppliers across multiple countries. Proactive, analytical, and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage complex projects, mitigate risks, and support continuous improvement initiatives. title: Junior Fund Accountant
description: Our Fund Office Support Services in Asset and Wealth Managed Services (“AWMS”) is expanding rapidly and we are looking for motivated professionals to join us. We are Singapore’s leading Asset and Wealth Management team, and you will experience a dynamic and fast-paced working environment. You will be responsible for a portfolio of fund manager, family office and asset owners clients (the “clients’) and will work as part of a team to achieve and meet their needs. You will be responsible for a portfolio of clients engagements to meet objectives and budget successfully. You will also act as the liaison and point of contact for these clients. Effective day-to-day management of clients; building and maintaining client relationships is crucial. You need to ensure that all deadlines and milestones are met as planned. You will also ensure that you hold yourself to the highest standard, in terms of work quality, risk management, issue escalation/resolution and client delivery. You need to understand the client’s issues and manage the expectations of different stakeholders by translating expectations into quality & balanced output/deliverables. You need to work closely with other functions (i.e., Tax, Valuation, etc) within PwC and address client needs by bringing relevant expertise from the firm to client discussions. Specific responsibilities include the following: Fund Accounting Responsible to onboard new clients and set-up new funds and fund structures into the accounting system in accordance to governing documents (i.e., PPM, LPA, etc). Responsible for providing quality accounting/bookkeeping and fund administration service for open ended/closed ended/evergreen Alternative Investments Funds (Private equity/Venture Capital/Real Estate and Fund-of funds), including family offices (the “Funds”). Preparation of monthly/quarterly/semi-annual/annual/ Net Asset Value (the “NAV”) reporting pack which includes detailed trial balance, balance sheet, statement of operations, investment, bank reconciliation and investment holdings report. Drafting of financial statements and related FRS/IFRS/RAP 7/US GAAP disclosures in accordance with accounting standards. Oversee and facilitate audits of the Funds and liaise with the auditors to ensure timely completion of audit. Perform follow-up with clients as necessary on accounting records and issues and develop solutions to these issues. Assist senior fund accountants to take care of day-to-day operation of the funds which includes resolving accounting issues and client service matters. Ensure optimal level of client service matters and all deliverables are on time and meet set quality standards. Transfer Agency Services Responsible for the maintenance of the shareholder/investor register. Prepare capital call/distribution/redemption/conversion/share transfer calculations and process these in a timely manner. Co-ordinate documentation for capital activities to ensure AML compliance. Management of commitment agreements and subscription forms including monitoring of commitments, drawdowns, and uncalled commitments. Deliver the organisation's FATCA/CRS/K1 service offering to our clients. Investors Services Assist with new client onboarding and ensure proper fund and fund structure set ups within the accounting system in accordance to governing documents (i.e., PPM, LPA, etc). Assist senior fund accountants in liaising, discussing, and resolving issues surrounding client onboarding with fund managers and stakeholders. Maintain and enforce adherence to the Fund’s policies and procedures in accordance with their business line operating model to safeguard clients’ interest Prepare investor onboarding documentations to ensure AML/KYC compliance. Take care of day-to-day operation of the funds and following up with third parties such as investors and fund managers. Assist in the organisation of board meetings and shareholder’ meetings. Responsible for maintaining client relationships, inquiries, and meetings. Operational Support Assist in setting up new client accounts and transitions by preparing required documentation and ensuring accurate system entries. Support resolution of routine fund accounting and administration issues by coordinating with relevant stakeholders. Provide timely updates to senior team members on any material issues affecting deliverables. Monitor and track performance to meet the requirements set out in the SLA. Contribute to identifying process improvement opportunities and suggest ways to enhance operational efficiency. Help maintain internal controls and compliance with SSAE 16 requirements through accurate record-keeping and adherence to procedures. FA/TA operational and organisational readiness and system implementation Assist in maintaining and updating Fund Accounting (FA) and Transfer Agency (TA) operational processes and system setups to meet evolving business needs. Support efforts to improve work processes by identifying inefficiencies and suggesting practical enhancements, including leveraging technology for greater scalability and efficiency. Provide administrative and documentation support for system implementation activities, ensuring accurate data entry and adherence to established procedures. ATTRIBUTES & SKILLS: You hold a Bachelor and/or master’s degree in Accounting or Economics. You have a minimum 1 to 3 years of relevant experience in alternative investment fund administration, Big 4 audit firm or domiciliation. You have good knowledge and understanding of the family office; and asset and wealth and management industry landscape and operations (including valuation (in private companies, debts, etc), accounting and payment procedures). You have good knowledge about Singapore fund structures (i.e., Singapore Variable Capital Company (VCC), Singapore Private Limited (Pte. Ltd.), SG limited partnership and unit trusts). You have good knowledge in accounting for investment products, ideally with fund-of-funds, single-manager funds, venture capital, real estate and private equity funds will be considered an asset. Competent accounting knowledge in Singapore FRS/IFRS/ RAP 7 and US GAAP standards. Previous experience in compliance and KYC procedures. Familiar with Singapore regulatory filing requirements. Professional, confident, and highly motivated self-starter, with an ability to handle multiple engagements/projects in a fast-paced environment with tight deadlines, positive attitude to learn and strong team player. Possess strong values, personal integrity, and emotional intelligence. Meticulous and attention to details, possessing an analytical and enquiring mind-set. Possess team spirit, a capacity to build strong client relationships and take initiative to act without waiting for direction (when appropriate). Advanced level MS excel, proficient with MS Word, MS PowerPoint. Knowledge and prior experience on Allvue System a plus. A professional designation such a C.A., C.P.A., or C.M.A. etc., would be an asset.

title: Head of Marketing, Singapore
description: IG Singapore is looking for a Head of Marketing, becoming a permanent member of our team. Working in the heart of the CBD on Raffles Place, you will be joining a team of passionate individuals seeking to grow our Singapore business and provide exceptional client service to our clients. So, who are we? Hello, we’re IG Group. No, not Instagram – though we're a pretty big deal ourselves. We’re a global, FTSE 250-listed company made up of a collection of progressive fintech brands in the world of online trading and investing. The best part? We’ve snapped up many awards for our top-class platforms, forward-thinking products, and incredible employee experiences. We believe in financial freedom. And our purpose is to help ambitious people achieve it. Around 400,000 people use our groundbreaking technology and forward-thinking platforms to trade the financial products they know and love. Did we mention we have offices in 18 countries? Yes, when you join IG Group, you'll have the chance to work across multiple brands, with people all over the world. Join us for an exciting future and let’s innovate together! Your role in the team We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Head of Marketing to lead our marketing efforts. Reporting directly to the CEO, this role will oversee a team and be responsible for driving the demanding growth of our new brokerage product while continuing to scale the existing CFD business. This is a key leadership position that requires both strategic vision and hands-on execution. You will own all marketing channels—digital, affiliates, social, PR, partnerships, and offline—and partner closely with sales, client servicing, and product teams to deliver measurable growth. The role is critical in shaping our regional brand presence, client acquisition, and engagement strategies, while ensuring marketing initiatives are compliant, data-driven, and aligned with business priorities. What you’ll do Lead and oversee all marketing channels, ensuring strategies are tailored to local market dynamics while aligned with global brand positioning. Manage the budget for markets and being responsible for cost effectiveness, resource allocation and measurable ROI. Drive the demanding growth of IG’s new brokerage product, while continuing to scale the existing CFD business. Partner closely with regional teams, performance team, sales, client servicing,  and product teams to ensure marketing initiatives directly support business objectives and client needs. Develop and execute integrated marketing strategies to drive awareness, acquisition, engagement, and retention across digital, performance, social, partnerships, PR, and offline channels. Manage agencies on record for communications, creative development and media buys. Continuously  track market trends and competitor activity to adapt strategies, seize  opportunities, and mitigate risks. Oversee  and inspire a team of marketers, building a high-performance, innovative, and collaborative culture. Uphold  regulatory and brand compliance standards across all campaigns and communications. Who we’re looking for You’re curious about things like the client experience, the rapid developments in tech, and the complex world of fintech regulation. You’re also a confident, creative thinker with a knack for innovating. We know that you know every problem has a solution. title: Geospatial Data Engineering
description: About the Role At Kyndryl, you play a key role in shaping how organisations use geospatial intelligence at scale. If you’re excited about designing enterprise‑grade GIS platforms, building modern geospatial applications, and working with advanced spatial analytics, this role gives you the depth, complexity, and career runway to grow into a true GIS expert or architect. Here, you won’t just maintain maps or process data—you’ll design, automate, and modernise full geospatial ecosystems for large enterprises across Singapore and the region. What You Will Do 1) Geospatial Data Engineering You’ll shape how spatial data is structured and managed across the organisation. You’ll: Design and maintain enterprise geodatabases (file/enterprise geodatabases, feature classes, domains, subtypes, topology rules). Build ETL/ELT pipelines for vector, raster, and imagery using ArcGIS Data Interoperability, ArcGIS API for Python, ModelBuilder, or FME. Implement data quality checks, metadata standards, schema versioning, and coordinate system workflows. 2) Services, Applications & Web GIS You’ll create the tools and experiences that users interact with every day. You’ll: Publish and optimise map, feature, and image services on ArcGIS Server. Manage Portal for ArcGIS content, groups, permissions, and sharing models. Build web GIS applications using Experience Builder, Web AppBuilder, Dashboards, Field Maps, Survey123, and StoryMaps. Develop custom web applications using ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript, ArcGIS REST API, and modern web frameworks. 3) Spatial Analytics & Modeling You turn raw data into actionable insights. You’ll: Execute advanced analysis (network, proximity, spatial statistics, suitability, 3D/scene layers) in ArcGIS Pro. Use GeoAnalytics, Raster/Spatial Analyst, Image Analyst, and Notebooks for large-scale analytics. Build repeatable geoprocessing models, notebooks, and scheduled jobs for production analytics. 4) Automation & Integration You ensure the GIS ecosystem is efficient, scalable, and well‑connected. You’ll: Automate admin tasks with ArcGIS API for Python (users, services, content, backups, monitoring). Integrate ArcGIS with enterprise platforms like SQL Server, PostgreSQL/PostGIS, ERP/CRM, data lakes, or APIs. Support real-time/event-driven GIS using GeoEvent Server, streaming services, and IoT feeds. 5) Platform Operations & Security You help keep the GIS platform reliable, secure, and high‑performing. You’ll: Support ArcGIS Enterprise deployments (Windows/Linux, on‑prem, Azure, AWS). Monitor caching, indexing, performance tuning, and ArcGIS Online credit usage. Enforce governance for roles, permissions, SSO/Entra ID, and security policies. Why Join Kyndryl? You’ll be part of a global technology services organisation driving digital transformation across Singapore’s key industries. You’ll get: Opportunities to work on meaningful national-scale or enterprise projects Exposure to cloud, automation, security, and data engineering A culture that values engineering depth, innovation, and continuous learning A supportive environment where your ideas shape the GIS landscape Required Skills & Experience Minimum 5+ years of relevant hands‑on experience in geospatial engineering, GIS development, or enterprise GIS platform management Strong hands‑on experience with ArcGIS Enterprise , ArcGIS Pro , and core Esri geospatial technologies Solid understanding of enterprise geodatabases (e.g., SQL Server, PostgreSQL/PostGIS), spatial data models, and coordinate systems Practical experience with Python for geospatial automation, scripting, or administrative tasks Experience working with web GIS tools or applications (e.g., Portal/ArcGIS Online, Web GIS app builders) Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret, process, and validate complex geospatial datasets Familiarity with GIS system administration concepts such as roles/permissions, security policies, or platform performance tuning Ability to work collaboratively in a technical environment and communicate effectively with non‑technical stakeholders Preferred Skills & Experience Experience with ETL tools such as FME, ArcGIS Data Interoperability, or other data integration platforms Knowledge of ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript or modern frontend frameworks for building custom GIS applications Exposure to GeoEvent Server , real‑time data streams, or IoT‑driven GIS workflows Experience deploying GIS solutions in cloud environments (Azure, AWS, Kubernetes) Familiarity with spatial analytics extensions such as GeoAnalytics, Spatial Analyst, Image Analyst, or Raster analytics Understanding of enterprise integrations with ERP/CRM systems , data lakes, or REST‑based APIs Professional certifications in geospatial technologies, cloud platforms, or data engineering

Key Roles & Responsibilities: 1) Investment & Capital Raising: Identify and engage high-net-worth investors and corporate entities from Mainland China to invest in Singapore’s vibrant nightlife and entertainment industry. 2) Tenant Acquisition: Strategically source and secure premium tenants from Mainland China to lease our 4-storey commercial property, ensuring a high-yield and synergetic tenant mix. 3) Project Design & Concept Planning: Conceptualize, plan, and design diverse nightlife operations (e.g., clubs, lounges, KTVs). Translate business visions into functional, high-aesthetic interior design layouts. 4) Renovation & Project Management: Oversee full-cycle renovation projects. Act as the primary liaison with general contractors to ensure projects are delivered on time, within budget, and to Singapore’s building standards. 5) International Procurement & Logistics: Negotiate directly with suppliers in Mainland China for furniture, lighting, sound system and specialized equipment. Manage the end-to-end supply chain, including quality control, shipping, and customs clearance into Singapore. 6) Strategic Investment Research: Conduct market research to identify lucrative real estate or business investment opportunities within Mainland China for the company’s portfolio. 7) Regulatory Compliance: Ensure all operations and tenant activities strictly adhere to Singapore’s nightlife regulations, liquor licensing, and MOM/URA/SCDF requirements. 8) Cross-Border Legal & Market Advisory: Provide expertise on Mainland China’s legal frameworks, trade policies, and market trends to mitigate risks in cross-border transactions. Requirements / Qualifications 9) Market Expertise: Deep familiarity with both the Singapore nightlife ecosystem and the Mainland China business landscape. 10) Language Proficiency: Fluency in English and Mandarin (written and spoken) is essential to negotiate legal contracts and deal with stakeholders in both regions. 11) Technical Knowledge: Experience in interior design, renovation workflows, and managing construction contractors. 12) Regulatory Knowledge: Strong understanding of Singapore’s licensing (Category 1 Public Entertainment, Liquor Licenses) and China’s foreign investment laws. 13) Network: An existing network of investors or hospitality operators in Mainland China is highly preferred.

title: Research Fellow (Aerospace Engineering/Transport/Mechanical Engineering/Computer Science)
description: Air Traffic Management Research Institute (ATMRI) is a research institute at Nanyang Technological University (NTU), Singapore, supported by the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS). The key objective of ATMRI is to support research and innovation in air traffic management. ATMRI focuses on developing practical and data-driven solutions for complex aviation systems, in close collaboration with industry and international partners. For more details, please view https://www.ntu.edu.sg/atmri . We are looking for a Research Fellow to conduct research on air traffic management (ATM) algorithms and data-driven methods. The role will focus on developing intelligent methods and operation-support tools for ATM applications, contributing to the advancement of research and innovation at Nanyang Technological University. Key Responsibilities: Conduct research on methods and approaches to support air traffic management studies and applications. Develop, test, and evaluate analytical models, algorithms, and tools using operational and simulated data. Support research activities related to airspace and air traffic operations under current and future ATM concepts. Work collaboratively with researchers and engineers to develop prototype solutions and decision-support tools. Analyse results and assess the performance and applicability of developed methods in realistic operational scenarios. Prepare technical reports, research papers, and project deliverables, and participate in project meetings and reviews. Contribute to research proposals, technical documentation, and other project-related activities as required. Job Requirements: PhD qualification degree in Aerospace Engineering, Transport, Mechanical Engineering, Computer Science, or other related fields. At least three (3) years of relevant research experience in air traffic management or related technical research roles. Familiarity with research related to air traffic management systems, airspace operations, or air traffic operations. Good written and oral communication skills with the ability to prepare technical reports and academic publications. Proficiency in job-specific technical skills, including programming for research and data analysis purposes (e.g. Python or similar languages), as well as strong numerical and analytical skills. Competent in general research-related skills and professional attributes, including problem-solving and analytical thinking. Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to work independently and collaboratively, develop solutions under defined timelines, and demonstrate good organisational and time management skills. title: Senior Associate, Voice of Brand
description: About KK39 KK39 is the investment arm of Wee Hur Holdings Ltd., a Singapore-listed group with a long-standing reputation in real estate in Singapore and Australia. We are evolving into a professionalised multi-asset investment platform spanning Private Equity, Private Credit, and Venture Capital, and are actively strengthening our industry presence and relationships with LPs, GPs, and ecosystem partners. To support this growth, KK39 is seeking a Voice of Brand (Senior Associate) who will spearhead our branding, communications, and engagement efforts to ensure KK39’s presence in the investment community is visible, credible, and differentiated. Role Overview The Voice of Brand (Senior Associate) will act as KK39’s branding and communications lead, responsible for building a consistent external voice for the firm. This role blends strategic communications, event curation, and stakeholder engagement to elevate KK39’s profile in the investment ecosystem. You will work closely with the investment team, translating KK39’s activities, perspectives, and initiatives into compelling narratives and touchpoints that resonate with LPs, GPs, and partners. Key Responsibilities 1. Brand Strategy & Positioning · Develop and refine KK39’s brand narrative to reflect our unique positioning as a listed-group-backed investment office. · Ensure consistency of tone, voice, and design across all communication channels. · Build branding frameworks (templates, messaging guides, outreach playbooks) that the investment team can easily adopt. 2. Events & Community Engagement · Lead the end-to-end planning, coordination, and execution of KK39-hosted events (eg. LP/GP networking sessions, roundtables, thought-leadership dinners). · Manage event logistics, invitations, attendee engagement, and follow-up communications. · Build and maintain a calendar of industry events and conferences; coordinate KK39’s participation, visibility, and speaking opportunities. 3. Content & Communications · Craft thought-leadership content (LinkedIn posts, press releases, newsletters, and op-eds) to highlight KK39’s activities and perspectives. · Produce post-event summaries and follow-up campaigns to ensure engagement is maximised beyond the event itself. · Coordinate with external partners (media, PR, design vendors) as needed. 4. title: Manager Airport and ANSP Charges
description: About the team you are joining At IATA, we speak for the airlines of the world, serving and supporting over 300 of them across all continents. We are passionate and knowledgeable about the aviation industry, and we strive to make it safer, smarter, more sustainable, and more inclusive. The Slots, Infrastructure & Customer Experience Team is dedicated to improving infrastructure and customer experience by working closely with airports, ANSPs, member airlines, and other stakeholders to drive efficiency improvements and reduce costs. Airlines are required to pay for access to airport facilities and air traffic control (ATC) services. Because many airport and ATC providers operate with limited competitive pressure, individual airlines often face challenges in evaluating whether proposed charges are equitable and aligned with the quality of infrastructure and service delivered. IATA’s role is to represent, lead and serve our members in this area. This position reports to the Senior Manager, Airport & ANSP Charges What your day would be like You will support IATA’s advocacy efforts on behalf of the aviation industry, engaging with some of the world’s largest air navigation service providers (ANSPs) and airports. You will also directly oversee campaign management with designated airports and ANSPs, working closely with country offices and advocacy teams in addition to working on global topics with the broader team. You will be based in Singapore. You will work with our global team in seven strategic locations, developing skills as a global subject matter expert. Actively lead selected airport and/or ATC charges campaign activities elaborating submissions with well-justified arguments, and advocating with relevant authorities. Work as part of a team to review and challenge airports and ANSP proposals, performing financial analysis, reviews for compliance with national regulation and ICAO/IATA policy. Use consultation data and other IATA data to perform analysis and prepare individual submissions and add to IATA’s broader dataset including supporting current and future commercial initiatives. Work closely with member airlines and IATA internal teams (notably airport development, ATM and charges experts) to implement agreed strategies and priorities for engagement on charges and infrastructure development. Contribute to learning and knowledge sharing within IATA and with our members and development of global guidance material and positions. Use global team tools to capture plans, progress, and results for the benefit of our members and IATA management. Collect intelligence and maintain global datasets for the region. We would love to hear from you if You have a university degree in a related field; suitable experience may be considered in lieu of a professional qualification. A master’s degree would be an advantage. Experience working for: - an airport, ANSP or regulated utility in commercial or regulatory functions - an Economics/consulting practice advising service providers in the development of pricing proposals or regulators in the assessment of pricing proposals from airports and ANSPs - a regulatory authority dealing with airports or ANSPs ​Strong background in financial statement and business case analysis. Comfortable with cost of capital concepts would be useful. Experience in managing pricing discussions/negotiations would be a strong asset. ​Knowledge of different regulatory approaches (e.g. price-cap, rate of return) and their application. Comfortable reviewing consultation materials, regulations and legislation to understand how frameworks work. Experience in regulatory appeals and court challenges will be an advantage. Proven teamwork abilities and a self-starter approach with the ability to proactively engage with stakeholders and progress work goals. Comfortable working in challenging and adversarial situations. Excellent communication skills in English. ​Experience in making clear presentations and reports based on technical merits and facts. ​Proficiency in Microsoft Office; PowerBI would be an advantage.

title: FM Governance & Performance Director
description: FM Governance & Performance Director Role Description The Business Operations Lead is a strategic leadership position dedicated to driving operational excellence and cross-functional consistency across a complex, multi-site regional portfolio. This role is accountable for the holistic contractual performance of the account, transforming high-level regional strategies into actionable, high-impact operational workflows. As the primary steward of account health, the Lead maintains a rigorous governance environment focused on performance optimization, proactive risk mitigation, and unwavering alignment with both JLL and client organizational frameworks. By delivering sophisticated data analytics and structural oversight, the Business Operations Lead empowers the Client leadership team to scale the partnership, ensuring that all contractual commitments are exceeded and the foundations for long-term growth are secured. Recurring Activities Performance Management & KPI Analytics Strategic Performance Oversight: Spearhead the consolidation and validation of regional performance data, ensuring all service lines consistently exceed contractual SLAs and KPIs. Data-Driven Insights: Design and distribute sophisticated performance dashboards that serve as the "single source of truth," providing leadership with real-time visibility into account health and operational efficiency. Continuous Optimization: Leverage advanced KPI analysis to identify emerging trends and performance gaps, translating data into actionable process optimization strategies. Contractual Stewardship & Governance Contractual Advisory: Provide high-level strategic support for all contracting matters, including renewals, scope-of-work modifications, and formal amendments. Governance Facilitation: Direct the Regional Governance Board’s rhythm, ensuring all leadership forums, workshops, and townhalls are structured, action-oriented, and rigorously tracked for execution. Compliance & Audit Readiness: Execute comprehensive reviews of operational protocols to guarantee absolute alignment with the Master Service Agreement (MSA) and JLL global standards. Strategic Documentation: Partner with Legal and Procurement teams to facilitate seamless contract revisions while maintaining a meticulous repository of all governance updates. Task Order Management & Strategic Alignment Lifecycle Administration: Oversee the end-to-end lifecycle of Task Orders, including the precise creation, strategic revision, and formal finalization of all work authorizations. Portfolio Integration: Ensure all Task Orders—spanning Integrated Facilities Management (IFM), Minor Works, and specialized contractual services—are meticulously aligned with client expectations and budgetary parameters. Contractual Synchronization: Act as the primary interface with the client to validate that Task Order scopes are technically accurate, compliant with the Master Service Agreement (MSA), and structured to support seamless operational delivery. Audit-Ready Documentation: Maintain a rigorous standard of documentation for all service modifications, ensuring that task-specific obligations are clearly defined and legally sound. Operational Excellence & Risk Mitigation Regional Standardization: Implement and refine unified operational processes across the multi-site portfolio to ensure seamless regional alignment and service consistency. Risk Management: Proactively identify and mitigate systemic operational risks, safeguarding the partnership and ensuring uninterrupted service delivery. Financial Stewardship: Collaborate with Finance teams to monitor regional expenditures, manage budget variances, and ensure all operational costs are optimized and compliant with client pass-through protocols. Executive Communications: Lead the strategy for internal and external communications, ensuring clarity, professionalism, and brand alignment across all stakeholder touchpoints. Strategic Ad-Hoc Initiatives Performance Recovery: Architect and oversee targeted "Get Well Plans" for specific sites or service lines to rapidly restore performance to required thresholds. Audit Leadership: Command the regional response to internal and external audits, coordinating evidence collection and the swift implementation of corrective actions. SOP Architecture: Identify regional operational inconsistencies and author robust Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to unify delivery across diverse geographies. Risk Intelligence: Conduct deep-dive investigations into emerging risks—such as supply chain volatility or regulatory shifts—and present sophisticated mitigation strategies to the executive team. Growth & Transition Support: Provide critical support for contract extensions, renewals, and new site integrations, ensuring that all expansions are immediately absorbed into the established governance framework. Executive Governance & Leadership Strategic Advisory (Regional Leadership Team): Act as a core member of the Account Leadership Team, providing critical operational insights and risk-based perspectives to inform all high-level strategic decision-making. Risk & Compliance Stewardship: Preside over the Regional Risk Committee, maintaining a rigorous Account Risk Register and ensuring that all mitigation strategies are adequately resourced, funded, and actively executed. Operational Integrity (Change Management Board): Serve as a final reviewer for significant operational modifications, ensuring that any shifts in delivery do not compromise the account's governance standards or compliance posture. Safety & Ethical Leadership: Serve as the regional executive champion for JLL’s Ethics and Health & Safety frameworks, embedding a culture of "Safety First" and ensuring total adherence to global governance across all regional team Qualifications & Experience Academic Foundations Education: Bachelor’s degree in Business, Operations, or Finance (Required). Advanced Degree: MBA or Master’s in Management (Highly Preferred). Professional Experience Tenure: 10–12+ years in Business Operations or Corporate Real Estate. Leadership: 5+ years in a regional "Head of Ops" or lead role managing multi-site portfolios. Contract Mastery: Expert-level management of MSAs, Task Orders, and complex work authorizations. Data & Analytics: Proven track record using KPI dashboards to drive performance and "Get Well" recovery plans

description: SME, P2P Transformation Procurement SME (contractor) Role purpose Provide director-level subject matter expertise to design and operationalise catalogue content, taxonomy, and guided buying capabilities within SAP Ariba, enabling scalable deployment across markets. Coach stakeholder groups on taxonomy, content standards, and how to sustain content quality in BAU. Required experience 14+ years in procurement / P2P transformation Deep hands-on experience with SAP Ariba (Guided Buying, catalogues, punchouts, supplier enablement concepts, and buying controls). Proven delivery experience in multi-market rollouts, ideally in regulated environments (banking/financial services preferred). Strong capability in taxonomy design, data quality standards, and operating model / governance. You would ideally have extensive experience in automotive retail, both new and pre-owned vehicles, and a first-hand understanding of the market in APAC with a proven track record in managing successful automotive businesses. Job Responsibilities · Develop and execute a comprehensive regional market strategy for buying and selling both new and pre-owned cars, including market analysis, identifying key growth areas, and setting clear objectives. · Accountable for the delivery of the marketplace annual plan P&L, including sales, contribution margin (ROI) and headcount as approved by Group management. · Lead the opening of new branches in multiple countries to expand our regional market presence and reach a wider customer base. · Oversee retail and wholesale operations in the region, managing a team of professionals to ensure optimal inventory management and pricing strategies in each market. · Recruit, train, and manage high-performing country teams, and ensuring that they are aligned with the company's goals and values, while motivating them to achieve via a structured incentive program. · Identify and develop valuable partnerships and collaborations in various markets to drive business growth and enhance the company's presence in the market. · Establish and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure the success of the operations, making data-driven decisions to optimize performance. · Ensure that all operations adhere to local regulations and standards and uphold Carro’s reputation and code of business conduct. · Maintain a deep knowledge of used car markets and automotive industry in the region. Job Requirements · Bachelor's degree in business, management, or any related field (MBA or equivalent is a plus). · At least 5+ years of proven experience in a senior leadership role within the automotive industry or a related field, preferable in a regional or global role. · Experience in and deep knowledge of the automotive market in APAC and familiar with the consumer trends of the region. · Demonstrated ability to drive growth and business expansion, P&L management, cost control. · Strong financial acumen with analytical and problem-solving skills. · Exceptional leadership and team management skills with proven record in managing and driving a high-performing team. · Excellent networking and communication skills and the ability to effectively manage internal and external stakeholders. · Technologically savvy and familiar with online retail of vehicles.

title: Data Vizualization Engineer
description: About GlobalFoundries GlobalFoundries is a leading full-service semiconductor foundry providing a unique combination of design, development, and fabrication services to some of the world’s most inspired technology companies. With a global manufacturing footprint spanning three continents, GlobalFoundries makes possible the technologies and systems that transform industries and give customers the power to shape their markets. For more information, visit www.gf.com . Introduction: As a Visualization Engineer, you will serve a crucial function in our Digital Manufacturing initiatives. Your primary mission will be to develop and deploy interactive data visualizations and digital applications that directly optimize and improve our manufacturing processes. You will be key to turning complex data into the actionable insights required to drive our digital transformation success. Your Job : Visualization Design & Development: Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) to provide key business insights and support data-driven decision-making. Data Analysis & Insight Generation: Perform statistical analysis and data plotting to solve complex business problems, derive actionable insights, and create clear correlation models for functional teams. Project & Stakeholder Management: Manage the full life cycle of data visualization projects, from requirements gathering with functional teams to final delivery, serving as the primary liaison between business stakeholders and IT/Engineering teams. Data Sourcing & Modeling: Execute data discovery and cataloging to efficiently locate and understand internal data sources. Apply foundational knowledge of data models and schemas to perform source-to-target mapping, ensuring accurate, structured data for reporting. Data Pipeline Collaboration: Work closely with data engineering teams, utilizing familiarity with AWS services and exposure to PySpark to understand and collaborate on the transformation scripts and data pipelines that process large volumes of manufacturing data. Cross-Functional Teamwork: Work closely with the Analytics Center of Excellence Manager and other stakeholders to define project objectives, deliverables, and timelines. Required Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in a computer science, information technology, or related fields 2 years of experience and knowledge in data visualization with a strong understanding of both front-end and back-end technologies. Proficiency in data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker). Familiarity with AWS cloud environment and data transformation tools such as PySpark. Proficiency in front-end and back-end technologies. Experience with API development and integration. Experience with version control systems and familiarity with Agile development methodologies. Excellent communication and collaboration skills. Ability work independently and as part of the cross-functional team. Strong problem-solving and analytical skills with a passion for building high-quality digital applications. GlobalFoundries is an equal opportunity employer, cultivating a diverse and inclusive workforce. We believe having a multicultural workplace enhances productivity, efficiency and innovation whilst our employees feel truly respected, valued and heard. As an affirmative employer, all qualified applicants are considered for employment regardless of age, ethnicity, marital status, citizenship, race, religion, political affiliation, gender, sexual orientation and medical and/or physical abilities. All offers of employment with GlobalFoundries are conditioned upon the successful completion of background checks, medical screenings as applicable and subject to the respective local laws and regulations.

title: Quantity Surveyor (Electrical)
description:
Job Description The Quantity Surveyor (Tender – Electrical) plays a key role in supporting the tendering and cost management processes for electrical works within construction projects. The role involves evaluating project requirements, preparing accurate cost estimates, negotiating with suppliers and contractors, and ensuring effective financial control from pre-contract to award stage. Job Responsibilities Cost Estimation &
Budget Preparation ·        Prepare detailed cost estimates and tender budgets for Electrical works in construction, ACMV, and A&A (Alteration & Addition) projects. ·        Analyze material, labour, equipment, and subcontractor costs to ensure accurate pricing. Tender Review & Construction Plan Analysis ·        Review and interpret electrical drawings, specifications, and construction plans to determine comprehensive project cost requirements. ·        Identify discrepancies, design issues, or cost-impact items and highlight them to the project team. Cost Optimization & Value Engineering ·        Identify opportunities for cost savings through alternative materials, design adjustments, or value engineering proposals. ·        Recommend solutions that maintain project quality and performance standards. Tender & Bid Preparation ·        Prepare, compile, and submit tender documents, Bills of Quantities (BOQ), and competitive bid proposals. ·        Source, evaluate, and compare quotations from suppliers and subcontractors . Contract Negotiation ·        Negotiate contract terms, pricing, and delivery conditions with suppliers and subcontractors to achieve cost-effective outcomes. ·        Ensure compliance with project budget, scope, and technical requirements. Cost Review & Financial Monitoring ·        Assist in tracking tender costs and prepare cost comparison reports for management review. ·        Highlight cost variations, risks, and financial considerations to support decision-making. Site Evaluation (Pre-Tender) ·        Conduct site visits when necessary to assess site conditions, access, and constructability issues that may affect pricing. Dispute & Clarification Management ·        Address queries, clarifications, and discrepancies related to tender pricing, BOQ items, or contractual terms. ·        Support resolution of cost-related claims or disputes when required. Client & Internal Coordination ·        Provide cost-related advice to clients, project managers, and engineers. ·        Collaborate closely with internal departments (Engineering, Procurement, Project Team) to ensure alignment on tender requirements. Job Requirements ·        Minimum 2 years of relevant working experience in Quantity Surveying, preferably in Electrical or MEP works. ·        Diploma in Engineering, Quantity Surveying, or related field. ·        Strong analytical and numerical skills with high attention to detail and accuracy. ·        Proficient in MS Office, cost estimation software, and project management tools. ·        Strong communication and interpersonal skills. ·        Able to work independently and collaboratively with cross-functional teams. ·        Good negotiation skills with experience in dealing with contractors and suppliers. ·        Understanding of risk management and contract administration. ·        Familiarity with construction industry regulations, standards, and tender procedures.

title: Technical Business Analyst
description: We are looking for a techincal business analyst who are passionate about delivering high quality IT applications and solutions. You will be part of the team that helps to improve and transform the processes in the banking and finance industry. You will have opportunities to learn new skill set and acquire valuable domain knowledge as you work on the solutions that meet multifaceted technical and business requirements. Roles and Responsibilities Bridge the gap between business needs and technical solutions: Act as a liaison between various business stakeholders and the development team to ensure that business requirements are clearly understood and translated into technical solutions. Collaborate with both business and technical teams to align objectives, prioritize tasks, and address any discrepancies or challenges that may arise during the development process. Translate requirements into technical specs and actionable tasks for the development team : Take ownership of translating business requirements into detailed technical specifications, ensuring clarity and feasibility for the development team. Break down complex requirements into manageable tasks and user stories, facilitating effective communication and task management within the distributed development team. Gather requirements and document business requirements: Conduct thorough requirements gathering sessions with stakeholders to identify and prioritize business needs, objectives, and constraints. Document business requirements accurately, ensuring completeness, clarity, and traceability throughout the project lifecycle. Develop detailed user stories and acceptance criteria: Create comprehensive user stories that capture end-user perspectives, functional requirements, and acceptance criteria. Define clear acceptance criteria to ensure that deliverables meet stakeholder expectations and quality standards. Work closely with development teams to ensure timely delivery of solutions: Collaborate closely with development teams to provide guidance, clarification, and support throughout the development process. Monitor project progress, identify potential bottlenecks, and proactively address any issues that may impact the timely delivery of solutions. Conduct thorough testing of implemented solutions that meet business requirements: Plan and execute rigorous testing procedures to validate that implemented solutions fulfill specified business requirements and functional specifications. Identify and report any defects or deviations from requirements, working with the development team to resolve issues promptly. Provide support and guidance to stakeholders throughout the development lifecycle: Serve as a primary point of contact for stakeholders, offering support, guidance, and regular updates on project status, milestones, and deliverables. Address stakeholder queries, concerns, and feedback in a timely and professional manner, ensuring a high level of satisfaction and engagement throughout the development lifecycle. Identify and communicate risks, issues, and dependencies related to project delivery: Proactively identify potential risks, issues, and dependencies that may impact project delivery, and communicate them effectively to stakeholders and project teams. Collaborate with relevant stakeholders to develop mitigation strategies, contingency plans, and resolutions for addressing identified risks and issues. Requirements Bachelor’s Degree: A bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or Business Administration. Experience in the Banking Domain will be an advantage: Previous experience or exposure to the banking or financial services industry, including knowledge of banking products, processes, and regulatory requirements. Experience in Agile methodologies and knowledgeable in software development Lifecycle and software testing: Hands-on experience with Agile methodologies (e.g., Scrum, Kanban) and a strong understanding of the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and software testing practices. Familiarity with database SQL queries: Proficiency in writing and executing SQL queries to retrieve, manipulate, and analyze data from relational databases. Familiarity with JIRA and Confluence: Experience using JIRA for project management and issue tracking, as well as Confluence for collaboration and documentation.

title: Specialist (Strategy, Governance & Enterprise Risk Management)
description:
The Corporate Strategy & Development Department drives CDAC’s annual planning cycle, aligning strategy, workplans, risk, and budget. You will partner departments to run the annual planning cycle end‑to‑end using a data‑driven, evidence‑based approach, support governance through policy development and Board/Committee secretariat work and drive organisation development initiatives such as capacity building and productivity improvements across CDAC. What You Will Work On Strategy & Performance Tracking Conduct and drive annual planning cycle, consolidating a clear portfolio of initiatives with outputs, outcomes, milestones, risks, budget, and resourcing. Analyse internal and external data to inform strategy, prioritisation, resource allocation, and risk identification. Run mid-year and year end reviews; build and maintain a balanced scorecard and dashboards to track results vs targets (timeline, outputs and outcomes). Facilitate deep dives with stakeholders; recommend keep/accelerate/stop/intervene decisions and reallocate
resources to priorities. Prepare strategy and decision papers for Senior Management and the Board. Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) Maintain and enhance the ERM framework and risk policy; embed risk into strategy, project gates, and budget trade-offs. Facilitate risk assessments at enterprise, departmental, and workplan levels. Maintain the enterprise risk register and track mitigation actions. Governance & Board Secretariat Serve as secretariat to the Board and/or relevant Committees where required. Support forward planning, prepare agendas, papers, minutes, track and follow through on action items. Maintain and strengthen governance policies (e.g., whistleblowing), ensure compliance with relevant regulatory requirements (e.g., Charities Act), industry codes and best practices (e.g., Code of Governance for Charities and IPCs). Stakeholder Engagement & Change Enablement Engage internal/external stakeholders (e.g., department heads, programme leads, partners, agencies) to inform strategy and secure buy-in. Drive change management for key initiatives (e.g., internal communications, training, AI adoption etc.). Requirements A degree in relevant field with at least 3 years of relevant experience in strategic
planning, committee/board governance support, data protection, analytics, policy
governance, enterprise risk and compliance. Candidates with relevant training
and experience in 3 or more of the areas listed are encouraged to apply. Prior experience in non-profit, public sector, social services and healthcare is preferred. Candidates from other sectors with relevant skills and experience are also encouraged to apply. Proven ability to drive annual strategic planning and review cadences (mid-year and year-end)
with integrated risk and budget alignment. Experience ii using logic model or Theory of Change to frame strategy, output, outcome and
impact. Good Track record at producing Board/Committee/Senior Management papers. Proficient in MS Office suite of applications. Experience in Salesforce CRM, data extraction, analysis and dashboarding (e.g. Power BI) will be
advantageous though not mandatory. Strategic thinking, excellent writing, synthesis, facilitation and stakeholder engagement.

title: Financial Controller
description: Role Overview We are seeking an experienced Financial Controller to oversee the company’s finance, accounting, compliance, and human resources functions, while providing strategic financial insights to support sustainable business performance. This is a key role requiring strong professional judgment, independence, and ownership of financial governance, cost management, and people administration. Key Responsibilities Lead and take overall accountability for the integrity, accuracy, and timeliness of financial reporting, including management accounts, balance sheets, and profit & loss statements. Provide strategic oversight of the company’s accounting operations, cash flow position, and financial controls. Drive financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and cost management initiatives to support sustainable business performance. Design, implement, and refine costing frameworks and cost control strategies to enhance financial visibility and decision-making. Ensure full compliance with all applicable accounting standards and statutory requirements in Singapore. Oversee GST, corporate tax, CPF, and statutory employee income reporting, ensuring timely and accurate submissions. Act as the primary liaison with auditors, tax agents, corporate secretaries, bankers, and regulatory bodies. Establish, enforce, and continuously improve accounting policies, procedures, and internal control systems to manage financial risk. Review and approve key financial transactions, payment runs, and expense claims with appropriate governance. Provide independent financial analysis, insights, and recommendations to management on business performance and strategic matters. Oversee human resources governance, including payroll oversight, statutory compliance, and alignment with MOM and Tripartite guidelines. Provide oversight of foreign workforce administration and labor compliance matters. Support overall business operations by ensuring effective administrative controls and compliance frameworks are in place. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Accountancy, Finance, or a related discipline. Minimum 7 years of relevant hands-on experience, preferably in an SME environment, with exposure to finance, accounting, and HR functions. Strong knowledge of Singapore statutory and regulatory requirements (IRAS, CPF, ACRA, MOM). High level of accuracy, integrity, and professional judgment. Strong analytical, planning, and problem-solving skills. Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and meet deadlines. Good communication and stakeholder management skills; ability to liaise with Chinese-speaking stakeholders is an advantage. Self-motivated, dependable, and committed to long-term business sustainability.
